Vera's Flavor

편입영어 독해

BASIC

독해에 맛을 더하다.
당신의 잠재력을 깨우는 특별한 한 스푼.

verajin academy

CONTENTS

  • PART 1. 내용 파악 (Passage Comprehension) 4
  • PART 2. 대의 파악 (Main Idea) 115
  • PART 3. 함축의미추론 (Inferring Meaning) 220
  • PART 4. 문장 삽입 (Sentence Insertion) 325
  • PART 5. 순서 배열 (Passage Ordering) 380
  • PART 6. 빈칸 추론 (Blank Completion) 485
2
PART 1

내용 파악

Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 1]

A Lost Wallet

Last Tuesday, Sujin was walking home from the library. She was thinking about her homework when she saw a brown leather wallet on the sidewalk. She picked it up and opened it. Inside, there was some money and a driver's license. Sujin realized that she should find the owner. She felt a little bit of panic, not knowing what to do. Then, she saw a police station across the street. She went inside and gave the wallet to a police officer. The next day, a man called her. He was the owner of the wallet. He was very thankful and called her an honest student. He even sent a small reward to her school. Sujin felt proud of doing the right thing.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • realize:깨닫다
  • panic:공황, 당황
  • honest:정직한
  • reward:보상, 사례

Questions

1-1.위 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① A visit to the police station
  • ② The importance of homework
  • ③ An experience of finding a lost wallet
  • ④ How to get a driver's license
  • ⑤ Dangers on the sidewalk

1-2.What did Sujin do right after she found the wallet?

  • ① She took the money out.
  • ② She went to the owner's house.
  • ③ She took it to the police station.
  • ④ She called the owner directly.
  • ⑤ She walked past it.

1-3.위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?

  • ① 수진이는 도서관에 다녀오는 길이었다.
  • ② 지갑 안에는 돈과 신분증이 들어 있었다.
  • ③ 지갑 주인은 수진이에게 고마움을 표현했다.
  • ④ 경찰관이 수진이에게 보상을 주었다.
  • ⑤ 수진이는 옳은 일을 한 자신을 자랑스러워했다.
4
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 2]

Mike's First Cooking Class

Mike loves eating pizza, but he has never made one. Last weekend, he joined a kids' cooking class. The chef first showed everyone how to make the dough. Mike followed the recipe carefully. He mixed flour, water, and yeast. It was a bit messy, and he got flour all over his apron. Next, he added tomato sauce, cheese, and his favorite toppings: pepperoni and mushrooms. He put his pizza into the big oven. While waiting, he was very nervous. Finally, his pizza was ready. It looked and smelled wonderful. He took a big bite. It was the most delicious pizza he had ever tasted! He couldn't wait to make one for his family.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • recipe:요리법, 레시피
  • messy:지저분한, 엉망인
  • apron:앞치마
  • delicious:맛있는

Questions

2-1.위 글의 제목으로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① The Best Pizza Restaurant
  • ② Mike's Fun Cooking Experience
  • ③ How to Eat Pizza
  • ④ The History of Pizza
  • ⑤ Dangers in the Kitchen

2-2.위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.

  • ① 마이크는 피자 만드는 것을 싫어한다.
  • ② 마이크는 요리 수업에서 파스타를 만들었다.
  • ③ 마이크는 피자를 오븐에 넣는 것을 잊었다.
  • ④ 마이크는 자신이 만든 피자가 아주 맛있다고 생각했다.
  • ⑤ 요리사가 마이크의 피자를 대신 만들어 주었다.

2-3.According to the passage, which topping did Mike NOT use?

  • ① Tomato sauce
  • ② Cheese
  • ③ Pepperoni
  • ④ Olives
  • ⑤ Mushrooms
5
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 3]

My Pet Hamster, Choco

I have a pet hamster named Choco. He is a small, round, and furry creature. He lives in a big cage with a wheel, a water bottle, and a small house. During the day, Choco mostly sleeps inside his house. But when the night comes, he becomes very active. He runs on his wheel for hours. Sometimes the sound of the wheel wakes me up, but I don't mind. I love to observe him. I am responsible for feeding him and cleaning his cage every week. Taking care of a pet is a lot of work, but it is also very rewarding. Choco is a small animal, but he is a big part of my life.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • creature:생물, 동물
  • cage:우리, 새장
  • observe:관찰하다
  • responsible:책임이 있는

Questions

3-1.What is this passage mainly about?

  • ① The different kinds of hamsters
  • ② The writer's experience with a pet
  • ③ The difficulties of cleaning a cage
  • ④ How to buy a healthy hamster
  • ⑤ Animals that are active at night

3-2.According to the passage, when is Choco most active?

  • ① In the morning
  • ② In the afternoon
  • ③ During the day
  • ④ At night
  • ⑤ When the writer is at school

3-3.위 글의 내용으로 알 수 없는 것은?

  • ① 햄스터의 이름은 초코이다.
  • ② 글쓴이는 매주 햄스터의 집을 청소한다.
  • ③ 햄스터는 밤에 쳇바퀴를 돌린다.
  • ④ 글쓴이는 초코를 2년 전에 샀다.
  • ⑤ 글쓴이는 애완동물을 돌보는 것에 보람을 느낀다.
6
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 4]

Volunteering at a Nursing Home

Last Saturday, my friend Jina and I did some volunteer work. We went to a local nursing home. A nursing home is a place where elderly people who need care live. At first, I was a little nervous because I had never been to one before. Our job was to read books to the residents and play board games with them. I was paired with an old woman named Mrs. Park. She couldn't see well, so I read a newspaper for her. She listened carefully and smiled. Later, Jina and I helped serve lunch. All the elderly people were very kind to us. It was a valuable experience. I learned that spending time with others is a great way to show love.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • volunteer:자원봉사(자)
  • nursing home:요양원
  • elderly:나이가 지긋한, 어르신들
  • valuable:가치 있는, 소중한

Questions

4-1.What is the main purpose of the writer?

  • ① To ask for donations for a nursing home
  • ② To describe a volunteer experience
  • ③ To complain about a boring weekend
  • ④ To give information about board games
  • ⑤ To explain how to read to the elderly

4-2.Which of the following is NOT mentioned as an activity the writer did?

  • ① Reading a newspaper
  • ② Playing board games
  • ③ Serving lunch
  • ④ Cleaning the rooms
  • ⑤ Talking with elderly people

4-3.글쓴이가 처음에 요양원에서 느꼈을 심경으로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① excited and happy
  • ② bored and tired
  • ③ proud and confident
  • ④ a little nervous
  • ⑤ angry and upset
7
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 5]

The First Snow

This morning when I woke up, I saw something amazing outside my window. The world was white! Big, soft flakes of snow were falling from the sky. It was the first snow of the year. The snow covered everything—the trees, the roofs, and the cars. It looked like the world was covered in a soft, white blanket. The whole town was quiet and peaceful. I quickly put on my warmest clothes and ran outside. The air was cold and fresh. I made a snowball and threw it at a tree. The atmosphere was so magical. I went back inside and made a cup of hot chocolate. Drinking it by the window and watching the snow fall made me feel very cozy.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • flake:(눈)송이
  • blanket:담요
  • atmosphere:분위기
  • cozy:아늑한, 편안한

Questions

5-1.위 글의 제목으로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① How to Make Hot Chocolate
  • ② The Dangers of Winter
  • ③ A Beautiful Snowy Day
  • ④ My Favorite Season
  • ⑤ A Fun Snowball Fight

5-2.위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?

  • ① 올해의 첫눈이 내렸다.
  • ② 글쓴이는 창밖을 보고 눈이 온 것을 알았다.
  • ③ 글쓴이는 밖으로 나가 눈싸움을 했다.
  • ④ 글쓴이는 따뜻한 코코아를 마셨다.
  • ⑤ 눈은 모든 것을 하얀 담요처럼 덮었다.

5-3.밑줄 친 cozy의 의미로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① cold and wet
  • ② warm and comfortable
  • ③ sad and lonely
  • ④ loud and exciting
  • ⑤ busy and tired
8
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 6]

School Sports Day

Our school's annual sports day was held yesterday. The sky was clear and everyone was full of energy. All students were divided into two teams: the Blue Team and the White Team. I was on the Blue Team. I decided to compete in the 100-meter race and the relay race. I didn't win the 100-meter race, but I tried my best. The most exciting event was the relay race. I was the last runner for my team. When I got the baton, the White Team was slightly ahead. I ran as fast as I could. My friends and teachers cheered for me loudly. In the end, I passed the other runner and we won! I was so tired, but incredibly happy. Sports day is not just about winning; it's about teamwork and effort.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • compete:경쟁하다, 참가하다
  • relay race:이어달리기
  • cheer:응원하다
  • effort:노력

Questions

6-1.What is the main idea of this passage?

  • ① The writer's exciting experience on sports day
  • ② How to win a relay race
  • ③ The rules of the 100-meter race
  • ④ The importance of being on the winning team
  • ⑤ The different teams at school

6-2.Which event did the writer's team win?

  • ① The 100-meter race
  • ② The soccer match
  • ③ The relay race
  • ④ The long jump
  • ⑤ All the events

6-3.위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.

  • ① 운동회는 비 오는 날 열렸다.
  • ② 글쓴이는 흰색 팀 소속이었다.
  • ③ 글쓴이는 100미터 달리기에서 1등을 했다.
  • ④ 글쓴이는 이어달리기의 마지막 주자였다.
  • ⑤ 글쓴이는 운동회가 끝난 후 매우 슬펐다.
9
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 7]

A Mysterious Noise

One night, I was trying to sleep when I heard a strange noise. It was a soft creak, and it seemed to come from downstairs. At first, I tried to ignore it, but I heard it again. My heart started beating faster. Was it a thief? I decided to be brave and investigate. I quietly got out of bed and tiptoed down the stairs. The noise was getting louder. It was coming from the kitchen. I peeked around the corner and saw a small shadow moving. I was so scared! But then, I saw what it was. It was just my new puppy, Max, trying to get a cookie from the jar on the counter. I was so relieved. I picked him up and said, "Max, you scared me!" The source of the mystery was my cute puppy.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • creak:삐걱거리는 소리
  • investigate:조사하다
  • tiptoe:발끝으로 살금살금 걷다
  • relieved:안도하는
  • source:근원, 출처

Questions

7-1.What is the best title for this story?

  • ① My Brave Puppy
  • ② The Midnight Thief
  • ③ A Mysterious Kitchen Noise
  • ④ How to Train a Puppy
  • ⑤ A Scary Dream

7-2.How did the writer feel when he first heard the noise?

  • ① Happy and excited
  • ② Calm and peaceful
  • ③ A little scared
  • ④ Bored and sleepy
  • ⑤ Angry and annoyed

7-3.What was the real source of the strange noise?

  • ① A thief in the kitchen
  • ② The writer's father
  • ③ The wind outside
  • ④ A new puppy
  • ⑤ A ghost
10
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 8]

Our Small Balcony Garden

My family lives in an apartment, so we don't have a big yard. But we have a small balcony, and my mom decided to start a small gardening project there. First, we bought some large pots, good soil, and a few seed packets. We chose to plant cherry tomatoes, lettuce, and some herbs like basil. My job was to fill the pots with soil and plant the seeds. It was fun to play with the dirt. Every day, I watered the plants and checked on them. For about a week, nothing happened. I was losing patience. But then, one morning, I saw a tiny green sprout coming out of the soil! I was so happy. Gardening teaches me that good things take time.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • gardening:원예, 정원 가꾸기
  • soil:흙, 토양
  • patience:인내심, 참을성
  • sprout:싹, 싹이 트다

Questions

8-1.What is the main purpose of this passage?

  • ① To explain the benefits of eating vegetables
  • ② To describe the experience of starting a small garden
  • ③ To compare apartment life and house life
  • ④ To sell gardening tools and seeds
  • ⑤ To complain about living in an apartment

8-2.According to the passage, which of the following did the family NOT plant?

  • ① Cherry tomatoes
  • ② Lettuce
  • ③ Basil
  • ④ Carrots
  • ⑤ Herbs

8-3.밑줄 친 patience의 의미로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① the ability to wait calmly
  • ② the skill of growing plants
  • ③ the knowledge about soil
  • ④ the feeling of happiness
  • ⑤ the need for water
11
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 9]

A Day at the Dinosaur Museum

Last weekend, my dad took me to the Natural History Museum. My favorite part of the museum is the dinosaur exhibit. As soon as we entered the hall, I saw a huge Tyrannosaurus Rex skeleton. It was so big that I felt like a tiny ant next to it. The sign said the T-Rex was a powerful hunter. I also saw the skeleton of a Triceratops, with its three big horns. There were many other fossils of ancient plants and sea creatures. I spent a long time looking at all the exhibits and reading the explanations. It's amazing to think that these giant creatures walked the Earth millions of years ago. It was a fascinating day.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • exhibit:전시, 전시품
  • skeleton:뼈대, 골격
  • fossil:화석
  • ancient:고대의

Questions

9-1.Where did the writer go last weekend?

  • ① To the zoo
  • ② To an art gallery
  • ③ To the library
  • ④ To the Natural History Museum
  • ⑤ To the park

9-2.What impressed the writer the most at the museum?

  • ① The museum gift shop
  • ② The ancient sea creatures
  • ③ The huge T-Rex skeleton
  • ④ The fossils of plants
  • ⑤ The friendly museum staff

9-3.위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?

  • ① 글쓴이는 아빠와 함께 박물관에 갔다.
  • ② 티라노사우루스 렉스는 거대한 사냥꾼이었다.
  • ③ 트리케라톱스는 뿔이 세 개 달려 있었다.
  • ④ 글쓴이는 공룡 전시가 지루하다고 생각했다.
  • ⑤ 박물관에는 고대 식물 화석도 있었다.
12
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 10]

The Power of Sleep

Why is sleep so important? Many students stay up late to study or play games, but not getting enough sleep is a bad idea. Sleep is essential for both your body and your brain. While you are sleeping, your body repairs itself and your brain organizes the information from the day. This helps you remember what you learned. If you don't get enough sleep, you will feel tired and won't be able to concentrate in class. It can also affect your mood, making you feel easily upset. Doctors say teenagers need about 8 to 10 hours of sleep every night. So, make sure to turn off your phone, go to bed early, and let your brain recharge!

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • essential:필수적인, 극히 중요한
  • concentrate:집중하다
  • affect:영향을 미치다
  • recharge:재충전하다

Questions

10-1.What is the main topic of this passage?

  • ① The reasons why sleep is important
  • ② The best way to study for a test
  • ③ The effects of playing computer games
  • ④ How many hours doctors sleep
  • ⑤ The different stages of sleep

10-2.According to the passage, what is NOT a result of lacking sleep?

  • ① Feeling tired
  • ② Difficulty concentrating
  • ③ Becoming a better student
  • ④ A bad mood
  • ⑤ Forgetting what you learned

10-3.위 글에 따르면, 십 대 청소년에게 필요한 수면 시간은 얼마인가?

  • ① 4~5시간
  • ② 6~7시간
  • ③ 8~10시간
  • ④ 11~12시간
  • ⑤ 정해진 시간이 없다.
13
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 11]

A Family History Project

For my history class, I had a special assignment: to create a family tree. At first, I thought it would be boring. But I was wrong. I started by interviewing my grandparents. They told me amazing stories about their parents and even their grandparents. I learned that my great-grandfather was a teacher in a small village. My great-grandmother was famous for her beautiful singing. I discovered so many interesting facts about my ancestors. I collected old black-and-white photos and put them on a large poster board. My family tree wasn't just a list of names; it was a map of stories. This project helped me understand who I am and where I come from.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • assignment:과제, 숙제
  • interview:인터뷰하다, 면담하다
  • discover:발견하다
  • ancestor:조상, 선조

Questions

11-1.What was the writer's history assignment about?

  • ① Writing a report about a famous person
  • ② Creating a family tree
  • ③ Visiting a history museum
  • ④ Reading a history book
  • ⑤ Making a map of a village

11-2.위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.

  • ① 글쓴이는 처음에 숙제가 재미있을 것이라고 생각했다.
  • ② 글쓴이의 증조할아버지는 유명한 가수였다.
  • ③ 글쓴이는 숙제를 하면서 조상에 대해 많은 것을 알게 되었다.
  • ④ 글쓴이는 숙제를 위해 컬러 사진을 사용했다.
  • ⑤ 글쓴이는 부모님을 인터뷰했다.

11-3.How did the writer feel about the project in the end?

  • ① Bored and tired
  • ② Angry and frustrated
  • ③ It was a valuable and meaningful experience.
  • ④ It was too difficult to finish.
  • ⑤ Disappointed with the result.
14
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 12]

My Stamp Collection

My hobby is collecting stamps. It started when my grandfather gave me his old stamp album. The album was full of colorful stamps from all over the world. I was fascinated. Now, I have my own collection. Each stamp is like a small window to another country. Some stamps show famous people, while others show beautiful animals or historic buildings. My favorite stamp is a rare one from Brazil that shows a toucan. Whenever I get a new stamp, I use a magnifying glass to look at all the tiny details. Then, I carefully place it in my album. Collecting stamps is a fun way to learn about geography and culture.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • fascinated:매료된, 마음을 다 빼앗긴
  • building:건물
  • rare:희귀한, 드문
  • culture:문화

Questions

12-1.위 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① A gift from a grandfather
  • ② The fun of collecting stamps
  • ③ How to travel around the world
  • ④ The geography of Brazil
  • ⑤ The history of the postal service

12-2.Why does the writer like collecting stamps?

  • ① It is an easy way to make money.
  • ② It helps him learn about different countries.
  • ③ His friends all have the same hobby.
  • ④ He wants to be a designer.
  • ⑤ He likes using a magnifying glass.

12-3.위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?

  • ① 글쓴이의 취미는 우표 수집이다.
  • ② 할아버지가 처음으로 우표 앨범을 주셨다.
  • ③ 글쓴이가 가장 좋아하는 우표는 브라질의 펭귄 우표이다.
  • ④ 글쓴이는 우표를 통해 지리와 문화를 배운다.
  • ⑤ 글쓴이는 돋보기를 사용해 우표를 관찰한다.
15
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 13]

A Language Learning App

I have always wanted to learn Japanese because I love anime. So, I downloaded a language learning app on my smartphone. The app is designed to make learning fun. It has many games and quizzes. For example, I can play a game to match Japanese words with pictures. The app also helps with my pronunciation. I can record my voice and compare it to a native speaker's. I try to use the app for at least 15 minutes every day. It's very convenient because I can use it on the bus or while waiting for a friend. Thanks to this app, I can now introduce myself and say simple greetings in Japanese. Technology is a great tool for learning new things.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • download:다운로드하다
  • pronunciation:발음
  • convenient:편리한
  • greeting:인사

Questions

13-1.What is the main purpose of the passage?

  • ① To review a Japanese anime
  • ② To explain how to use a smartphone
  • ③ To share a fun experience of learning a language with an app
  • ④ To complain about the difficulty of learning Japanese
  • ⑤ To advertise a new language school

13-2.According to the passage, which is NOT a feature of the app?

  • ① Games and quizzes
  • ② Pronunciation practice
  • ③ Matching words with pictures
  • ④ Watching full anime movies
  • ⑤ Comparing the user's voice to a native speaker's

13-3.위 글의 내용으로 보아, 글쓴이가 일본어를 배우는 주된 이유는?

  • ① 일본으로 여행을 가고 싶어서
  • ② 일본인 친구를 사귀고 싶어서
  • ③ 학교 시험을 잘 보기 위해서
  • ④ 애니메이션을 좋아하기 때문에
  • ⑤ 부모님이 시켜서
16
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 14]

Grandma's Surprise Birthday Party

My grandmother's 70th birthday was last Sunday. My family decided to throw her a surprise party. We all had different roles. My dad was in charge of inviting all our relatives. My mom and I baked a special sweet potato cake because it's my grandmother's favorite. My younger brother made a birthday card with lots of colorful drawings. On the day of the party, we all hid in the living room. When my grandmother walked in, we all shouted, "Surprise!" She was so shocked and happy that she started to cry a little. She said it was the best present she had ever received. Seeing her happy face made all the preparation worthwhile. It was a day full of love and joy.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • role:역할
  • relative:친척
  • present:선물
  • preparation:준비
  • worthwhile:~할 가치가 있는

Questions

14-1.What did the family do for the grandmother's birthday?

  • ① They took her to a restaurant.
  • ② They bought her an expensive gift.
  • ③ They threw her a surprise party.
  • ④ They went on a trip with her.
  • ⑤ They sent her a letter.

14-2.위 글에서 글쓴이가 맡은 역할은 무엇이었는가?

  • ① 친척들 초대하기
  • ② 거실 꾸미기
  • ③ 생일 카드 만들기
  • ④ 엄마와 함께 케이크 굽기
  • ⑤ 할머니를 파티 장소로 모셔오기

14-3.How did the grandmother react to the surprise?

  • ① She was angry because she didn't know.
  • ② She was sad because some people didn't come.
  • ③ She was shocked and very happy.
  • ④ She was too tired to enjoy the party.
  • ⑤ She pretended not to be surprised.
17
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 15]

Planting a Tree for the Future

On Arbor Day, my school held a special event. The goal was to make our school campus greener. Each class was given a small tree to plant. Our teacher explained why trees are so vital for our planet. They produce the oxygen we breathe, provide shade on hot days, and become homes for birds and insects. Our class received a small maple tree. We all worked together. Some students dug a hole, and others carefully placed the tree inside. I helped cover the roots with soil. Finally, we gave the tree plenty of water. Our teacher said, "This tree will grow with you." I felt a sense of accomplishment. I hope to see this tree grow tall and strong in the future.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • Arbor Day:식목일
  • vital:필수적인, 매우 중요한
  • produce:생산하다
  • soil:흙, 토양
  • accomplishment:성취, 업적

Questions

15-1.위 글의 제목으로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① A Fun School Event: Planting a Tree
  • ② The Different Kinds of Maple Trees
  • ③ How Trees Produce Oxygen
  • ④ My Favorite Day of the Year
  • ⑤ The Problem of Air Pollution

15-2.According to the passage, which is NOT a reason why trees are important?

  • ① They produce oxygen.
  • ② They provide shade.
  • ③ They become homes for animals.
  • ④ They clean the water.
  • ⑤ They make our environment greener.

15-3.위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.

  • ① 이 행사는 어린이날에 열렸다.
  • ② 각 학생은 나무를 한 그루씩 심었다.
  • ③ 글쓴이의 반은 소나무를 심었다.
  • ④ 글쓴이는 흙으로 나무뿌리를 덮는 것을 도왔다.
  • ⑤ 선생님은 나무가 빨리 자라지 않을 것이라고 말했다.
18
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 16]

Learning to Ride a Bike

I still remember the day I learned how to ride a bike. I was seven years old, and my bike was bright blue. My dad took me to an empty park. He held the back of the bike while I pedaled. But every time he let go, I would lose my balance and fall. My knees got scraped, and I was scared. I wanted to give up. But my dad encouraged me. He said, "It's okay to fall. The important thing is to get back up." I tried again and again. Suddenly, I was pedaling on my own! I was actually riding the bike without any help. I felt like I was flying. It was a feeling of pure freedom and achievement. That day, I learned a valuable lesson: never give up.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • balance:균형
  • scrape:긁히다, 찰과상을 입다
  • encourage:격려하다, 용기를 북돋우다
  • achievement:성취, 달성

Questions

16-1.What is the main theme of this passage?

  • ① The importance of bike safety
  • ② A memory of learning to ride a bike
  • ③ A father's love for his child
  • ④ The best parks for bike riding
  • ⑤ How to choose a good bike

16-2.How did the writer's father help him?

  • ① He bought him a new bike.
  • ② He showed him a video.
  • ③ He encouraged him not to give up.
  • ④ He fixed the broken bike.
  • ⑤ He rode the bike for him.

16-3.글쓴이가 자전거 타기에 성공했을 때 느낀 감정으로 가장 알맞은 것은?

  • ① 슬픔과 외로움 (sadness and loneliness)
  • ② 지루함과 피곤함 (boredom and tiredness)
  • ③ 자유로움과 성취감 (freedom and achievement)
  • ④ 두려움과 걱정 (fear and worry)
  • ⑤ 분노와 실망 (anger and disappointment)
19
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 17]

A Visit to the Aquarium

Our class went on a field trip to the city aquarium last Friday. I was so excited because I love marine animals. The aquarium was huge and had many different sections. We saw scary sharks with sharp teeth and graceful rays that looked like they were flying in the water. My favorite part was the underwater tunnel. As we walked through it, fish of all colors and sizes swam all around us. It felt like I was walking on the ocean floor. We also watched a dolphin show. The dolphins were incredibly intelligent. They jumped high out of the water and did amazing tricks. The trip was not only fun but also educational. I learned a lot about the importance of protecting our oceans.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • marine:바다의, 해양의
  • graceful:우아한
  • intelligent:똑똑한, 지능이 높은
  • educational:교육적인

Questions

17-1.Where did the writer go on a field trip?

  • ① To a zoo
  • ② To a museum
  • ③ To an aquarium
  • ④ To a park
  • ⑤ To a farm

17-2.What was the writer's favorite part of the aquarium?

  • ① The dolphin show
  • ② The shark tank
  • ③ The gift shop
  • ④ The underwater tunnel
  • ⑤ The penguin exhibit

17-3.위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?

  • ① 글쓴이는 해양 동물을 좋아한다.
  • ② 상어는 날카로운 이빨을 가지고 있었다.
  • ③ 돌고래들은 똑똑하고 놀라운 묘기를 보여주었다.
  • ④ 글쓴이는 바다를 보호할 필요가 없다고 생각했다.
  • ⑤ 수중 터널을 걸을 때 물고기들이 주변에서 헤엄쳤다.
20
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 18]

A Friend in Need

My best friend, Jiho, loves playing soccer. During a match last week, he fell badly and sprained his ankle. The doctor said he had to use crutches for two weeks and couldn't carry heavy things. Jiho was worried about school because his classroom is on the third floor and he has a lot of books. I decided to help him. Every morning, I went to his house to help him carry his bag to school. During breaks, I helped him get his lunch. Some of my other friends helped too. Jiho was very grateful. He said, "A friend in need is a friend indeed." This experience taught me that true friendship is not just about having fun together, but also about supporting each other in difficult times.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • sprain:(발목, 손목 등을) 삐다
  • crutches:목발
  • grateful:고마워하는, 감사하는
  • difficult:어려운

Questions

18-1.What happened to Jiho last week?

  • ① He won a soccer match.
  • ② He moved to another school.
  • ③ He sprained his ankle.
  • ④ He got a perfect score on a test.
  • ⑤ He lost his school bag.

18-2.How did the writer help Jiho?

  • ① He did Jiho's homework for him.
  • ② He carried Jiho's bag to school.
  • ③ He taught Jiho how to play soccer.
  • ④ He visited Jiho in the hospital every day.
  • ⑤ He bought him new crutches.

18-3.The phrase "A friend in need is a friend indeed" means:

  • ① A true friend helps you when you have problems.
  • ② You should only be friends with people who need you.
  • ③ It is important to have many friends.
  • ④ Friends should always play together.
  • ⑤ You should not trust your friends.
21
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 19]

Making Kimchi with My Family

In Korea, there is a tradition called "Kimjang" in late fall. It's when families get together to make large quantities of kimchi for the winter. Last weekend, my family did our Kimjang. My grandmother is the expert. She showed my mom and me how to properly salt the cabbage and mix the special sauce. The sauce is made with red pepper powder, garlic, ginger, and other ingredients. My job was to spread the sauce between the cabbage leaves. It was messy, but fun. We made enough kimchi to fill ten large jars. We shared some with our neighbors. Kimjang is more than just making food. It's a cultural heritage that brings family and community together.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • tradition:전통
  • quantity:양, 수량
  • expert:전문가
  • ingredient:재료, 성분
  • heritage:유산

Questions

19-1.What is "Kimjang"?

  • ① A Korean holiday in the spring
  • ② The tradition of making a lot of kimchi for winter
  • ③ A special kind of spicy soup
  • ④ A traditional Korean dance
  • ⑤ The name of a famous Korean chef

19-2.위 글에 언급된 김치 양념 재료가 아닌 것은?

  • ① 고춧가루 (red pepper powder)
  • ② 마늘 (garlic)
  • ③ 생강 (ginger)
  • ④ 설탕 (sugar)
  • ⑤ 글쓴이의 역할은 양념을 배추 잎 사이에 바르는 것이었다.

19-3.What does the writer think Kimjang is about?

  • ① It is just a way to prepare food.
  • ② It is a difficult and boring chore.
  • ③ It is a cultural heritage that connects people.
  • ④ It is a competition to make the best kimchi.
  • ⑤ It is a good way to sell kimchi.
22
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 20]

The Most Important Meal of the Day

Do you often skip breakfast because you are in a hurry? Many people do, but doctors say that breakfast is the most important meal of the day. It literally means to "break the fast" after a long night of sleep. Your brain and body need fuel to start the day. Eating a healthy breakfast helps you concentrate better at school and gives you the energy to be active. People who eat breakfast tend to have a healthier diet overall. If you skip breakfast, you might feel tired and hungry by mid-morning, and you may eat unhealthy snacks later. You don't need a big meal. A simple bowl of cereal, a piece of fruit, or some toast can make a big difference.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • skip:거르다, 건너뛰다
  • fuel:연료
  • concentrate:집중하다
  • diet:식단, 식습관
  • difference:차이

Questions

20-1.What is the main idea of the passage?

  • ① The importance of eating breakfast
  • ② The best snacks to eat at school
  • ③ How to lose weight by skipping meals
  • ④ The history of breakfast foods
  • ⑤ Different breakfast menus around the world

20-2.According to the passage, what is a benefit of eating breakfast?

  • ① It makes you feel more tired.
  • ② It helps you concentrate better.
  • ③ It makes you gain weight quickly.
  • ④ It saves you time in the morning.
  • ⑤ It makes you want to eat more snacks.

20-3.위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?

  • ① 아침 식사는 하루 중 가장 중요한 식사이다.
  • ② 아침 식사를 거르면 학교에서 집중하기 어렵다.
  • ③ 아침 식사는 뇌와 몸에 에너지를 공급한다.
  • ④ 아침 식사는 항상 거창하게 먹어야 한다.
  • ⑤ 아침 식사를 거르면 건강에 좋지 않은 간식을 먹게 될 수 있다.
23
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 21]

The School Flea Market

Our school hosted a flea market last Saturday to raise money for a local charity. A flea market is a market where people sell used goods. My friends and I decided to participate. We gathered things we no longer used, like old books, toys, and clothes. We set up a small table in the school playground. At first, not many people visited our table. We were a bit disappointed. Then, my friend Minjun had a great idea. He made a colorful sign that said, "Great Items, Low Prices!" Soon, more students and parents started to come. We sold almost everything! In total, we earned 50,000 won. We felt proud to donate all the money to the charity. It was a fun way to help others and learn about economics.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • charity:자선 단체
  • participate:참가하다
  • disappointed:실망한
  • donate:기부하다

Questions

21-1.What was the purpose of the school flea market?

  • ① To help students clean their rooms
  • ② To raise money for a charity
  • ③ To give students a day off
  • ④ To teach students how to make new things
  • ⑤ To let students buy new school supplies

21-2.What did the writer and his friends do to attract more customers?

  • ① They lowered their prices to zero.
  • ② They shouted loudly.
  • ③ They made a colorful sign.
  • ④ They gave out free snacks.
  • ⑤ They moved their table to a different spot.

21-3.위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?

  • ① 벼룩시장은 중고 물품을 파는 시장이다.
  • ② 글쓴이와 친구들은 낡은 책, 장난감 등을 팔았다.
  • ③ 처음부터 많은 사람들이 그들의 가게를 방문했다.
  • ④ 그들은 번 돈을 모두 자선 단체에 기부했다.
  • ⑤ 그들은 총 5만 원을 벌었다.
24
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 22]

A Hike to the Summit

Last weekend, my family and I went hiking on Mount Seorak. Our goal was to reach the Ulsanbawi Rock. The trail was steep and rocky. At the beginning, I was full of energy. But after an hour, my legs started to feel heavy, and I was out of breath. I wanted to turn back. My dad noticed I was struggling. He gave me some water and a piece of chocolate. He said, "Look how far we've come! The view from the top will be worth it." His words gave me new strength. We rested for a while and then continued our climb. Finally, we reached the summit. The view was breathtaking. I could see the entire city below me. I felt like I was on top of the world.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • goal:목표
  • steep:가파른
  • view:경치, 전망
  • strength:힘
  • summit:정상, 꼭대기

Questions

22-1.What is the best title for this passage?

  • ① The Dangers of Mountain Climbing
  • ② A Challenging but Rewarding Hike
  • ③ How to Prepare for a Hike
  • ④ The Different Trails of Mount Seorak
  • ⑤ Family Picnics

22-2.How did the writer's father encourage him?

  • ① He offered to carry him.
  • ② He told him the view from the top would be great.
  • ③ He promised to buy him a gift.
  • ④ He said they could go home.
  • ⑤ He showed him a map.

22-3.위 글에 나타난 글쓴이의 심경 변화로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① excited → bored
  • ② tired and discouraged → proud and happy
  • ③ calm → nervous
  • ④ hopeful → disappointed
  • ⑤ angry → relieved
25
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 23]

My First Time on a Plane

When I was ten, my family went to Jeju Island for a vacation. It was my first time flying on an airplane. I was both excited and nervous. At the airport, I was amazed by the huge planes. When we boarded our plane, I found my window seat. I looked out the window as the plane started to move faster and faster. Then, I felt a gentle push as we took off. The houses and cars below us got smaller and smaller. Soon, we were flying above the clouds. It looked like a sea of white cotton. The flight attendant gave us juice and snacks. The one-hour flight felt very short. Landing was a bit bumpy, but it was exciting. It was an unforgettable experience.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • board:탑승하다
  • take off:이륙하다
  • flight attendant:객실 승무원
  • bumpy:울퉁불퉁한, 덜컹거리는
  • unforgettable:잊을 수 없는

Questions

23-1.What is this passage mainly about?

  • ① The writer's family vacation on Jeju Island
  • ② The writer's experience of flying for the first time
  • ③ The job of a flight attendant
  • ④ How airplanes fly
  • ⑤ The different types of airplanes

23-2.What did the writer see from the window after taking off?

  • ① The airport and other planes
  • ② The city getting smaller and the clouds below
  • ③ The ocean and many ships
  • ④ The inside of the cockpit
  • ⑤ The stars and the moon

23-3.위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.

  • ① 글쓴이는 비행기를 타는 것이 지루했다.
  • ② 글쓴이는 창가 자리에 앉지 못했다.
  • ③ 비행기는 구름 위를 날았다.
  • ④ 비행시간은 3시간이 걸렸다.
  • ⑤ 착륙은 매우 부드러웠다.
26
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 24]

The Magic of Reading

I used to think reading was boring. I preferred playing video games or watching TV. One rainy afternoon, my mom gave me a book called "The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe." She said it was her favorite book when she was a child. I had nothing else to do, so I started reading. The book was about four children who discover a magical land called Narnia. I was completely captivated. I forgot about the rain and the time. I felt like I was right there with the characters, having an adventure. Since that day, reading has become my favorite hobby. Books can take you to different worlds and let you live a thousand lives. It's a special kind of magic.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • prefer:~을 더 좋아하다, 선호하다
  • wardrobe:옷장
  • captivated:마음을 사로잡힌
  • character:등장인물
  • magic:마법

Questions

24-1.What did the writer use to think about reading?

  • ① It was exciting.
  • ② It was his favorite hobby.
  • ③ It was boring.
  • ④ It was difficult.
  • ⑤ It was better than video games.

24-2.What made the writer change his mind about reading?

  • ① His teacher gave him homework.
  • ② He read a magical story about Narnia.
  • ③ His friends told him to read more.
  • ④ He won a book in a contest.
  • ⑤ He wanted to learn new words.

24-3.위 글을 통해 알 수 있는 것은?

  • ① 글쓴이는 비 오는 날을 싫어한다.
  • ② 글쓴이의 엄마는 독서를 좋아하지 않는다.
  • ③ '나니아 연대기'는 네 명의 아이들에 대한 이야기이다.
  • ④ 글쓴이는 책을 읽은 후 다시는 비디오 게임을 하지 않았다.
  • ⑤ 글쓴이는 책을 다 읽는 데 일주일이 걸렸다.
27
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 25]

A City Mouse and a Country Mouse

Once, a city mouse visited his cousin in the country. The country mouse offered him simple food like beans and corn. The city mouse didn't like it. "My dear cousin," he said, "your life is so poor. Come with me to the city and I will show you the good life." So, the country mouse went to the city. The city mouse lived in a big house. They went to the dining room. The table was full of delicious food like cake and cheese. But as they started to eat, a big cat jumped in. The mice ran for their lives. Later, two big dogs came in, barking loudly. The country mouse was terrified. "Goodbye, cousin," he said. "I would rather eat my simple food in peace than have your fine food in fear."

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • cousin:사촌
  • poor:가난한, 초라한
  • terrified:겁에 질린, 무서워하는
  • rather A than B:B하느니 차라리 A하겠다
  • in peace:평화롭게

Questions

25-1.위 글의 장르로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Fable (우화)
  • ② Science fiction (공상 과학 소설)
  • ③ Biography (전기)
  • ④ News report (뉴스 기사)
  • ⑤ Poem (시)

25-2.Why did the country mouse decide to go back to the country?

  • ① He missed his family.
  • ② He did not like the city food.
  • ③ The city was too noisy.
  • ④ He was scared of the dangers in the city.
  • ⑤ The city mouse was not kind to him.

25-3.이 이야기가 주는 교훈으로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① 도시 생활이 시골 생활보다 항상 더 좋다.
  • ② 부유하지만 위험한 삶보다 소박하지만 평화로운 삶이 낫다.
  • ③ 맛있는 음식을 친구와 나눠 먹어야 한다.
  • ④ 항상 새로운 것을 시도해야 한다.
  • ⑤ 고양이는 쥐에게 매우 위험한 동물이다.
28
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 26]

How to Be a Good Friend

What does it mean to be a good friend? It's not just about having someone to hang out with. A good friend is someone you can trust. They keep your secrets and are honest with you, even when the truth is hard to hear. A good friend is also a good listener. They listen to your problems without judging you. They celebrate your successes and support you when you fail. Friendship is a two-way street. It means you also have to be a good friend to others. You should be loyal and kind. Remember to make time for your friends and show them you care. A true friendship is a precious gift.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • trust:신뢰하다, 믿다
  • judge:판단하다
  • loyal:충실한, 의리 있는
  • precious:소중한, 귀중한

Questions

26-1.What is the main topic of the passage?

  • ① The best ways to have fun with friends
  • ② The qualities of a good friend
  • ③ The problems that can happen in a friendship
  • ④ How to make new friends at school
  • ⑤ Why secrets are important

26-2.According to the passage, which is NOT a quality of a good friend?

  • ① They are trustworthy.
  • ② They are good listeners.
  • ③ They are always honest.
  • ④ They only talk about themselves.
  • ⑤ They are supportive.

26-3.위 글에서 "Friendship is a two-way street"가 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① 친구와는 길을 함께 걸어야 한다.
  • ② 좋은 친구가 되려면 나 자신도 좋은 친구가 되어주어야 한다.
  • ③ 친구 사이에는 비밀이 없어야 한다.
  • ④ 우정은 쉽게 변할 수 있다.
  • ⑤ 친구는 두 명만 사귀어야 한다.
29
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 27]

The Life of a Honeybee

Honeybees are fascinating insects. They live together in large groups called colonies. Each bee in the colony has a specific job. The queen bee is the mother of all the other bees. Her only job is to lay eggs. The male bees are called drones. Their job is to mate with the queen. Most bees are female worker bees. They have many important jobs. They collect nectar and pollen from flowers, build the honeycomb, and defend the hive from enemies. They work very hard their whole lives. The honey they produce is not for us; it's their food for the winter. So next time you enjoy some sweet honey, remember the hard work of these amazing creatures.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • colony:군집, 집단
  • lay eggs:알을 낳다
  • nectar:(꽃의) 꿀
  • pollen:꽃가루
  • defend:방어하다, 지키다
  • creature:생물

Questions

27-1.What is the passage mainly about?

  • ① The dangers of bee stings
  • ② The different roles of bees in a colony
  • ③ How to make honey at home
  • ④ The life cycle of a flower
  • ⑤ Why bees are disappearing

27-2.What is the main job of the queen bee?

  • ① To collect nectar
  • ② To build the honeycomb
  • ③ To defend the hive
  • ④ To lay eggs
  • ⑤ To lead the worker bees

27-3.위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?

  • ① 꿀벌은 군집을 이루어 산다.
  • ② 수컷 벌은 드론이라고 불린다.
  • ③ 대부분의 벌은 일벌이다.
  • ④ 일벌은 꽃에서 꿀과 꽃가루를 모은다.
  • ⑤ 꿀벌은 인간을 위해 꿀을 만든다.
30
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 28]

My Dream Job: A Webtoon Artist

When I grow up, I want to be a webtoon artist. A webtoon is a type of digital comic that you read on your smartphone or computer. I love drawing and telling stories, so I think it's the perfect job for me. To become a good webtoon artist, I need to practice drawing every day. I also need to read a lot of books and watch movies to get new ideas for my stories. A successful webtoon needs interesting characters and an engaging plot. It's also important to learn how to use digital drawing tools. It's a very competitive field, but I am willing to work hard to achieve my dream. I hope one day people will enjoy reading my webtoons.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • digital:디지털의
  • engaging:마음을 사로잡는, 매력적인
  • plot:줄거리, 구성
  • competitive:경쟁이 치열한
  • achieve:성취하다, 이루다

Questions

28-1.What is the writer's dream job?

  • ① A movie director
  • ② A novelist
  • ③ A webtoon artist
  • ④ A computer programmer
  • ⑤ A teacher

28-2.According to the passage, what is NOT needed to become a good webtoon artist?

  • ① Good drawing skills
  • ② Interesting story ideas
  • ③ The ability to sing well
  • ④ Knowledge of digital tools
  • ⑤ Hard work

28-3.위 글을 통해 알 수 있는 글쓴이의 성격으로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① 게으르고 소극적이다.
  • ② 꿈이 없고 비관적이다.
  • ③ 목표가 뚜렷하고 노력하는 자세를 가졌다.
  • ④ 남의 말을 잘 듣지 않고 고집이 세다.
  • ⑤ 쉽게 포기하고 변덕이 심하다.
31
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 29]

The Benefits of Laughter

They say that "laughter is the best medicine," and it's true. Laughter has many positive effects on our body and mind. When you laugh, your body releases chemicals called endorphins. Endorphins are natural pain relievers and make you feel happy. Laughter also improves your immune system, which helps your body fight off disease. It can even be a good workout for your muscles! A good, hearty laugh can relax your whole body. Furthermore, laughter is a social activity. It connects us with other people and strengthens our relationships. So, try to find humor in your daily life. Watch a funny movie, share a joke with a friend, or just laugh at yourself. It's good for your health.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • laughter:웃음
  • effect:효과, 영향
  • release:방출하다
  • immune system:면역 체계
  • health:건강

Questions

29-1.What is the main idea of the passage?

  • ① The different kinds of jokes
  • ② Why funny movies are popular
  • ③ The positive effects of laughter
  • ④ How to make other people laugh
  • ⑤ The science behind endorphins

29-2.According to the passage, what does laughter release in our body?

  • ① Adrenaline
  • ② Stress
  • ③ Endorphins
  • ④ Vitamins
  • ⑤ Tears

29-3.위 글에 언급된 웃음의 효과가 아닌 것은?

  • ① 고통을 줄여준다.
  • ② 면역 체계를 향상시킨다.
  • ③ 근육을 이완시킨다.
  • ④ 기억력을 향상시킨다.
  • ⑤ 사람들과의 관계를 좋게 한다.
32
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 30]

A Message in a Bottle

One day, a young boy named Finn was walking along the beach. He loved collecting seashells and interesting stones. Suddenly, he saw a small glass bottle in the sand. Inside, there was a piece of rolled-up paper. His heart beat with excitement. He carefully opened the bottle and took out the paper. It was a letter, but the writing was faded and hard to read. It seemed very old. The letter was from a sailor named Arthur. He wrote that his ship was caught in a big storm in 1985. He didn't know if he would survive. He just wanted someone to know his story. Finn felt a mix of sadness and wonder. He decided to keep the letter as a treasure, a secret from the sea.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • faded:색이 바랜, 희미해진
  • sailor:선원, 뱃사람
  • survive:살아남다, 생존하다
  • wonder:경이로움, 놀라움
  • treasure:보물

Questions

30-1.What did Finn find on the beach?

  • ① A valuable seashell
  • ② An old map
  • ③ A bottle with a letter inside
  • ④ A sailor's hat
  • ⑤ A piece of a shipwreck

30-2.What was the letter about?

  • ① A map to a hidden treasure
  • ② A love story
  • ③ A message from a sailor in a storm
  • ④ A list of things to do on a ship
  • ⑤ A poem about the sea

30-3.위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.

  • ① 핀은 보물 지도를 발견했다.
  • ② 병 속에 담긴 편지는 읽기 쉬웠다.
  • ③ 편지는 1985년에 쓰여졌다.
  • ④ 편지를 쓴 선원은 폭풍우에서 살아남았다.
  • ⑤ 핀은 편지를 경찰서에 가져다주었다.
33
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 31]

The School Play

Our English class decided to perform a short play. We chose "The Tortoise and the Hare." I was chosen to play the role of the Tortoise. I was happy because the Tortoise is the hero of the story. My best friend, Minho, played the Hare. We practiced for weeks. I had to learn how to walk very slowly and speak with a confident voice. Minho was good at pretending to be fast and a little bit arrogant. On the day of the play, I was nervous, but I remembered all my lines. The audience laughed when the Hare took a nap, and they cheered when I crossed the finish line first. It was a great experience to work together with my classmates and create something fun for everyone.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • tortoise:거북이
  • hare:산토끼
  • confident:자신감 있는
  • arrogant:거만한, 오만한
  • audience:관객, 청중

Questions

31-1.What is the passage mainly about?

  • ① The story of "The Tortoise and the Hare"
  • ② The writer's experience in a school play
  • ③ The friendship between the writer and Minho
  • ④ How to become a good actor
  • ⑤ The importance of winning

31-2.What role did the writer play?

  • ① The Hare
  • ② The Narrator
  • ③ The Tortoise
  • ④ The Fox
  • ⑤ A Tree

31-3.위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?

  • ① 글쓴이의 반은 '토끼와 거북이' 연극을 했다.
  • ② 글쓴이는 거북이 역할을 맡은 것을 기뻐했다.
  • ③ 민호는 빠르고 거만한 산토끼 연기를 잘했다.
  • ④ 글쓴이는 연극 당일 대사를 모두 잊어버렸다.
  • ⑤ 관객들은 거북이가 결승선을 통과했을 때 환호했다.
34
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 32]

A Day Without a Smartphone

My teacher gave us a strange homework assignment: to live for one whole day without our smartphones. I thought it would be impossible. My phone is my alarm clock, my camera, my music player, and my way to talk to friends. The next morning, I woke up late because I didn't have my phone's alarm. During the day, I felt anxious. I kept reaching for my phone in my pocket, but it wasn't there. I couldn't check social media or watch videos. But then, something interesting happened. I started to notice the world around me more. I talked to my family more during dinner. I even picked up a book that I had wanted to read for months. It was a difficult but valuable day. It taught me that I should control my phone, not let my phone control me.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • impossible:불가능한
  • anxious:불안한, 걱정하는
  • notice:알아차리다, 주목하다
  • valuable:가치 있는, 소중한

Questions

32-1.What was the writer's special homework?

  • ① To write a report about smartphones
  • ② To spend a day without a smartphone
  • ③ To create a new smartphone app
  • ④ To fix a broken phone
  • ⑤ To teach his parents how to use a smartphone

32-2.How did the writer feel at the beginning of the day?

  • ① Happy and free
  • ② Calm and relaxed
  • ③ Anxious and uncomfortable
  • ④ Excited and hopeful
  • ⑤ Bored and sleepy

32-3.위 글을 통해 '스마트폰 없는 하루'가 글쓴이에게 준 긍정적인 변화가 아닌 것은?

  • ① 가족과 더 많은 대화를 나누었다.
  • ② 주변 세상에 더 관심을 갖게 되었다.
  • ③ 오랫동안 읽고 싶었던 책을 읽었다.
  • ④ 늦잠을 자지 않고 일찍 일어났다.
  • ⑤ 스마트폰 사용을 조절해야겠다고 깨달았다.
35
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 33]

The Northern Lights

Have you ever heard of the Aurora Borealis, also known as the Northern Lights? It is a natural light display in the sky, usually seen in the high-latitude regions around the Arctic. The lights appear in many colors, from pale green and pink to shades of red, yellow, and blue. They dance across the sky in beautiful patterns. What causes this amazing phenomenon? It happens when electrically charged particles from the sun enter the Earth's atmosphere. For many ancient cultures, the Northern Lights were a source of mystery and legends. Some believed they were spirits of the dead. Today, many tourists travel to places like Alaska, Norway, and Iceland just to witness this spectacular show of nature.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • natural:자연의
  • phenomenon:현상
  • particle:입자
  • atmosphere:대기
  • spectacular:장관을 이루는, 극적인

Questions

33-1.What is the main topic of the passage?

  • ① A travel guide to Alaska
  • ② Ancient legends about spirits
  • ③ An explanation of the Northern Lights
  • ④ The dangers of solar particles
  • ⑤ The different colors of the rainbow

33-2.Where can the Northern Lights usually be seen?

  • ① In tropical regions near the equator
  • ② In big cities with a lot of lights
  • ③ In high-latitude regions near the Arctic
  • ④ Under the ocean
  • ⑤ On the sun itself

33-3.위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.

  • ① 오로라는 인공적으로 만들어진 빛이다.
  • ② 오로라는 태양에서 온 입자가 지구 대기와 만나 발생한다.
  • ③ 옛날 사람들은 오로라의 원인을 정확히 알고 있었다.
  • ④ 오로라는 항상 똑같은 모양으로 나타난다.
  • ⑤ 오로라는 붉은색으로만 보인다.
36
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 34]

My First Pet, a Goldfish

When I was six years old, I won a goldfish at a school fair. I named him Goldie. He was my very first pet. I was so excited. I put him in a small fishbowl in my room. Every day, I would feed him and watch him swim around. But I didn't know how to take care of a fish properly. I gave him too much food, and I didn't change the water often enough. One morning, I found Goldie floating at the top of the water. He was gone. I was heartbroken. My mom explained that it wasn't my fault and that taking care of a living creature is a big responsibility. That experience, though sad, taught me an important lesson about life and responsibility.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • goldfish:금붕어
  • fishbowl:어항
  • properly:제대로, 적절하게
  • heartbroken:비통해하는, 가슴이 찢어지는
  • creature:생물
  • responsibility:책임

Questions

34-1.How did the writer get his first pet?

  • ① His parents bought it for him.
  • ② He found it in a river.
  • ③ He won it at a school fair.
  • ④ His friend gave it to him.
  • ⑤ He bought it at a pet store.

34-2.Why did the goldfish die?

  • ① It was too old.
  • ② The writer didn't feed it at all.
  • ③ The writer didn't take care of it properly.
  • ④ The fishbowl was too big.
  • ⑤ A cat ate it.

34-3.What lesson did the writer learn from this experience?

  • ① Goldfish are not good pets.
  • ② School fairs are not fun.
  • ③ Taking care of a pet is a big responsibility.
  • ④ He should never have a pet again.
  • ⑤ Crying does not help solve problems.
37
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 35]

The Power of a Simple "Thank You"

How often do you say "thank you"? These two simple words are more powerful than you might think. Saying "thank you" is a way of showing gratitude. It tells someone that you appreciate what they have done for you. It can be for a big thing, like a birthday present, or a small thing, like someone holding a door open for you. When you thank someone, it makes them feel good. It makes them feel valued and respected. This can encourage them to be kind to others as well. Showing gratitude is also good for you. It helps you focus on the positive things in your life and can make you feel happier. So, don't forget to use this magic phrase. It costs nothing but can make a big difference.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • gratitude:감사, 고마움
  • appreciate:고마워하다, 진가를 알아보다
  • valued:소중한, 귀한
  • encourage:격려하다, 부추기다
  • difference:차이

Questions

35-1.What is the main message of the passage?

  • ① The importance of saying "thank you"
  • ② How to get more birthday presents
  • ③ The different ways to open a door
  • ④ Why people should be more positive
  • ⑤ The definition of gratitude

35-2.According to the passage, how does saying "thank you" affect the person who hears it?

  • ① It makes them feel tired.
  • ② It makes them feel valued and respected.
  • ③ It makes them feel confused.
  • ④ It makes them feel sorry.
  • ⑤ It costs them money.

35-3.위 글에 따르면, "감사합니다"라고 말하는 것이 우리 자신에게 좋은 이유는 무엇인가?

  • ① 돈을 벌게 해준다.
  • ② 새로운 친구를 사귀게 해준다.
  • ③ 삶의 긍정적인 면에 집중하게 하고 더 행복하게 만든다.
  • ④ 시험을 더 잘 보게 해준다.
  • ⑤ 다른 사람들을 이기게 해준다.
38
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 36]

A Mistake in a Test

Last week, I had a big math test. I studied really hard for it. During the test, I thought I did well. But when I got my test paper back, I saw a big red "X" on the last question. I had made a simple calculation mistake. Because of that one mistake, I got a B instead of an A. I was so disappointed and angry with myself. I complained to my older sister. She listened and then said, "Everyone makes mistakes. The important thing is what you do next. Do you understand why you made the mistake? Can you learn from it?" Her words made me think. I realized that one test score doesn't define who I am. I decided to learn from my mistake and be more careful next time.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • calculation:계산
  • mistake:실수
  • disappointed:실망한
  • complain:불평하다
  • define:규정하다, 정의하다

Questions

36-1.Why was the writer disappointed with his math test?

  • ① The test was too difficult.
  • ② He didn't have enough time to finish.
  • ③ He made a simple mistake and got a lower grade.
  • ④ He forgot to write his name on the paper.
  • ⑤ He failed the test completely.

36-2.What advice did the writer's sister give him?

  • ① She told him to complain to the teacher.
  • ② She said math is not important.
  • ③ She told him to learn from his mistake.
  • ④ She offered to do his homework for him.
  • ⑤ She told him to give up on math.

36-3.이 글을 통해 글쓴이가 얻은 교훈으로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① 수학 시험은 항상 어렵다.
  • ② 실수는 절대 해서는 안 된다.
  • ③ 시험 점수가 그 사람의 모든 것을 결정한다.
  • ④ 실수로부터 배우고 다음을 준비하는 것이 중요하다.
  • ⑤ 누나에게 불평하는 것은 도움이 되지 않는다.
39
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 37]

The Secret Life of Trees

We see trees every day, but do we really know them? Trees are much more complex than they look. They have a secret life underground. Their roots spread out like a huge network. Through this network, trees can "talk" to each other. They can share water and nutrients with trees that are sick or in need. They can also send warning signals. For example, if one tree is attacked by harmful insects, it can release a chemical signal through its roots to warn its neighbors. The other trees then produce chemicals to defend themselves. This underground network is sometimes called the "Wood Wide Web." It shows that trees are not just individual plants, but a community that helps each other.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • complex:복잡한
  • network:망, 네트워크
  • nutrient:영양분
  • produce:생산하다
  • community:공동체

Questions

37-1.What is the "Wood Wide Web"?

  • ① A website about trees
  • ② A type of harmful insect
  • ③ The underground root network that connects trees
  • ④ A book about the secret life of trees
  • ⑤ A new kind of computer network

37-2.How can trees "talk" to each other?

  • ① By shaking their leaves
  • ② Through their underground root network
  • ③ By making sounds
  • ④ By changing their colors
  • ⑤ They cannot talk to each other.

37-3.위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?

  • ① 나무는 보이는 것보다 훨씬 복잡하다.
  • ② 나무는 뿌리를 통해 서로 영양분을 공유할 수 있다.
  • ③ 한 나무가 공격을 받으면 이웃 나무에게 경고를 보낼 수 있다.
  • ④ 나무는 서로 돕는 공동체를 이루고 있다.
  • ⑤ 나무는 오직 자기 자신만을 위해 살아간다.
40
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 38]

My First Part-time Job

When I turned 16, I decided to get my first part-time job. I wanted to earn my own money and gain some work experience. I got a job at a local bakery. My main duty was to pack bread and cookies into bags for customers. I also had to keep the display cases clean and tidy. The job was not easy. I had to stand for hours, and it was busy, especially on weekends. But I enjoyed the smell of fresh bread and the friendly atmosphere. My boss was kind and taught me a lot. I learned how to be more responsible and how to communicate with different people. The best part was getting my first paycheck. I felt so proud because I had earned it myself.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • duty:임무, 의무
  • tidy:깔끔한, 정돈된
  • atmosphere:분위기
  • responsible:책임감 있는
  • paycheck:급료, 봉급

Questions

38-1.Why did the writer want to get a part-time job?

  • ① To meet new friends
  • ② To learn how to bake bread
  • ③ To earn money and gain experience
  • ④ Because his parents told him to
  • ⑤ To get free bread

38-2.What was the writer's main duty at the bakery?

  • ① Baking bread and cookies
  • ② Taking orders from customers
  • ③ Washing the dishes
  • ④ Packing bread and cookies for customers
  • ⑤ Delivering bread to houses

38-3.이 아르바이트 경험을 통해 글쓴이가 배운 점이 아닌 것은?

  • ① 책임감 있게 행동하는 법
  • ② 다양한 사람들과 소통하는 법
  • ③ 돈을 버는 것의 가치
  • ④ 맛있는 빵을 굽는 기술
  • ⑤ 깨끗하고 단정하게 유지하는 것의 중요성
41
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 39]

The Art of Origami

Origami is the Japanese art of paper folding. "Ori" means "folding," and "kami" means "paper." Using a single sheet of square paper, you can create amazing shapes like animals, flowers, or boxes, without using scissors or glue. Origami is not just a fun craft; it is also very beneficial. It helps improve concentration because you have to follow the instructions carefully. It also develops hand-eye coordination and fine motor skills. Furthermore, it teaches patience and problem-solving. When your model doesn't look right, you have to figure out what went wrong. The most famous origami shape is the paper crane. There is a Japanese legend that if you fold one thousand paper cranes, your wish will come true.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • beneficial:유익한, 이로운
  • concentration:집중력
  • instruction:설명, 지시
  • legend:전설

Questions

39-1.What is Origami?

  • ① The Japanese art of painting
  • ② The Japanese art of paper folding
  • ③ The Japanese art of flower arrangement
  • ④ The Japanese art of calligraphy
  • ⑤ The Japanese art of sword fighting

39-2.According to the passage, what is a benefit of doing origami?

  • ① It makes you stronger.
  • ② It helps you make money.
  • ③ It improves your cooking skills.
  • ④ It helps improve concentration.
  • ⑤ It teaches you how to use scissors.

39-3.위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.

  • ① 종이접기를 할 때는 가위와 풀이 필수적이다.
  • ② 종이접기는 인내심을 기르는 데 도움이 된다.
  • ③ 가장 유명한 종이접기 모양은 종이배이다.
  • ④ 종이학 천 마리를 접으면 돈을 많이 벌게 된다는 전설이 있다.
  • ⑤ 종이접기는 주로 둥근 모양의 종이를 사용한다.
42
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 40]

A Solar Eclipse

A solar eclipse is a rare and amazing astronomical event. It happens when the Moon passes directly between the Sun and Earth, and the Moon fully or partially blocks the Sun. This casts a shadow onto Earth. For a total solar eclipse, the sky becomes dark as if it were night. The stars come out, and the air gets cooler. It is a truly awesome sight. However, it is very dangerous to look directly at the sun during an eclipse. The sun's rays can permanently damage your eyes. You must use special eclipse glasses or a pinhole projector to watch it safely. The next time you hear about a solar eclipse happening, make sure you are prepared to view it safely.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • astronomical:천문학의
  • partially:부분적으로
  • shadow:그림자
  • dangerous:위험한
  • damage:손상시키다
  • permanently:영구적으로

Questions

40-1.What causes a solar eclipse?

  • ① The Earth passes between the Sun and the Moon.
  • ② The Moon passes between the Sun and the Earth.
  • ③ A big cloud covers the Sun.
  • ④ The Sun moves away from the Earth.
  • ⑤ The Earth stops spinning.

40-2.Why is it dangerous to look directly at a solar eclipse?

  • ① It can make you feel dizzy.
  • ② The sky is too dark to see.
  • ③ The sun's rays can damage your eyes.
  • ④ It can make you feel cold.
  • ⑤ It is against the law.

40-3.위 글에서 일식을 안전하게 관찰하는 방법으로 언급된 것은?

  • ① 맨눈으로 직접 보기
  • ② 일반 선글라스 사용하기
  • ③ 특수 안경이나 바늘구멍 사진기 사용하기
  • ④ 망원경으로 보기
  • ⑤ 창문을 통해 보기
43
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 41]

The School Newspaper Club

I am a member of the school newspaper club. We publish a new edition of our newspaper, "The Eagle Times," every month. As a reporter, my job is to find interesting stories happening in our school. Last month, I wrote an article about the new recycling program. I interviewed the science teacher and some students. It was exciting to see my article in print. Our club has different roles. Some students are editors who check for grammar and spelling mistakes. Others are photographers who take pictures for the articles. We all work together as a team. Being in the newspaper club has improved my writing skills and taught me how to meet a deadline. It's a lot of work, but it's very rewarding.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • publish:발행하다, 출판하다
  • edition:(간행물의) 판, 호
  • interview:인터뷰하다
  • deadline:마감 기한
  • rewarding:보람 있는

Questions

41-1.What is the writer's role in the newspaper club?

  • ① Editor
  • ② Photographer
  • ③ Reporter
  • ④ Designer
  • ⑤ Publisher

41-2.According to the passage, what is NOT a benefit of being in the club?

  • ① It improves writing skills.
  • ② It teaches how to meet a deadline.
  • ③ It helps make a lot of money.
  • ④ It is a rewarding experience.
  • ⑤ It teaches teamwork.

41-3.위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.

  • ① 학교 신문의 이름은 'The Lion News'이다.
  • ② 학교 신문은 매주 발행된다.
  • ③ 글쓴이는 지난달에 학교 축제에 대한 기사를 썼다.
  • ④ 신문 동아리에는 기자, 편집자, 사진 기자 등 다양한 역할이 있다.
  • ⑤ 신문 동아리 활동은 일이 적고 매우 쉽다.
44
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 42]

The Four Seasons

Each season of the year has its own special beauty. Spring is a time of new beginnings. The weather gets warmer, flowers bloom, and birds return from the south. Summer is the warmest season. The days are long, and it's a perfect time for swimming and eating ice cream. Then comes autumn, or fall. The leaves on the trees change color to red, yellow, and orange. The air becomes cool and crisp. It's the season of harvest. Finally, winter arrives. It can be very cold, and sometimes it snows. It's a cozy time to drink hot chocolate and read books by the fire. Each season has its own unique charm, and I enjoy them all.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • bloom:꽃이 피다
  • crisp:(공기가) 상쾌한, 쌀쌀한
  • harvest:수확, 추수
  • unique:독특한, 고유의
  • charm:매력

Questions

42-1.What is the main purpose of the passage?

  • ① To complain about the weather
  • ② To describe the beauty of each season
  • ③ To give advice on what to wear in winter
  • ④ To explain why leaves change color
  • ⑤ To compare summer and winter

42-2.According to the passage, in which season do flowers bloom?

  • ① Spring
  • ② Summer
  • ③ Autumn
  • ④ Winter
  • ⑤ All year round

42-3.위 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?

  • ① 봄에는 날씨가 따뜻해진다.
  • ② 여름은 수영하기에 좋은 계절이다.
  • ③ 가을에는 나뭇잎이 초록색으로 변한다.
  • ④ 겨울은 춥고 때로는 눈이 온다.
  • ⑤ 글쓴이는 모든 계절의 매력을 즐긴다.
45
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 43]

A Public Library

A public library is a wonderful place in our community. It's not just a building full of books. It's a place where you can learn, explore, and connect with others. The best part is that most of its services are free. You can borrow books, magazines, and even movies. If you need a quiet place to study, the library is perfect. Many libraries also offer free Wi-Fi and have computers for public use. They often host events like story time for children, book clubs for adults, and workshops on various topics. The librarians are always there to help you find what you need. A library is a valuable resource for everyone, no matter how old you are. It opens up a world of knowledge and imagination.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • explore:탐험하다
  • borrow:빌리다
  • workshop:워크숍, 공동 연구회
  • resource:자원, 자료
  • imagination:상상력

Questions

43-1.What is the main idea of the passage?

  • ① The history of public libraries
  • ② The many benefits and services of a public library
  • ③ How to become a librarian
  • ④ The rules you must follow in a library
  • ⑤ The most popular books in the library

43-2.Which service is NOT mentioned in the passage?

  • ① Borrowing books and movies
  • ② A quiet place to study
  • ③ Free Wi-Fi and computers
  • ④ Selling coffee and snacks
  • ⑤ Events like story time and workshops

43-3.위 글에 따르면, 도서관이 우리에게 왜 가치 있는 장소인가?

  • ① 최신 영화를 가장 먼저 볼 수 있어서
  • ② 돈을 많이 벌 수 있는 기회를 제공해서
  • ③ 지식과 상상력의 세계를 열어주는 귀중한 자원이기 때문에
  • ④ 친구들과 자유롭게 떠들며 놀 수 있어서
  • ⑤ 항상 조용하고 사람이 없어서
46
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 44]

The Story of Helen Keller

Helen Keller was born in 1880. When she was a baby, she had a serious illness that left her both blind and deaf. She could not see or hear. She lived in a dark and silent world, and she was often angry and frustrated. When she was six, her parents hired a teacher named Anne Sullivan. Anne was very patient. She taught Helen how to communicate by spelling words into her hand. The first word Helen understood was "water." It was a breakthrough moment. From then on, Helen learned quickly. She learned to read Braille and even went to college. She became a famous writer and speaker, inspiring millions of people around the world. Her story teaches us that with courage and determination, we can overcome any obstacle.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • blind:눈이 먼
  • deaf:귀가 먹은
  • Braille:(맹인용) 점자
  • determination:결심, 투지
  • obstacle:장애물, 난관

Questions

44-1.What happened to Helen Keller when she was a baby?

  • ① She learned to read and write.
  • ② She became blind and deaf due to an illness.
  • ③ She met her teacher, Anne Sullivan.
  • ④ She wrote her first book.
  • ⑤ She traveled around the world.

44-2.Who was Anne Sullivan?

  • ① Helen's mother
  • ② Helen's doctor
  • ③ Helen's best friend
  • ④ Helen's teacher
  • ⑤ The president of her college

44-3.헬렌 켈러의 이야기가 우리에게 주는 교훈으로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① 질병은 매우 위험하다.
  • ② 좋은 선생님을 만나는 것은 행운이다.
  • ③ 용기와 굳은 결심이 있다면 어떤 어려움도 극복할 수 있다.
  • ④ 대학 교육은 성공에 필수적이다.
  • ⑤ 유명한 작가가 되는 것은 어렵다.
47
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 45]

The Importance of Teamwork

In a basketball game, one player, no matter how talented, cannot win the game alone. They need their teammates. They need to pass the ball, defend together, and communicate on the court. This is called teamwork. Teamwork is not just important in sports. It's essential in many parts of our lives, like in school projects or even in a family. When we work as a team, we can combine our different skills and ideas. Someone might be good at planning, while another person is good at drawing. By working together, we can achieve a goal that would be difficult to achieve alone. Teamwork also teaches us how to listen to others, compromise, and respect different opinions. It makes the final result much better.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • essential:필수적인
  • combine:결합하다
  • compromise:타협하다
  • respect:존중하다
  • result:결과

Questions

45-1.What is the main topic of the passage?

  • ① The rules of basketball
  • ② The benefits of working alone
  • ③ The importance and benefits of teamwork
  • ④ How to be a good leader
  • ⑤ The best way to plan a project

45-2.According to the passage, what can we do when we work as a team?

  • ① We can always win.
  • ② We can ignore other people's opinions.
  • ③ We can combine different skills and ideas.
  • ④ We can finish the work faster without planning.
  • ⑤ We can make one person do all the work.

45-3.위 글에서 언급된 팀워크의 장점이 아닌 것은?

  • ① 혼자서는 어려운 목표를 달성할 수 있다.
  • ② 다른 사람의 의견을 듣는 법을 배운다.
  • ③ 항상 내 의견을 주장할 수 있다.
  • ④ 다양한 기술과 아이디어를 합칠 수 있다.
  • ⑤ 더 나은 결과를 만들어 낸다.
48
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 46]

A Surprise Visitor

One evening, while I was doing my homework, I heard a tapping sound on my window. I ignored it, thinking it was just the wind. But the tapping came again, a little louder this time. I felt a bit scared. I slowly walked to the window and pulled back the curtain. To my surprise, a small owl was sitting on the windowsill! It had big, round, yellow eyes and was looking right at me. I had never seen an owl so close before. I stood there for a moment, just watching it. The owl tilted its head and then flew away silently into the night. It was a magical and unexpected moment. I will never forget my little surprise visitor.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • tapping:톡톡 두드리는 소리
  • ignore:무시하다
  • curtain:커튼
  • surprise:놀라움
  • unexpected:예기치 않은, 뜻밖의

Questions

46-1.What was the source of the tapping sound on the window?

  • ① The wind
  • ② A tree branch
  • ③ A small owl
  • ④ A person
  • ⑤ The rain

46-2.How did the writer feel when he first heard the sound?

  • ① Happy
  • ② Excited
  • ③ A little scared
  • ④ Bored
  • ⑤ Angry

46-3.위 글의 분위기로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① sad and gloomy (슬프고 우울한)
  • ② busy and chaotic (바쁘고 혼란스러운)
  • ③ funny and humorous (재미있고 유머러스한)
  • ④ magical and mysterious (마법 같고 신비로운)
  • ⑤ romantic and sweet (낭만적이고 달콤한)
49
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 47]

The History of Chocolate

Chocolate is one of the world's most popular foods, but where did it come from? The story of chocolate begins in ancient Central America with the Maya and Aztec people. They made a bitter, frothy drink from the beans of the cacao tree. They believed cacao was a gift from the gods. This drink was very different from the sweet chocolate we eat today. In the 1500s, Spanish explorers brought cacao beans back to Europe. The Europeans added sugar and milk to the drink, which made it much more popular. For a long time, chocolate was a luxury item only for the rich. It wasn't until the 19th century that solid chocolate bars were invented, making chocolate a treat that everyone could afford.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • ancient:고대의
  • bitter:(맛이) 쓴
  • luxury:사치, 호화로움
  • invent:발명하다
  • afford:(~을 살) 형편이 되다, 여유가 되다

Questions

47-1.What is the passage mainly about?

  • ① How to make chocolate
  • ② The health benefits of chocolate
  • ③ The history of chocolate
  • ④ The different types of chocolate
  • ⑤ The economy of Central America

47-2.How was the original chocolate drink made by the Maya and Aztec people?

  • ① It was sweet and solid.
  • ② It was bitter and frothy.
  • ③ It was mixed with milk and sugar.
  • ④ It was served cold with ice.
  • ⑤ It was eaten as a bar.

47-3.위 글의 내용과 일치하는 것을 고르시오.

  • ① 초콜릿은 유럽에서 처음 만들어졌다.
  • ② 고대 마야인들은 카카오를 신의 선물이라고 믿었다.
  • ③ 스페인 탐험가들은 유럽에 설탕을 가져갔다.
  • ④ 초콜릿은 처음부터 모든 사람이 즐길 수 있는 음식이었다.
  • ⑤ 오늘날 우리가 먹는 초콜릿과 옛날 초콜릿 음료는 맛이 똑같다.
50
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 48]

My Fear of Public Speaking

I have a big fear of public speaking. Whenever I have to give a presentation in front of the class, my hands get sweaty, my heart beats like a drum, and my mind goes blank. Last week, I had to present my science project. I was so nervous. The night before, I practiced in front of the mirror many times. I also made cue cards with key points to help me remember. When it was my turn, I took a deep breath and started. At first, my voice was shaky. But as I talked, I started to feel more confident. I remembered my practice and used my cue cards. In the end, I finished my presentation without any big mistakes. My teacher and friends clapped. I was so relieved and proud of myself for facing my fear.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • presentation:발표
  • blank:텅 빈, 백지의
  • cue card:(발표자를 위한) 대본 카드
  • shaky:떨리는
  • confident:자신감 있는
  • relieved:안도하는

Questions

48-1.What is the writer's biggest fear?

  • ① Taking tests
  • ② Public speaking
  • ③ Science projects
  • ④ Talking to the teacher
  • ⑤ Making mistakes

48-2.What did the writer do to prepare for his presentation?

  • ① He asked a friend to do it for him.
  • ② He practiced in front of a mirror and made cue cards.
  • ③ He decided not to go to school.
  • ④ He read a book about public speaking.
  • ⑤ He drank a lot of water.

48-3.글쓴이가 발표를 마친 후 느꼈을 심경으로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① disappointed and angry (실망하고 화난)
  • ② bored and indifferent (지루하고 무관심한)
  • ③ sad and lonely (슬프고 외로운)
  • ④ relieved and proud (안도하고 자랑스러운)
  • ⑤ confused and worried (혼란스럽고 걱정되는)
51
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 49]

How Do Birds Fly?

Have you ever watched a bird fly and wondered how it does that? Birds are masters of flight, and their bodies are perfectly designed for it. First, they have very light bones. Many of their bones are hollow, which makes their skeletons much lighter than those of mammals. Second, they have powerful flight muscles in their chest to flap their wings. Third, and most importantly, they have feathers. Feathers are light, strong, and flexible. They help birds to lift off the ground and steer in the air. The shape of a bird's wings is also important. It creates a difference in air pressure, which helps to lift the bird up. It's a perfect combination of lightness, power, and aerodynamic design.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • design:설계하다, 디자인하다
  • hollow:속이 빈
  • mammal:포유동물
  • steer:조종하다
  • aerodynamic:공기 역학의

Questions

49-1.What is the main question this passage answers?

  • ① Why do birds sing?
  • ② Where do birds go in winter?
  • ③ How do birds fly?
  • ④ What do birds eat?
  • ⑤ How do birds build nests?

49-2.Which of the following is NOT a reason why birds can fly, according to the passage?

  • ① They have light, hollow bones.
  • ② They have powerful flight muscles.
  • ③ They eat very little food.
  • ④ They have feathers.
  • ⑤ Their wings have a special shape.

49-3.위 글에서 새의 뼈가 가진 특징으로 언급된 것은 무엇인가?

  • ① 매우 단단하고 무겁다.
  • ② 속이 비어 있어 가볍다.
  • ③ 쉽게 부러진다.
  • ④ 근육이 많이 붙어 있다.
  • ⑤ 깃털로 덮여 있다.
52
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 50]

A Letter to My Future Self

Today in class, our teacher asked us to write a letter to our future selves. We had to write about our current life, our friends, our dreams, and our worries. We then put the letters in a time capsule, which we will open on our graduation day in three years. In my letter, I wrote about my dream of becoming a veterinarian because I love animals. I wrote about my best friend, Sora, and the funny things we do together. I also wrote about my worry that I'm not good enough at math. I asked my future self: "Did you get into a good university? Are you still friends with Sora? Did you get a puppy?" It was a strange but interesting experience. I wonder how much I will have changed in three years.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • current:현재의
  • time capsule:타임캡슐
  • graduation:졸업
  • veterinarian:수의사
  • worry:걱정

Questions

50-1.What did the writer do in class?

  • ① He wrote a letter to his teacher.
  • ② He wrote a letter to his best friend.
  • ③ He wrote a letter to his future self.
  • ④ He made a time capsule.
  • ⑤ He gave a presentation about his dream.

50-2.Which of the following did the writer NOT write about in his letter?

  • ① His dream of becoming a veterinarian
  • ② His best friend, Sora
  • ③ His favorite food
  • ④ His worry about math
  • ⑤ Questions for his future self

50-3.When will the writer open the time capsule?

  • ① Next week
  • ② Next year
  • ③ On his birthday
  • ④ On graduation day
  • ⑤ He will never open it.
53
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 51]

The World of Sign Language

Have you ever seen people talking with their hands? They are likely using sign language. Sign language is a complete language that uses hand gestures, facial expressions, and body movements to communicate. It is not a universal language; different countries have their own sign languages, just like spoken languages. For example, American Sign Language (ASL) is different from British Sign Language (BSL). For many deaf and hard-of-hearing people, sign language is their primary way of communication. Learning sign language can be a wonderful experience. It allows you to communicate with a new community of people. It also helps you become more aware of the diverse ways people interact with the world.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • gesture:몸짓, 제스처
  • facial expression:얼굴 표정
  • communicate:의사소통하다
  • universal:보편적인, 세계적인
  • interact:소통하다, 교류하다

Questions

51-1.What is sign language?

  • ① A secret code used by spies
  • ② A universal language that everyone knows
  • ③ A language that uses hands, face, and body to communicate
  • ④ A simple way to say "hello" and "goodbye"
  • ⑤ A written language for deaf people

51-2.According to the passage, which statement is TRUE?

  • ① Sign language is the same all over the world.
  • ② Only deaf people can learn sign language.
  • ③ American Sign Language and British Sign Language are identical.
  • ④ Sign language uses only hand gestures.
  • ⑤ Different countries have their own sign languages.

51-3.위 글에서 수화를 배우는 것의 장점으로 언급되지 않은 것은?

  • ① 새로운 공동체의 사람들과 의사소통할 수 있다.
  • ② 사람들이 세상을 살아가는 다양한 방식에 대해 더 잘 알게 된다.
  • ③ 더 좋은 직업을 쉽게 구할 수 있다.
  • ④ 청각 장애를 가진 사람들과 소통하는 데 도움이 된다.
  • ⑤ 손과 얼굴 표정을 사용하는 새로운 언어를 배우게 된다.
54
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 52]

A Camping Trip Gone Wrong

My family loves camping. Last summer, we went to a new campsite in the mountains. We set up our tent and were excited for a night under the stars. However, the weather forecast was wrong. Suddenly, dark clouds gathered, and it started to rain heavily. The wind was so strong that our tent almost blew away. We were all huddled inside, listening to the thunder. The rain leaked into our tent, and our sleeping bags got wet. We couldn't make a campfire or cook our dinner. We had to eat cold sandwiches. It was not the fun camping trip we had expected. But looking back, it was a memorable adventure. It taught us to always be prepared for unexpected situations.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • campsite:야영지, 캠핑장
  • forecast:예보
  • huddle:옹기종기 모이다
  • leak:(물, 빛 등이) 새다
  • expect:기대하다, 예상하다
  • adventure:모험

Questions

52-1.What was the main problem during the camping trip?

  • ① They forgot to bring their tent.
  • ② They got lost in the mountains.
  • ③ The weather suddenly turned bad.
  • ④ They saw a wild animal.
  • ⑤ They ran out of food.

52-2.What did the family have for dinner?

  • ① Hot dogs cooked over a campfire
  • ② Barbecue
  • ③ Fish they caught from the river
  • ④ Cold sandwiches
  • ⑤ They skipped dinner.

52-3.이 경험을 통해 글쓴이의 가족이 배운 교훈은 무엇인가?

  • ① 캠핑은 항상 재미있다.
  • ② 산에서는 날씨가 변하지 않는다.
  • ③ 예상치 못한 상황에 항상 대비해야 한다.
  • ④ 샌드위치는 최고의 캠핑 음식이다.
  • ⑤ 캠핑은 다시는 가지 말아야 한다.
55
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 53]

The Great Wall of China

The Great Wall of China is one of the most impressive structures ever built by humans. It is not a single wall but a series of walls and fortifications. It stretches for thousands of kilometers across northern China. The main purpose of the wall was to protect China from invasions by nomadic tribes from the north. The construction of the wall started over 2,000 years ago and continued for centuries. Millions of soldiers and common people worked to build it. The wall was built using various materials, including stone, brick, and earth. Today, the Great Wall is a famous tourist attraction. It is a symbol of China's long history and the perseverance of its people.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • structure:구조물
  • fortification:요새, 방어 시설
  • purpose:목적
  • invasion:침략, 침입
  • attraction:명소, 명물
  • perseverance:인내, 끈기

Questions

53-1.What was the main purpose of the Great Wall of China?

  • ① To be a tourist attraction
  • ② To protect China from invasions
  • ③ To be a road for trade
  • ④ To mark the center of the country
  • ⑤ To show the emperor's power

53-2.Which statement about the Great Wall is NOT true?

  • ① It is a single, continuous wall.
  • ② It stretches for thousands of kilometers.
  • ③ It was built over many centuries.
  • ④ Millions of people worked to build it.
  • ⑤ It is now a famous tourist site.

53-3.위 글을 통해 만리장성이 상징하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① 중국의 빠른 경제 발전
  • ② 중국의 아름다운 자연
  • ③ 중국의 오랜 역사와 인내심
  • ④ 북방 유목민과의 평화
  • ⑤ 현대 건축 기술의 우수성
56
Vera's Flavor - PART 1 내용 파악
[지문 54]

Why Do We Yawn?

Everyone yawns. You might yawn when you are tired or bored. But have you ever wondered why we do it? For a long time, scientists believed that we yawn to get more oxygen into our blood. However, recent studies suggest a different reason. The main theory now is that yawning helps to cool down our brain. Your brain works best when it is at a certain temperature. When your brain gets too warm, a yawn can help bring cooler blood to it. This might explain why we yawn more when we are tired, as tiredness can raise brain temperature. It also explains why yawning is contagious. Seeing someone else yawn might be a signal to our brain that it's time to cool down too.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • yawn:하품하다
  • oxygen:산소
  • cool down:식히다
  • temperature:온도
  • contagious:전염성이 있는

Questions

54-1.What is the new theory about why we yawn?

  • ① To get more oxygen
  • ② To show that we are bored
  • ③ To exercise our jaw muscles
  • ④ To cool down our brain
