  • 25-1: ①동물을 의인화하여 교훈을 주는 이야기이므로 '우화'에 해당함
  • 25-2: ④고양이와 개들 때문에 겁에 질려서("terrified") 시골로 돌아가기로 결심함
  • 25-3: ②시골쥐의 마지막 말 "I would rather eat my simple food in peace than have your fine food in fear"가 이야기의 핵심 교훈임

Passage 26

  • 26-1: ②글 전체가 좋은 친구가 되기 위한 조건, 즉 '좋은 친구의 자질'에 대해 설명하고 있음
  • 26-2: ④좋은 친구는 자신의 문제에 대해 잘 들어주는 사람("a good listener")이라고 했으므로, 자기 얘기만 하는 것은 좋은 친구의 자질이 아님
  • 26-3: ②'우정은 쌍방향 길이다'라는 의미로, 상대방에게 좋은 친구가 되어주기를 바란다면 나 자신도 그래야 한다는 상호적인 관계를 의미함

Passage 27

  • 27-1: ②여왕벌, 수벌, 일벌 등 군집 내에서 각 꿀벌이 맡은 다른 역할에 대해 설명하고 있음
  • 27-2: ④본문에 "Her only job is to lay eggs"라고 명시됨
  • 27-3: ⑤꿀은 겨울을 나기 위한 자신들의 식량("their food for the winter")이라고 했으므로, 인간을 위해 만든다는 것은 틀린 설명

Passage 28

  • 28-1: ③글의 첫 문장에 "I want to be a webtoon artist"라고 명시됨
  • 28-2: ③그림 실력, 이야기 아이디어, 디지털 도구 사용법, 노력 등은 언급되었지만 노래 실력은 언급되지 않음
  • 28-3: ③자신의 꿈이 무엇인지 명확히 알고, 그것을 이루기 위해 무엇을 해야 하는지 구체적으로 계획하고 노력하려는 자세를 보임

Passage 29

  • 29-1: ③웃음이 우리 몸과 마음에 미치는 여러 가지 긍정적인 효과에 대해 설명하고 있음
  • 29-2: ③웃을 때 우리 몸은 엔도르핀이라는 화학 물질을 방출한다고("releases chemicals called endorphins") 나옴
  • 29-3: ④고통 완화, 면역력 향상, 근육 이완, 관계 강화 등은 언급되었지만 기억력 향상은 언급되지 않음

Passage 30

  • 30-1: ③해변에서 편지가 든 유리병을 발견했다고 나옴
  • 30-2: ③1985년 폭풍우에 갇힌 선원이 쓴 편지였음
  • 30-3: ③편지에 "his ship was caught in a big storm in 1985"라고 쓰여 있었음
106
Vera's Flavor - Answer Key Part 1: 내용 파악 (31-40)

Passage 31

  • 31-1: ②학교 연극에 참여한 경험을 중심으로 글이 전개됨
  • 31-2: ③본문에 "I was chosen to play the role of the Tortoise"라고 명시됨
  • 31-3: ④본문에 "I remembered all my lines"라고 했으므로, 대사를 잊어버렸다는 것은 틀린 설명

Passage 32

  • 32-1: ②선생님이 내준 숙제는 하루 동안 스마트폰 없이 지내는 것이었음
  • 32-2: ③본문에 "I felt anxious"라고 명시되어 있으며, 주머니에서 계속 폰을 찾는 등 불안하고 불편한 모습을 보임
  • 32-3: ④스마트폰 알람이 없어서 늦잠을 잤다고("woke up late") 했으므로, 일찍 일어났다는 것은 틀린 설명

Passage 33

  • 33-1: ③오로라가 무엇인지, 원인, 관련된 전설, 관측 장소 등을 설명하고 있음
  • 33-2: ③본문에 "usually seen in the high-latitude regions around the Arctic"이라고 명시됨
  • 33-3: ②본문에 "It happens when electrically charged particles from the sun enter the Earth's atmosphere"라고 명시됨

Passage 34

  • 34-1: ③학교 축제에서 금붕어를 탔다고("won a goldfish at a school fair") 나옴
  • 34-2: ③너무 많은 먹이를 주고 물을 자주 갈아주지 않는 등 제대로 돌보지 못했다고("didn't take care of a fish properly") 나옴
  • 34-3: ③글의 마지막에 "taking care of a living creature is a big responsibility"라는 교훈을 배웠다고 명시됨

Passage 35

  • 35-1: ①글 전체가 '감사합니다'라는 말이 왜 중요하고 어떤 힘을 가졌는지 설명하고 있음
  • 35-2: ②본문에 "it makes them feel valued and respected"라고 명시됨
  • 35-3: ③본문에 "It helps you focus on the positive things in your life and can make you feel happier"라고 명시됨

Passage 36

  • 36-1: ③간단한 계산 실수를 해서 A 대신 B를 받았기 때문에 실망했다고 나옴
  • 36-2: ③누나가 실수로부터 배우는 것이 중요하다고("learn from it") 조언함
  • 36-3: ④누나의 조언을 통해 실수에서 배우고 다음을 준비하는 것이 중요하다는 교훈을 얻음

Passage 37

  • 37-1: ③나무들을 연결하는 지하의 뿌리 네트워크를 "Wood Wide Web"이라고 부른다고 설명함
  • 37-2: ②뿌리 네트워크를 통해 화학 신호를 보내 "대화"한다고 나옴
  • 37-3: ⑤나무들은 서로 영양분을 나누고 경고를 보내는 등 서로 돕는 공동체라고 했으므로, 자기 자신만을 위해 살아간다는 것은 틀린 설명

Passage 38

  • 38-1: ③스스로 돈을 벌고 직업 경험을 얻고 싶어서("earn my own money and gain some work experience") 일을 시작함
  • 38-2: ④주된 임무는 빵과 쿠키를 봉투에 포장하는 것이라고("pack bread and cookies into bags") 나옴
  • 38-3: ④책임감, 소통 능력, 돈의 가치 등은 배웠지만, 빵을 굽는 기술을 배웠다는 내용은 언급되지 않음

Passage 39

  • 39-1: ②글의 첫 문장에 "Origami is the Japanese art of paper folding"이라고 정의됨
  • 39-2: ④본문에 "It helps improve concentration"이라고 명시됨
  • 39-3: ②본문에 "it teaches patience"라고 명시됨

Passage 40

  • 40-1: ②달이 태양과 지구 사이를 지나갈 때 일어난다고 설명됨
  • 40-2: ③본문에 "The sun's rays can permanently damage your eyes"라고 명시됨
  • 40-3: ③본문에 "use special eclipse glasses or a pinhole projector"라고 명시됨
107
Vera's Flavor - Answer Key Part 1: 내용 파악 (41-50)

Passage 41

  • 41-1: ③본문에 "As a reporter, my job is to find interesting stories"라고 명시됨
  • 41-2: ③글쓰기 능력 향상, 마감 준수, 보람, 팀워크 등은 언급되었지만 돈을 버는 것과는 관련이 없음
  • 41-3: ④기자, 편집자, 사진 기자 등 다양한 역할이 있다고 설명함

Passage 42

  • 42-1: ②봄, 여름, 가을, 겨울 각 계절의 특징과 아름다움을 묘사하고 있음
  • 42-2: ①본문에 "Spring is a time... flowers bloom"이라고 명시됨
  • 42-3: ③가을에는 나뭇잎이 빨강, 노랑, 주황색으로 변한다고 했으므로 초록색으로 변한다는 것은 틀린 설명

Passage 43

  • 43-1: ②도서관이 책을 빌리는 곳 이상의 다양한 서비스와 혜택을 제공하는 장소임을 설명하고 있음
  • 43-2: ④책 대여, 공부 장소, 와이파이, 행사 등은 언급되었지만 커피나 간식을 파는 기능은 언급되지 않음
  • 43-3: ③글의 마지막에 "A library is a valuable resource... It opens up a world of knowledge and imagination"이라고 요약하고 있음

Passage 44

  • 44-1: ②아기 때 심각한 병으로 인해 시력과 청력을 모두 잃었다고("left her both blind and deaf") 나옴
  • 44-2: ④헬렌에게 세상을 가르쳐준 선생님 이었음
  • 44-3: ③글의 마지막에 "with courage and determination, we can overcome any obstacle"라고 교훈을 명시하고 있음

Passage 45

  • 45-1: ③농구 경기를 예시로 들어 팀워크의 중요성과 여러 장점을 설명하고 있음
  • 45-2: ③본문에 "we can combine our different skills and ideas"라고 명시됨
  • 45-3: ③팀워크는 다른 사람의 의견을 듣고 타협하는 법을 가르쳐준다고 했으므로, 항상 내 의견만 주장하는 것은 팀워크의 장점이 아님

Passage 46

  • 46-1: ③커튼을 열어보니 창가에 작은 올빼미가 앉아 있었다고 나옴
  • 46-2: ③본문에 "I felt a bit scared"라고 명시됨
  • 46-3: ④한밤중에 예기치 않은 올빼미의 방문은 마법 같고 신비로운 분위기를 자아냄

Passage 47

  • 47-1: ③고대 중앙아메리카에서 시작되어 유럽을 거쳐 오늘날에 이르기까지 초콜릿의 변천 과정을 설명하고 있음
  • 47-2: ②마야와 아즈텍인들은 쓰고 거품이 많은 음료를 만들었다고 나옴
  • 47-3: ②본문에 "They believed cacao was a gift from the gods"라고 명시됨

Passage 48

  • 48-1: ②글의 첫 문장에 "I have a big fear of public speaking"이라고 명시됨
  • 48-2: ②발표 전날 거울 앞에서 연습하고 대본 카드("cue cards")를 만들었다고 나옴
  • 48-3: ④두려움을 이겨내고 발표를 성공적으로 마친 후 안도감과 자랑스러움을("relieved and proud") 느낌

Passage 49

  • 49-1: ③글 전체가 새가 어떻게 날 수 있는지 그 원리를 여러 가지 측면에서 설명하고 있음
  • 49-2: ③가벼운 뼈, 강력한 근육, 깃털, 날개 모양은 언급되었지만, 음식을 적게 먹는다는 내용은 언급되지 않음
  • 49-3: ②본문에 "Many of their bones are hollow, which makes their skeletons much lighter"라고 명시됨

Passage 50

  • 50-1: ③미래의 자신에게 편지를 쓰는("write a letter to our future selves") 활동을 함
  • 50-2: ③수의사라는 꿈, 친구 소라, 수학에 대한 걱정 등은 썼지만, 좋아하는 음식에 대한 내용은 언급되지 않음
  • 50-3: ④타임캡슐을 3년 후 졸업식 날("on our graduation day in three years") 열어볼 것이라고 나옴
108
Vera's Flavor - Answer Key Part 1: 내용 파악 (51-60)

Passage 51

  • 51-1: ③손짓, 얼굴 표정, 몸짓을 사용해 의사소통하는 완전한 언어라고 설명함
  • 51-2: ⑤미국 수화와 영국 수화가 다른 것처럼, 나라마다 고유의 수화가 있다고("different countries have their own sign languages") 명시됨
  • 51-3: ③새로운 공동체와 소통, 다양한 삶의 방식 이해 등은 언급되었지만, 직업을 구하기 쉽다는 내용은 없음

Passage 52

  • 52-1: ③일기예보가 틀리고 갑자기 날씨가 나빠져 비바람이 몰아쳤다고 나옴
  • 52-2: ④모닥불을 피울 수 없어서 차가운 샌드위치를 먹었다고("had to eat cold sandwiches") 나옴
  • 52-3: ③글의 마지막에 "it taught us to always be prepared for unexpected situations"라고 교훈을 명시함

Passage 53

  • 53-1: ②북쪽 유목민들의 침략으로부터 중국을 보호하는 것이 주된 목적이었다고("to protect China from invasions") 나옴
  • 53-2: ①하나의 단일한 벽이 아니라 여러 벽과 요새의 연속이라고("not a single wall but a series of walls") 했으므로, 단일한 벽이라는 설명은 틀림
  • 53-3: ③글의 마지막에 "a symbol of China's long history and the perseverance of its people"이라고 명시됨

Passage 54

  • 54-1: ④최근 이론은 하품이 뇌를 식히는 데 도움을 준다는 것이라고 설명함
  • 54-2: ②'전염성이 있는'이라는 의미로, 한 사람에게서 다른 사람에게 쉽게 퍼지는 것을 뜻함
  • 54-3: ④피곤할 때 뇌 온도가 올라갈 수 있고, 이것이 하품을 더 많이 하는 이유일 수 있다고 설명함

Passage 55

  • 55-1: ②시장은 붐비고 시끄러웠지만 활기와 에너지가 넘쳤다고 묘사됨
  • 55-2: ③할머니가 신선한 생선을 고르는 법과 가격을 흥정하는 법을 가르쳐주었다고 나옴
  • 55-3: ④글의 마지막에 "a place full of human connection and culture"라고 결론 내리고 있음

Passage 56

  • 56-1: ③알(egg) → 애벌레(larva) → 번데기(pupa) → 나비(butterfly) 순서로 설명됨
  • 56-2: ③애벌레의 주된 임무는 먹고 자라는 것이라고("main job is to eat and grow") 명시됨
  • 56-3: ④번데기 단계는 "pupa stage"라고 하며, 번데기 자체는 "pupa" 또는 "chrysalis"라고 함

Passage 57

  • 57-1: ③강아지 사진과 전화번호를 넣은 '주인 찾는 개' 포스터를 만들어 동네에 붙였다고 나옴
  • 57-2: ③몇 블록 떨어진 곳에 사는 할아버지("an elderly man")가 주인으로 밝혀짐
  • 57-3: ②스파키와 헤어지는 것은 슬펐지만, 집을 찾는 것을 도와줄 수 있어서 기뻤다고("sad to say goodbye... but she was happy that she could help") 나옴

Passage 58

  • 58-1: ②글 전체가 종이 신문을 읽는 것의 여러 가지 이점에 대해 설명하고 있음
  • 58-2: ②전문 기자들이 쓰고 편집자들이 확인하기 때문에("written by professional journalists and checked by editors") 정보가 더 신뢰할 만하다고 함
  • 58-3: ④정보의 신뢰성, 어휘력 향상, 세상 이해 등은 언급되었지만, 소셜 미디어보다 빠르다는 내용은 없음

Passage 59

  • 59-1: ②많은 배와 비행기가 미스터리하게 사라진 것으로 유명하다고("famous for a mystery: many ships and airplanes have reportedly disappeared") 나옴
  • 59-2: ③과학자들은 허리케인과 같은 악천후("bad weather, including many hurricanes")를 논리적인 설명으로 제시함
  • 59-3: ②글의 마지막 부분에서 미스터리는 흥미롭지만, 현실은 초자연적이기보다는 악천후 등 현실적인 이유일 가능성이 높다고 서술함

Passage 60

  • 60-1: ③누군가를 처음 만났을 때 그 사람에 대해 형성되는 느낌이나 의견이라고("the feeling or opinion someone forms about you when they meet you for the first time") 정의됨
  • 60-2: ③미소, 눈 맞춤, 좋은 자세, 경청은 언급되었지만, 자기 자신에 대해 많이 이야기하라는 팁은 없음. 오히려 경청하라고 조언함
  • 60-3: ②바른 자세는 당신을 더 자신감 있어 보이게 만든다고("makes you look more self-assured") 함
109
Vera's Flavor - Answer Key Part 1: 내용 파악 (61-70)

Passage 61

  • 61-1: ③그리스 병사들로 가득 찬 거대한 목마라고 설명됨
  • 61-2: ②트로이인들을 속여 성 안으로 들어가기 위한 계략이었음
  • 61-3: ③목마 안에는 그리스 병사들이 숨어 있었다고("filled with Greek soldiers") 했으므로, 아무도 없었다는 것은 틀린 설명

Passage 62

  • 62-1: ③물의 중요성을 설명하고, 우리가 물을 아껴 써야 한다고 주장하고 있음
  • 62-2: ④짧은 샤워, 양치할 때 물 잠그기, 새는 수도꼭지 고치기는 언급되었지만, 병에 든 물을 마시라는 내용은 없음
  • 62-3: ④음식 소화, 영양분 운반, 체온 조절은 언급되었지만, 뼈를 튼튼하게 한다는 내용은 없음

Passage 63

  • 63-1: ④밴드에서 리드 싱어로서 노래를 불렀다고("I was the lead singer") 나옴
  • 63-2: ③무대에 오르기 전 심장이 두근거렸다고("my heart was pounding") 했으므로 긴장되고 흥분된 상태였음
  • 63-3: ④글의 마지막에 "The most important thing was sharing our passion with others"라고 명시됨

Passage 64

  • 64-1: ②문어의 가장 놀라운 능력 중 하나는 피부색과 질감을 바꾸는 위장술이라고나옴
  • 64-2: ②위험에 처하면 먹물 구름을 내뿜어 적을 혼란시킨다고 나옴
  • 64-3: ③문어는 심장이 세 개이고 피는 파란색이라고 했으므로, 심장이 한 개이고 피가 붉은색이라는 설명은 틀림

Passage 65

  • 65-1: ②멀리 사는 사람과 정기적으로 편지를 주고받는 친구라고 설명됨
  • 65-2: ④시드니에 사는 호주 소년 레오라고 나옴
  • 65-3: ④영어 실력 향상, 문화 이해, 새로운 친구를 얻는 것 등은 언급되었지만, 호주 여행 기회를 얻었다는 내용은 없음

Passage 66

  • 66-1: ③알렉산더 그레이엄 벨이 전화기를 발명한 것으로 알려져 있음
  • 66-2: ③최초의 통화 내용은 "Mr. Watson, come here. I want to see you."였음
  • 66-3: ②전화기 발명 전에는 편지나 전보를 사용했다고 나옴

Passage 67

  • 67-1: ③글의 첫 문장에 "I love the thrill of roller coasters"라고 명시됨
  • 67-2: ④대관람차 꼭대기에서 공원 전체를 볼 수 있었다고 나옴
  • 67-3: ③'스릴', '너무 재미있었다', '극도로 행복했다' 등의 표현을 통해 신나고 행복한 기분임을 알 수 있음

Passage 68

  • 68-1: ③대부분의 플라스틱이 자연 분해되지 않아 환경 오염을 일으키는 것이 큰 문제라고 설명함
  • 68-2: ③해양 동물들이 플라스틱을 먹이로 착각하거나 플라스틱에 몸이 얽힐 수 있다고("mistake plastic bags for food or get tangled") 나옴
  • 68-3: ④재사용 가능한 제품 사용, 재활용 등은 제안되었지만, 플라스틱 제품을 전혀 사용하지 말라는 극단적인 제안은 없음

Passage 69

  • 69-1: ③친구와 함께 케이크를 구우려고("decided to bake a cake") 했다고 나옴
  • 69-2: ④밀가루를 쏟고, 계란을 흘리고, 케이크를 태우는 등 여러 사건이 있었지만, 화재경보기가 울렸다는 내용은 언급되지 않음
  • 69-3: ③글의 마지막에 "follow the recipe carefully!"라는 교훈을 배웠다고 명시됨

Passage 70

  • 70-1: ①시각, 청각, 후각, 미각, 촉각이라는 다섯 가지 감각과 각각의 기능에 대해 설명하고 있음
  • 70-2: ③연기 냄새처럼 후각이 위험을 경고해 줄 수 있다고("smell, which can warn us of danger (like smoke)") 나옴
  • 70-3: ③피부가 우리에게 촉각을 느끼게 해준다고("Our skin gives us the sense of touch") 명시됨
110
Vera's Flavor - Answer Key Part 1: 내용 파악 (71-80)

Passage 71

  • 71-1: ③말을 사용하지 않고 몸짓과 얼굴 표정으로 이야기를 전달한다고 나옴
  • 71-2: ②얼굴 표정을 더 명확하게 보이게 하기 위해 흰색 분장을 한다고 함
  • 71-3: ④재미있을 수도 있고 감동적일 수도 있다고 했으므로, 항상 재미있기만 하다는 것은 틀린 설명

Passage 72

  • 72-1: ②대기 중의 가스들이 열을 가두어 지구를 따뜻하게 유지하는 자연적인 과정이라고 설명됨
  • 72-2: ③인간이 화석 연료를 태워 너무 많은 이산화탄소를 배출함으로써 온실 효과를 강화시킨다고 나옴
  • 72-3: ②대기 중 가스들이 온실의 유리벽처럼 열을 가두는 역할을 하기 때문임

Passage 73

  • 73-1: ③운동회의 주제가 '협동과 재미'였다고 명시됨
  • 73-2: ③인간 매듭, 물 전달하기 게임은 묘사되었지만, 2인 3각 경주는 언급되지 않음
  • 73-3: ③글의 마지막에 "a great way to build teamwork and make new friends from different grades"라고 명시됨

Passage 74

  • 74-1: ③부자에게서 도둑질하여 가난한 사람들에게 나눠주는 것으로 유명했다고 나옴
  • 74-2: ③로빈 후드를 따르는 무리를 '메리 멘'이라고 불렀다고 나옴
  • 74-3: ②로빈 후드는 전문적인 궁수, 즉 활을 매우 잘 쏘는 사람이었다고 나옴

Passage 75

  • 75-1: ②빵을 만드는 네 가지 간단한 재료로 밀가루, 물, 이스트, 소금을 언급함
  • 75-2: ③반죽을 주무르는 것은 글루텐을 발달시켜 빵의 구조를 만들고 반죽을 부드럽게 하기 위함이라고 설명됨
  • 75-3: ②간단한 재료들이 인내와 여러 과정을 거쳐 놀라운 결과물인 빵으로 변신하는 전체 과정이 마법 같다는 의미임

Passage 76

  • 76-1: ③지구 궤도를 도는 우주비행사들의 집이자 과학 실험실이라고 설명됨
  • 76-2: ③미세 중력 때문에 우주비행사들이 걷는 대신 떠다닌다고 나옴
  • 76-3: ①미국, 러시아, 일본, 유럽, 캐나다 5개 우주 기관의 협력 프로젝트라고 했으므로, 한 국가가 만들었다는 것은 틀린 설명

Passage 77

  • 77-1: ③물이 반쯤 찬 컵을 보고도 긍정적인 면에 집중하는 낙관주의자의 태도를 의미함
  • 77-2: ②연구에 따르면 낙관주의자들은 종종 더 건강하고 스트레스를 덜 받는다고 나옴
  • 77-3: ①글 전체가 긍정적인 사고방식이 건강, 스트레스, 목표 달성 등 삶의 여러 측면에 좋은 영향을 줄 수 있다고 설명하고 있음

Passage 78

  • 78-1: ②한 식물에서 다른 식물로 꽃가루를 옮기는 동물이라고 정의됨
  • 78-2: ③우리가 먹는 음식의 약 3분의 1이 벌의 수분 활동에 의존한다고 나옴
  • 78-3: ④서식지 감소, 살충제, 기후 변화는 원인으로 언급되었지만, 꿀을 너무 많이 생산하는 것은 원인으로 언급되지 않음

Passage 79

  • 79-1: ②장난감을 보기 위해 엄마 손을 잠시 놓았다가 길을 잃었다고 나옴
  • 79-2: ③친절한 가게 직원이 아이를 발견하고 고객 서비스 센터로 데려가 안내 방송을 해주었음
  • 79-3: ④글의 마지막에 "how important it is to stay close to my parents in a crowded place"라는 교훈을 배웠다고 명시됨

Passage 80

  • 80-1: ②글 전체가 좋은 대화가가 되기 위해 잘 말하는 것만큼이나 잘 듣는 것이 중요하며, 그 방법들을 설명하고 있음
  • 80-2: ④상대방이 말하는 동안 끼어들거나 대답을 생각하지 말라고 조언함
  • 80-3: ②단순히 기다리는 것이 아니라, 상대방에게 온전히 주의를 기울이는 능동적인 과정이라고 설명함
111
Vera's Flavor - Answer Key Part 1: 내용 파악 (81-90)

Passage 81

  • 81-1: ②음악의 힘과 이점에 대해 설명하고 있음
  • 81-2: ②음악이 뇌의 화학 작용을 바꾸고 엔도르핀을 분비시킨다고 명시됨
  • 81-3: ③협응력 향상, 규율과 인내심 등은 언급되었지만, 키가 크는 것과는 관련이 없음

Passage 82

  • 82-1: ①18세기 이탈리아 나폴리에서 시작되었다고 명시됨
  • 82-2: ②이탈리아 국기의 색깔을 나타내는 재료를 사용했다고 설명됨
  • 82-3: ②2차 대전 후 미국 군인들이 피자에 대한 사랑을 가져왔다고 나옴

Passage 83

  • 83-1: ③글쓴이가 가장 좋아하는 부분은 꽃 섹션이라고 명시됨
  • 83-2: ②씨앗 심는 법과 채소가 언제 익었는지 알아보는 법을 가르쳐주었음
  • 83-3: ③시간과 노력이 필요하지만 아름다움과 음식이라는 보상을 준다는 의미

Passage 84

  • 84-1: ②꿈은 잠들 때 마음속에서 일어나는 이미지, 감정, 생각의 연속이라고 정의됨
  • 84-2: ③대부분의 꿈은 REM 수면 중에 일어난다고 명시됨
  • 84-3: ②과학자들은 여전히 꿈을 완전히 이해하지 못한다고 했으므로, 모든 것을 이해한다는 것은 틀린 설명

Passage 85

  • 85-1: ③헬퍼는 여러 가지 일을 할 수 있었지만, 요리는 언급되지 않음
  • 85-2: ③"Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system"이라고 답했음
  • 85-3: ③병원, 가정, 사무실 등 다양한 곳에서 사용된다고 설명됨

Passage 86

  • 86-1: ②환경 보호와 자원 절약이 주된 이점으로 언급됨
  • 86-2: ①종이 1톤을 재활용하면 나무 17그루와 물 7,000갤런을 절약할 수 있다고 명시됨
  • 86-3: ③사람들이 참여해서 쓰레기를 분리하고 올바른 곳에 버려야 효과적이라고 함

Passage 87

  • 87-1: ②할아버지의 다락방에서 발견했다고 명시됨
  • 87-2: ②일상생활, 미래에 대한 희망, 할머니에 대한 사랑을 썼다고 나옴
  • 87-3: ③손으로 쓴 글이 얼마나 소중하고 중요한 기억을 보존할 수 있는지 깨달았음

Passage 88

  • 88-1: ③최대 3,000마일을 여행한다고 명시됨
  • 88-2: ③여행을 시작한 나비와 끝내는 나비가 다르며, 여러 세대에 걸쳐 완성된다는 점이 특이함
  • 88-3: ④태양, 자기장, 유전적 기억은 언급되었지만, GPS 기술은 언급되지 않음

Passage 89

  • 89-1: ②전략, 인내심, 깊은 사고가 필요하기 때문이라고 설명됨
  • 89-2: ③나이트가 L자 모양으로 움직인다고 명시됨
  • 89-3: ⑤집중력, 문제해결능력 등은 언급되었지만, 상대방과 빨리 친해지는 방법은 언급되지 않음

Passage 90

  • 90-1: ③햇빛이 물방울을 통과하면서 여러 색깔로 나뉘어지는 것이 원인
  • 90-2: ③태양이 뒤에 있고 앞에 물방울이 있어야 한다고 명시됨
  • 90-3: ②바깥쪽이 빨간색, 안쪽이 보라색이라고 명시됨
112
Vera's Flavor - Answer Key Part 1: 내용 파악 (91-100)

Passage 91

  • 91-1: ②종이접기를 의미한다고 정의됨
  • 91-2: ②전통적인 종이접기에서는 정사각형 종이 한 장만 허용됨
  • 91-3: ②기하학과 분수 이해에 도움이 되기 때문이라고 설명됨

Passage 92

  • 92-1: ②방문객들이 실험할 수 있는 인터랙티브 전시물이 있어서 특별했음
  • 92-2: ②진자는 항상 같은 높이로 되돌아간다고 배웠음
  • 92-3: ③천체 투영관이 가장 좋아하는 곳이었다고 명시됨

Passage 93

  • 93-1: ②무거운 장바구니를 들고 다니는 데 어려움을 겪고 있었음
  • 93-2: ③가방을 들어주고 버스에 타는 것을 도와주었음
  • 93-3: ②친절한 행동을 보면 다른 사람들도 친절해지고 싶어진다는 의미

Passage 94

  • 94-1: ②기원전 776년 그리스 올림피아에서 첫 고대 올림픽이 열렸음
  • 94-2: ②올림픽 기간 중 모든 전쟁이 중단되는 기간이었음
  • 94-3: ⑤고대 올림픽은 그리스에서만 열렸고, 전 세계에서 참가한 것은 현대 올림픽부터임

Passage 95

  • 95-1: ②어머니가 면접 질문을 연습하는 것을 도와주었음
  • 95-2: ②책에 대한 경험과 근무 가능한 시간에 대해 물어보았음
  • 95-3: ③책을 좋아하고 서점에서 일하는 것이 완벽할 것 같다고 답했음

Passage 96

  • 96-1: ②잠들 때 뇌는 매우 바쁘게 기억을 처리하고 몸을 회복시킨다고 설명됨
  • 96-2: ③집중력, 의사결정, 감정 조절에 문제가 생긴다고 명시됨
  • 96-3: ③매일 같은 시간에 잠자리에 들고 잠자기 전 스크린을 피하는 것이 좋은 수면 습관

Passage 97

  • 97-1: ③제빵용 초콜릿이 떨어져서 우연히 만들어졌음
  • 97-2: ②초콜릿 조각들이 녹아서 초콜릿 쿠키가 될 것이라고 예상했음
  • 97-3: ②평생 초콜릿을 공급받는 조건으로 레시피를 팔았음

Passage 98

  • 98-1: ②고대에는 부유한 사람과 학자들만 이용했지만, 지금은 모든 사람이 이용함
  • 98-2: ②DVD, 컴퓨터, 워크숍, 메이커스페이스, 지역 행사 등 다양한 서비스 제공
  • 98-3: ④최신 영화 무료 상영은 언급되지 않음

Passage 99

  • 99-1: ②실수를 두려워하지 말라고 조언했음
  • 99-2: ②스페인어 영화를 보고 스페인어 음악을 들으며 연습했음

PART 2

대의 파악

Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 1]

The Benefits of Urban Green Spaces

Urban green spaces, such as parks, gardens, and tree-lined streets, play a crucial role in improving the quality of life in cities. Research has shown that these green areas provide numerous environmental benefits. They help reduce air pollution by absorbing harmful gases and releasing oxygen. Green spaces also help control the temperature in cities by providing shade and cooling the air through a process called evapotranspiration. Additionally, they manage stormwater by allowing rainwater to soak into the ground instead of flowing into storm drains. Beyond environmental benefits, green spaces significantly impact human health and well-being. Studies indicate that people who live near parks and gardens experience lower levels of stress and anxiety. The presence of nature in urban areas encourages physical activity, such as walking, jogging, and cycling. Furthermore, green spaces provide opportunities for social interaction and community building, which strengthens neighborhood bonds.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • environmental:환경의
  • absorb:흡수하다
  • cooling:냉각, 식히기
  • soak:스며들다, 흡수되다

Questions

1.다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① The history of urban planning and development
  • ② The challenges of maintaining parks in cities
  • ③ The various benefits of green spaces in urban areas
  • ④ The cost of creating gardens in city centers
  • ⑤ The difference between rural and urban environments
115
Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 2]

Sleep and Academic Performance

The relationship between sleep and academic performance has been extensively studied by researchers worldwide. Adequate sleep is essential for cognitive functions such as memory consolidation, attention, and problem-solving abilities. During sleep, the brain processes and organizes information learned during the day, making it easier to retrieve this information later. Students who get sufficient sleep typically show better concentration in class and improved performance on tests and assignments. Conversely, sleep deprivation can lead to decreased attention span, difficulty focusing, and poor decision-making skills. Research has found that teenagers need approximately 8-10 hours of sleep per night for optimal functioning. However, many students fail to meet this requirement due to heavy academic workloads, extracurricular activities, and electronic device usage before bedtime. Schools and parents must work together to promote healthy sleep habits among students to enhance their academic success.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • researcher:연구자
  • cognitive:인지의, 인식의
  • retrieve:회수하다, 되찾다
  • deprivation:부족, 결핍

Questions

2.다음 글의 요지로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ② 청소년들의 전자기기 사용 문제
  • ③ 학교와 부모의 협력 방안
  • ④ 기억력 향상을 위한 학습 방법
  • ⑤ 숙제와 과외활동의 균형 맞추기

Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 3]

The Science Behind Recycling Plastic

Plastic recycling is a complex process that involves several scientific principles and technologies. The first step is sorting different types of plastic, as each type has unique chemical properties that require specific recycling methods. The most commonly recycled plastics include PET (used in water bottles) and HDPE (used in milk jugs). After sorting, the plastics are cleaned to remove labels, adhesives, and contaminants. The clean plastic is then shredded into small flakes and melted down to form pellets. These pellets can be used to manufacture new products, reducing the need for virgin plastic production. However, plastic recycling faces significant challenges. Each time plastic is recycled, its quality degrades slightly, limiting the number of times it can be reprocessed. Additionally, some plastic products contain additives that make recycling difficult or impossible. Despite these limitations, recycling remains crucial for reducing plastic waste and protecting the environment.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • process:과정, 공정
  • sorting:분류
  • contaminant:오염물질
  • shred:잘게 자르다, 파쇄하다
  • degrade:저하되다, 악화되다

Questions

3.다음 글의 제목으로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① "Why Plastic Recycling Often Fails"
  • ② "The Process and Challenges of Plastic Recycling"
  • ③ "How to Sort Plastic Waste at Home"
  • ④ "The Environmental Impact of Virgin Plastic"
  • ⑤ "New Technologies in Plastic Manufacturing"
117
Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 4]

The Impact of Social Media on Teenage Communication

Social media platforms have fundamentally changed the way teenagers communicate and interact with each other. Traditional face-to-face conversations have been increasingly replaced by digital interactions through messaging apps, social networks, and video calls. Research indicates that while social media allows teenagers to maintain connections with friends and family across distances, it also presents certain challenges. Studies show that heavy social media use can lead to reduced empathy and difficulty in reading nonverbal cues, such as facial expressions and body language. Additionally, the constant availability of digital communication can create pressure to respond immediately to messages, leading to anxiety and stress. However, social media also provides platforms for creative expression and enables teenagers to find communities with shared interests. Mental health experts suggest that balanced usage, combined with regular in-person interactions, is key to maintaining healthy communication skills during the teenage years.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • communicate:의사소통하다
  • interaction:상호작용
  • empathy:공감 능력
  • nonverbal:비언어적인

  • ③ The history of social networking platforms
  • ④ The importance of face-to-face conversations
  • ⑤ The role of parents in monitoring social media use

Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 5]

Photosynthesis and Climate Change

Photosynthesis, the process by which plants convert sunlight into energy, plays a critical role in regulating Earth's climate. During photosynthesis, plants absorb carbon dioxide from the atmosphere and release oxygen, helping to maintain the balance of gases in our atmosphere. This natural process acts as a carbon sink, removing significant amounts of carbon dioxide that would otherwise contribute to global warming. Forests, especially tropical rainforests, are particularly effective at sequestering carbon due to their large biomass and rapid growth rates. However, climate change is affecting photosynthesis in complex ways. Rising temperatures can increase the rate of photosynthesis in some plants, but extreme heat can also inhibit the process. Additionally, changes in precipitation patterns and increased frequency of droughts can stress plants and reduce their ability to photosynthesize effectively. Scientists are studying these interactions to better understand how climate change and photosynthesis influence each other in what is known as a feedback loop.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • regulate:조절하다
  • carbon sink:탄소 흡수원
  • sequester:격리하다, 흡수하다
  • inhibit:억제하다, 방해하다

Questions

5.다음 글의 요지로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① 광합성과 기후 변화 사이의 복잡한 상호작용
  • ② 열대 우림 보호의 필요성
  • ③ 극한 기후가 식물에 미치는 피해
  • ④ 탄소 흡수원의 종류와 특징
  • ⑤ 지구 온난화의 주요 원인들

[지문 6]

The Psychology of Color in Learning Environments

The colors present in learning environments can significantly affect students' concentration, mood, and academic performance. Educational psychologists have conducted extensive research on how different colors influence cognitive processes and emotional states. Warm colors, such as red and orange, tend to increase alertness and energy levels but can also cause agitation if used excessively. Cool colors, like blue and green, promote calmness and focus, making them ideal for studying and concentration tasks. Yellow is associated with creativity and optimism, which can enhance problem-solving abilities. However, too much yellow can be overwhelming and distracting. Neutral colors, including white and beige, provide a clean and organized appearance but may lack the stimulating effects needed for active learning. Researchers recommend using a balanced combination of colors in classrooms, with cool colors dominating study areas and warm accents used sparingly to create visual interest without causing distraction.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • concentration:집중력
  • educational psychologist:교육 심리학자
  • agitation:동요, 불안
  • creativity:창의성

[지문 7]

Antibiotic Resistance: A Growing Concern

Antibiotic resistance occurs when bacteria evolve and develop the ability to survive exposure to antibiotics that were previously effective against them. This phenomenon has become one of the most pressing public health challenges of the 21st century. The overuse and misuse of antibiotics in both human medicine and agriculture have accelerated the development of resistant bacterial strains. When antibiotics are used unnecessarily or not taken as prescribed, some bacteria survive and multiply, passing their resistance traits to future generations. Common practices that contribute to resistance include taking antibiotics for viral infections, stopping treatment early, and using antibiotics in livestock feed for growth promotion. The consequences of antibiotic resistance are severe, including longer hospital stays, higher medical costs, and increased mortality rates. Healthcare professionals emphasize the importance of using antibiotics responsibly and developing new treatment strategies to combat resistant bacteria.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • resistance:저항성, 내성
  • accelerate:가속화하다
  • multiply:번식하다, 증식하다
  • mortality:사망률

Questions

  • ② The causes and consequences of antibiotic resistance
  • ③ The difference between bacteria and viruses
  • ④ The cost of developing new medicines
  • ⑤ The role of hospitals in preventing infections
121
Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 8]

The Economics of Renewable Energy

The transition to renewable energy sources has become increasingly important from both environmental and economic perspectives. Solar, wind, and hydroelectric power are becoming more cost-effective as technology improves and production scales up. The initial investment costs for renewable energy infrastructure are often high, but the long-term operational costs are significantly lower than those of fossil fuel-based power plants. Renewable energy sources do not require fuel purchases, and their maintenance costs are relatively minimal. Additionally, the renewable energy sector has created millions of jobs worldwide, from manufacturing solar panels to installing wind turbines. Governments are supporting this transition through subsidies, tax incentives, and favorable policies. However, challenges remain, including the intermittent nature of some renewable sources and the need for improved energy storage technologies. Despite these challenges, economic analyses consistently show that renewable energy investments provide substantial returns over time while reducing environmental impact.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • transition:전환
  • cost-effective:비용 효율적인
  • investment:투자
  • subsidy:보조금
  • intermittent:간헐적인

  • ③ 정부 정책의 중요성

[지문 9]

The Science of Memory Formation

Memory formation is a complex neurological process that involves multiple regions of the brain working together. When we experience something new, neurons create connections called synapses that store information. The strength of these connections determines how well we remember the experience. The process begins with encoding, where sensory information is converted into a form that the brain can store. This information then moves to short-term memory, which can hold a limited amount of data for a brief period. Through a process called consolidation, important information is transferred to long-term memory, where it can be stored indefinitely. Factors such as attention, emotion, and repetition significantly influence memory formation. Strong emotions, in particular, can enhance memory consolidation, which is why we often remember emotionally significant events more clearly. Understanding these processes has important implications for education, as teachers can use memory research to develop more effective learning strategies.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • neurological:신경학의
  • neuron:뉴런, 신경세포
  • synapse:시냅스, 신경연접
  • encoding:부호화, 암호화
  • consolidation:강화, 통합

Questions

  • ② "The Role of Teachers in Student Success"
  • ③ "Understanding How Our Brain Forms Memories"
  • ④ "The Difference Between Short and Long-term Memory"
  • ⑤ "Why Some People Have Better Memory Than Others"

[지문 10]

Urban Heat Islands and Public Health

Urban heat islands are areas within cities that experience significantly higher temperatures than surrounding rural areas. This phenomenon occurs due to the absorption and retention of heat by buildings, roads, and other infrastructure made of concrete and asphalt. The lack of vegetation in urban areas also contributes to higher temperatures, as plants naturally cool the air through evapotranspiration. Urban heat islands pose serious public health risks, particularly during heat waves. Vulnerable populations, including elderly people, children, and individuals with chronic health conditions, are at increased risk of heat-related illnesses such as heat exhaustion and heat stroke. Studies have shown that urban heat islands can increase mortality rates during extreme heat events. Additionally, higher temperatures increase energy consumption as people rely more heavily on air conditioning, leading to higher electricity bills and increased strain on power grids. Urban planners are implementing strategies to mitigate heat islands, including increasing green spaces, using reflective building materials, and creating cool roofs.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • absorption:흡수
  • infrastructure:사회기반시설
  • evapotranspiration:증발산
  • vulnerable:취약한

Questions

  • ② The causes and health impacts of urban heat islands
  • ③ The importance of air conditioning in cities
  • ④ How to design energy-efficient buildings
  • ⑤ The economic costs of urban planning
124
Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 11]

The Role of Gut Bacteria in Human Health

The human digestive system contains trillions of bacteria, collectively known as the gut microbiome, which plays a crucial role in maintaining overall health. These beneficial bacteria help break down food, produce essential vitamins, and support the immune system. Recent research has revealed that the composition of gut bacteria can influence various aspects of health, including metabolism, mood, and even brain function. A balanced microbiome helps prevent the growth of harmful bacteria and pathogens that can cause illness. Factors such as diet, antibiotics, stress, and lifestyle choices can significantly alter the bacterial balance in the gut. Diets rich in fiber, fruits, and vegetables promote the growth of beneficial bacteria, while processed foods and excessive sugar can harm the microbiome. Scientists are increasingly recognizing the gut-brain connection, where gut bacteria can affect neurotransmitter production and influence mental health. This growing field of research suggests that maintaining a healthy gut microbiome may be key to preventing various diseases and promoting overall wellness.

  • digestive system:소화계
  • microbiome:미생물군
  • metabolism:신진대사
  • pathogen:병원균
  • neurotransmitter:신경전달물질

[지문 12]

The Economics of Fast Fashion

Fast fashion refers to the rapid production of inexpensive clothing that mimics current fashion trends. This business model has revolutionized the clothing industry by making trendy clothes accessible to consumers at low prices. Companies can design, produce, and distribute new styles within weeks, allowing them to respond quickly to changing fashion demands. However, this approach has significant economic and environmental consequences. The emphasis on low costs often leads to poor working conditions in manufacturing facilities and extremely low wages for workers. Additionally, the fast fashion model encourages overconsumption, as consumers frequently purchase new items and discard old ones quickly. This creates enormous amounts of textile waste, with millions of tons of clothing ending up in landfills each year. The environmental cost includes water pollution from dyeing processes, excessive use of natural resources, and carbon emissions from transportation. Critics argue that the true cost of fast fashion is hidden in these externalities, and consumers are beginning to demand more sustainable alternatives.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • revolutionize:혁신하다
  • consequence:결과
  • overconsumption:과소비
  • landfill:매립지
  • sustainable:지속가능한

Questions

  • ② The economic and environmental impacts of fast fashion
  • ③ How to start a successful clothing business
  • ④ The working conditions in textile factories
  • ⑤ Consumer preferences in clothing purchases

Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 13]

The Science of Addiction

Addiction is a complex neurological condition that affects the brain's reward system and decision-making processes. When a person uses addictive substances or engages in certain behaviors, the brain releases dopamine, a neurotransmitter associated with pleasure and reward. Over time, repeated exposure causes the brain to adapt by reducing natural dopamine production and requiring larger amounts of the substance or behavior to achieve the same effect. This process, known as tolerance, is accompanied by physical and psychological dependence. Brain imaging studies show that addiction affects areas responsible for judgment, learning, memory, and behavior control. Genetic factors account for approximately 50% of an individual's risk for developing addiction, while environmental factors such as stress, trauma, and social influences contribute to the remaining risk. Understanding addiction as a medical condition rather than a moral failing has led to more effective treatments, including therapy, medication, and support programs that address both the biological and psychological aspects of the disorder.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • neurological:신경학의
  • dopamine:도파민
  • tolerance:내성
  • dependence:의존성
  • therapy:치료법

Questions

13.다음 글의 제목으로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ② "How Genetics Determine Human Behavior"
  • ③ "Understanding Addiction as a Brain Disease"
  • ④ "The Social Costs of Drug Use"
  • ⑤ "Effective Prevention Programs for Youth"
127
Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 14]

Precision Agriculture and Food Security

Precision agriculture uses advanced technology to optimize farming practices and increase crop yields while minimizing environmental impact. This approach involves collecting detailed data about soil conditions, weather patterns, and crop health using sensors, drones, and satellite imagery. Farmers can then apply fertilizers, pesticides, and water precisely where and when needed, reducing waste and costs. GPS-guided tractors can plant seeds with exact spacing and depth, ensuring optimal growing conditions. The data collected allows farmers to identify problems early, such as pest infestations or nutrient deficiencies, enabling quick interventions that prevent crop losses. As the global population continues to grow, precision agriculture becomes increasingly important for food security. Traditional farming methods may not be sufficient to feed an estimated 9 billion people by 2050. By maximizing efficiency and productivity while reducing environmental damage, precision agriculture offers a sustainable solution to meet future food demands. However, the high initial costs of technology and the need for technical training present challenges for widespread adoption, particularly among small-scale farmers.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • technology:기술
  • yield:수확량
  • sensor:센서
  • GPS:위성위치확인시스템
  • intervention:개입, 조치

Questions

14.다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ② The environmental problems caused by agriculture
  • ③ How precision agriculture addresses food security challenges
  • ④ The cost of implementing new farming technology
  • ⑤ Traditional farming methods and their effectiveness
128
Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 15]

The Psychology of Decision Making

Human decision-making is influenced by numerous cognitive biases that can lead to irrational choices. These mental shortcuts, while often helpful in daily life, can sometimes result in poor decisions. The availability heuristic causes people to overestimate the likelihood of events that are easily remembered, often because they were recent or dramatic. For example, people may fear airplane crashes more than car accidents, despite statistics showing that flying is much safer. Confirmation bias leads individuals to seek information that supports their existing beliefs while ignoring contradictory evidence. The anchoring effect occurs when people rely too heavily on the first piece of information encountered when making decisions. Additionally, loss aversion means that people typically feel the pain of losing something more intensely than the pleasure of gaining the same thing. Understanding these biases is crucial for improving decision-making in various contexts, from personal financial choices to medical diagnoses. Researchers suggest that awareness of these tendencies, combined with structured decision-making processes, can help people make more rational choices.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • cognitive:인지의
  • bias:편견, 편향
  • availability heuristic:가용성 휴리스틱
  • confirmation bias:확증 편향
  • anchoring effect:닻 내림 효과
  • loss aversion:손실 회피
  • rational:합리적인

Questions

15.다음 글의 요지로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ② 비행기 사고와 자동차 사고의 통계적 비교
  • ③ 개인 재정 관리의 중요성
  • ④ 의료진단에서 발생하는 오류들
  • ⑤ 구조화된 의사결정의 장점
129
Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 16]

Ocean Acidification and Marine Ecosystems

Ocean acidification, often called the "other CO2 problem," occurs when the ocean absorbs carbon dioxide from the atmosphere, causing seawater to become more acidic. Since the Industrial Revolution, the ocean has absorbed approximately 30% of human-produced CO2, leading to a significant decrease in ocean pH levels. This chemical change poses serious threats to marine ecosystems, particularly organisms that build shells and skeletons from calcium carbonate, such as corals, mollusks, and certain plankton species. As ocean acidity increases, these organisms struggle to maintain their calcium carbonate structures, which can dissolve in acidic conditions. Coral reefs, which support about 25% of all marine species, are especially vulnerable to acidification. The weakening of these ecosystems has cascading effects throughout the food chain, potentially affecting fish populations that billions of people depend on for food and income. Scientists are studying various approaches to address ocean acidification, including reducing CO2 emissions and developing alkalinization techniques to counteract acidity. However, the most effective solution remains significant reductions in global carbon dioxide emissions.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • acidic:산성의
  • ecosystem:생태계
  • dissolve:용해되다
  • vulnerable:취약한
  • cascading:연쇄적인
  • alkalinization:알칼리화

Questions

16.다음 글의 제목으로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① "The Industrial Revolution and Its Environmental Legacy"
  • ② "Ocean Acidification: Causes and Impacts on Marine Life"
  • ③ "The Importance of Coral Reefs in Ocean Ecosystems"
  • ④ "Carbon Dioxide and Global Climate Change"
  • ⑤ "Fishing Industries and Food Security Challenges"
130
Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 17]

The Digital Divide and Educational Inequality

The digital divide refers to the gap between individuals who have access to modern information technology and those who do not. This disparity has become increasingly important in education, as schools and universities rely more heavily on digital tools and online resources. Students without reliable internet access or adequate devices face significant disadvantages in their educational pursuits. The COVID-19 pandemic highlighted these inequalities when schools shifted to remote learning, leaving many students unable to participate effectively in online classes. Rural areas often lack the infrastructure necessary for high-speed internet, while low-income families may not be able to afford computers or internet services. This digital divide perpetuates educational inequality, as students with better technology access can take advantage of online courses, research resources, and educational software. The consequences extend beyond immediate academic performance, affecting long-term career prospects and economic opportunities. Governments and educational institutions are implementing various strategies to bridge this gap, including providing devices to students, improving internet infrastructure, and creating public Wi-Fi access points. However, addressing the digital divide requires sustained effort and significant investment in both technology and digital literacy programs.

Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 18]

Biomimicry in Engineering and Design

Biomimicry is an innovative approach to design that seeks solutions to human challenges by studying and imitating natural processes and structures. This field has led to numerous breakthrough innovations across various industries. For example, the study of bird flight patterns inspired the design of more efficient airplane wings, while the structure of shark skin led to the development of swimsuits that reduce drag in water. Velcro was invented after observing how burr seeds stick to animal fur using tiny hooks. In architecture, termite mounds have inspired building designs that maintain comfortable temperatures without air conditioning through natural ventilation systems. The lotus leaf's self-cleaning properties have influenced the creation of stain-resistant fabrics and paints. Recent advances include studying gecko feet to develop stronger adhesives and examining spider silk properties to create ultra-strong materials. Biomimicry not only leads to innovative products but often results in more sustainable and efficient solutions than traditional engineering approaches. As technology advances, researchers continue to discover new applications by observing the ingenious solutions that have evolved in nature over millions of years.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • design:설계
  • imitate:모방하다
  • innovation:혁신
  • architecture:건축학
  • adhesive:접착제
  • sustainable:지속가능한

Questions

18.다음 글의 요지로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ② 전통적인 공학 방법의 한계점
  • ③ 새로운 재료 개발의 중요성
  • ④ 건축 기술의 역사적 발전 과정
  • ⑤ 환경 친화적 제품의 경제적 가치

Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 19]

The Impact of Music on Brain Development

Scientific research has demonstrated that music has profound effects on brain development, particularly in children and adolescents. Learning to play musical instruments creates new neural pathways and strengthens connections between different brain regions. Studies using brain imaging technology show that musicians have larger areas of the brain responsible for motor control, auditory processing, and spatial reasoning. Children who receive music education often demonstrate improved mathematical abilities, as music and math share similar patterns and structures. The discipline required to practice an instrument also enhances focus, patience, and perseverance. Additionally, group music activities, such as playing in orchestras or bands, develop social skills and teamwork abilities. Research indicates that the benefits of music education extend beyond childhood, with adults who learned music showing better cognitive flexibility and slower age-related cognitive decline. Music therapy is now used to help individuals with various conditions, including autism, depression, and dementia. The therapeutic effects of music suggest that rhythm and melody can access parts of the brain that other treatments cannot reach, making music a powerful tool for healing and development.

  • neural:신경의
  • pathway:경로
  • motor control:운동 조절
  • mathematical:수학의
  • discipline:훈련, 규율
  • perseverance:인내
  • cognitive:인지의
  • therapeutic:치료의

  • ④ "Music Therapy for Mental Health Conditions"
  • ⑤ "The Social Benefits of Playing in Groups"

Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 20]

Renewable Energy Storage Solutions

One of the primary challenges facing renewable energy adoption is the intermittent nature of sources like solar and wind power. Unlike traditional power plants that can generate electricity on demand, renewable sources depend on weather conditions and time of day. This variability creates a need for effective energy storage solutions that can capture excess energy when production is high and release it when demand exceeds generation. Battery technology has advanced significantly, with lithium-ion batteries becoming more efficient and affordable. However, large-scale storage requires diverse approaches beyond conventional batteries. Pumped hydro storage uses excess electricity to pump water uphill, which can later be released to generate power through turbines. Compressed air energy storage systems use surplus energy to compress air in underground caverns, which is then released to drive generators. Other innovative approaches include thermal storage, where heat is stored in molten salt, and flywheel systems that store energy in rotating masses. The development of efficient, cost-effective storage technologies is crucial for achieving a reliable renewable energy grid and reducing dependence on fossil fuels.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • adoption:채택
  • intermittent:간헐적인
  • variability:변동성
  • battery:배터리
  • pumped hydro storage:양수 발전
  • turbine:터빈
  • cavern:동굴
  • thermal:열의
  • flywheel:플라이휠

Questions

20.다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ② Various energy storage solutions for renewable power
  • ③ The cost comparison between renewable and fossil fuels
  • ④ How weather affects solar and wind power generation
  • ⑤ The future of lithium-ion battery technology
134
Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 21]

The Science of Happiness

Positive psychology research has identified several key factors that contribute to human happiness and well-being. While many people believe that external circumstances such as wealth or fame determine happiness, studies show that these factors account for only about 10% of our overall life satisfaction. Genetics play a larger role, contributing approximately 50% to our happiness baseline, which explains why some people naturally tend to be more optimistic or resilient. However, the remaining 40% is within our control through intentional activities and choices. Research indicates that strong social relationships are the most significant predictor of happiness, with people who maintain close friendships and family connections reporting higher levels of life satisfaction. Gratitude practices, such as keeping a gratitude journal, have been shown to increase positive emotions and overall well-being. Regular physical exercise, adequate sleep, and mindfulness meditation also contribute significantly to happiness levels. Acts of kindness and helping others create what researchers call a "helper's high," releasing endorphins and increasing feelings of purpose and connection. Understanding the science of happiness empowers individuals to make choices that promote long-term well-being rather than pursuing temporary pleasures.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • genetics:유전학
  • baseline:기준선
  • predictor:예측 인자
  • gratitude:감사
  • mindfulness:마음챙김
  • meditation:명상

Questions

  • ② 유전자가 성격에 미치는 절대적 영향
  • ③ 부와 명성이 행복에 미치는 중요성
  • ④ 사회적 관계의 경제적 가치
  • ⑤ 명상과 운동의 신체적 효과

Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 22]

Microplastics in the Food Chain

Microplastics, defined as plastic particles smaller than 5 millimeters, have become a pervasive environmental pollutant that is increasingly found throughout the global food chain. These tiny particles originate from various sources, including the breakdown of larger plastic items, synthetic clothing fibers released during washing, and microbeads from personal care products. Marine organisms, from microscopic plankton to large fish, ingest these particles either directly or through consuming contaminated prey. Studies have detected microplastics in seafood consumed by humans, including fish, shellfish, and salt. The particles have also been found in drinking water, honey, beer, and other food products. While the long-term health effects of microplastic consumption are still being studied, researchers are concerned about potential impacts on human health. Microplastics can carry toxic chemicals and may cause inflammation in body tissues. The accumulation of these particles in organs could potentially disrupt normal biological functions. Addressing this issue requires reducing plastic waste, improving waste management systems, and developing biodegradable alternatives to conventional plastics. The widespread presence of microplastics highlights the urgent need for comprehensive approaches to plastic pollution.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • pervasive:널리 퍼진
  • pollutant:오염물질
  • plankton:플랑크톤
  • ingest:섭취하다
  • toxic:독성의
  • inflammation:염증
  • accumulation:축적

Questions

  • ③ "How to Reduce Plastic Waste in Daily Life"
  • ④ "The Manufacturing Process of Synthetic Materials"
  • ⑤ "Marine Conservation and Fishing Industry Challenges"

Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 23]

The Role of Sleep in Athletic Performance

Sleep plays a critical role in athletic performance, recovery, and injury prevention, yet it is often overlooked by athletes and coaches. During sleep, the body releases growth hormone, which is essential for muscle repair and adaptation following training. REM sleep, in particular, is important for consolidating motor skills and improving coordination. Studies have shown that athletes who get adequate sleep demonstrate faster reaction times, improved accuracy, and better decision-making abilities during competition. Sleep deprivation can significantly impair performance, reducing endurance, strength, and cognitive function. Research with basketball players found that extending sleep to 10 hours per night improved free-throw shooting accuracy by 9% and three-point shooting by 9.2%. Sleep also affects injury risk, as tired athletes have slower reaction times and impaired judgment, making them more susceptible to accidents. Additionally, insufficient sleep compromises the immune system, increasing the likelihood of illness that can disrupt training schedules. Sports scientists now recognize sleep as the ultimate performance enhancer, leading many professional teams to employ sleep specialists and create optimal sleep environments for their athletes.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • recovery:회복
  • repair:수리, 회복
  • adaptation:적응
  • REM:렘수면
  • consolidate:통합하다
  • deprivation:부족
  • cognitive:인지의
  • susceptible:취약한
  • compromise:손상시키다

Questions

  • ② How sleep affects athletic performance and recovery
  • ③ The role of coaches in athlete development
  • ④ Injury prevention strategies in professional sports
  • ⑤ The psychology of competitive sports

Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 24]

Vertical Farming and Urban Agriculture

Vertical farming represents an innovative approach to agriculture that involves growing crops in vertically stacked layers, often in controlled indoor environments. This method uses artificial lighting, climate control, and hydroponic or aeroponic systems to create optimal growing conditions year-round. Vertical farms can produce significantly higher yields per square meter compared to traditional farming, with some facilities claiming up to 365 times more productivity. The controlled environment eliminates the need for pesticides and herbicides, while precise nutrient delivery systems ensure optimal plant growth. Water usage is dramatically reduced through recirculation systems that can use up to 95% less water than conventional farming. Vertical farming also eliminates weather-related risks and allows for crop production in urban areas, reducing transportation costs and carbon emissions from food distribution. However, the technology faces significant challenges, including high initial investment costs and substantial energy requirements for artificial lighting. Critics argue that the energy consumption may offset environmental benefits unless renewable energy sources are used. Despite these challenges, vertical farming shows promise for addressing food security in densely populated urban areas and regions with limited arable land.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • vertically:수직으로
  • artificial lighting:인공 조명
  • hydroponic:수경재배의
  • aeroponic:공기재배의
  • yield:수확량
  • nutrient:영양분
  • recirculation:순환
  • urban:도시의
  • emission:배출
  • investment:투자
  • arable:경작할 수 있는

Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 25]

The Psychology of Procrastination

Procrastination is a common behavioral pattern characterized by the voluntary delay of important tasks despite knowing that this delay may lead to negative consequences. Research in psychology has identified several underlying factors that contribute to procrastination. Perfectionism often plays a significant role, as individuals may delay starting tasks because they fear their work will not meet their own high standards. The brain's reward system also influences procrastination, as people naturally prefer immediate gratification over long-term benefits. Tasks that seem overwhelming or ambiguous are more likely to be postponed, as the brain perceives them as threats and triggers avoidance behaviors. Low self-confidence and fear of failure can create a cycle where individuals delay tasks to avoid potential disappointment. Interestingly, some people engage in what researchers call "productive procrastination," where they avoid one task by completing other useful activities. Understanding the psychological roots of procrastination has led to effective interventions, including breaking large tasks into smaller, manageable steps, setting specific deadlines, and using accountability systems. Cognitive-behavioral therapy techniques can also help individuals change thought patterns that contribute to chronic procrastination.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • behavioral:행동의
  • procrastination:미루기
  • perfectionism:완벽주의
  • reward system:보상 체계
  • ambiguous:모호한
  • avoidance:회피
  • intervention:개입, 중재
  • accountability:책임감
  • cognitive-behavioral:인지행동의

  • ⑤ "The Role of Rewards in Motivating Students"

[지문 26]

Gene Therapy and Modern Medicine

Gene therapy represents a revolutionary approach to treating diseases by modifying or replacing defective genes within a patient's cells. This cutting-edge medical technique involves introducing healthy genetic material into cells to correct inherited disorders or acquired diseases. Several methods are used to deliver therapeutic genes, including modified viruses that serve as vectors to transport genes into target cells. CRISPR-Cas9 technology has emerged as a particularly promising tool, allowing scientists to make precise edits to DNA sequences with unprecedented accuracy. Gene therapy has shown remarkable success in treating certain genetic conditions, such as severe combined immunodeficiency and some forms of inherited blindness. Clinical trials have demonstrated the potential to treat hemophilia, sickle cell disease, and various types of cancer using gene therapy approaches. However, the field faces significant challenges, including ensuring the safety of gene delivery methods, controlling where and how long therapeutic genes remain active, and addressing ethical concerns about genetic modification. The high cost of development and treatment also limits accessibility. Despite these challenges, gene therapy offers hope for treating previously incurable diseases and represents a paradigm shift toward personalized medicine based on individual genetic profiles.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • revolutionary:혁명적인
  • modify:수정하다
  • defective:결함이 있는
  • inherited:유전된
  • vector:벡터
  • CRISPR:크리스퍼
  • emerge:나타나다
  • immunodeficiency:면역결핍
  • hemophilia:혈우병
  • ethical:윤리적인
  • incurable:치료 불가능한

  • ② Gene therapy: potential and challenges in modern medicine
  • ③ The cost-effectiveness of traditional medical treatments

  • ⑤ The role of viruses in disease transmission

Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 27]

The Economics of the Sharing Economy

The sharing economy, exemplified by companies like Uber, Airbnb, and TaskRabbit, has transformed how people access goods and services by enabling individuals to monetize underutilized assets. This economic model is based on the principle of collaborative consumption, where people share resources rather than owning them individually. Technology platforms connect service providers with consumers, creating peer-to-peer marketplaces that bypass traditional business models. The sharing economy offers several economic benefits, including increased efficiency in resource utilization, lower costs for consumers, and new income opportunities for individuals. For example, car-sharing services reduce the need for individual car ownership while providing mobility solutions, and home-sharing platforms allow homeowners to generate income from unused space. However, this model also presents challenges to traditional industries and regulatory frameworks. Critics argue that sharing economy companies often avoid the costs and responsibilities associated with traditional employment, leaving workers without benefits or job security. Additionally, the concentration of market power in a few large platforms raises concerns about monopolistic behavior and fair competition. The sharing economy's impact on taxation, urban planning, and worker rights continues to evolve as governments adapt regulations to this new economic paradigm.

  • transform:변화시키다
  • monetize:수익화하다
  • asset:자산
  • consumption:소비

  • peer-to-peer:개인 간
  • marketplace:시장
  • efficiency:효율성
  • mobility:이동성
  • regulatory:규제의
  • monopolistic:독점적인
  • taxation:과세

[지문 28]

The Impact of Artificial Intelligence on Healthcare

Artificial intelligence is revolutionizing healthcare by enhancing diagnostic accuracy, improving treatment outcomes, and streamlining administrative processes. Machine learning algorithms can analyze vast amounts of medical data, including imaging scans, laboratory results, and patient records, to identify patterns that might be missed by human clinicians. AI-powered diagnostic tools have demonstrated remarkable accuracy in detecting diseases such as cancer, diabetic retinopathy, and heart conditions from medical images. Natural language processing enables AI systems to extract valuable information from unstructured medical notes and research papers, helping doctors stay current with medical literature. AI also assists in drug discovery by analyzing molecular structures and predicting how potential medications will interact with the human body, potentially reducing the time and cost of developing new treatments. Robotic surgery systems enhanced with AI provide surgeons with greater precision and control during complex procedures. However, the integration of AI in healthcare raises important concerns about privacy, data security, and the need to maintain human oversight in medical decision-making. The liability questions surrounding AI-assisted diagnoses and the potential for algorithmic bias also require careful consideration as these technologies become more widespread in clinical practice.

  • diagnostic:진단의

[지문 29]

Climate Change and Agricultural Adaptation

Climate change is significantly affecting global agriculture through alterations in temperature, precipitation patterns, and extreme weather events. Rising temperatures are shifting growing seasons and altering the geographical distribution of crops, with some regions becoming unsuitable for traditional agricultural practices while others gain new growing potential. Changes in rainfall patterns create both droughts and flooding, disrupting crop production and soil health. Increased frequency of extreme weather events, such as hurricanes, hailstorms, and heat waves, poses additional risks to agricultural productivity. These changes threaten global food security, particularly in developing countries where agriculture is a primary economic activity. However, farmers and agricultural scientists are developing adaptation strategies to address these challenges. Drought-resistant crop varieties, developed through both traditional breeding and genetic modification, can survive in water-scarce conditions. Precision agriculture technologies help farmers optimize water and nutrient use, reducing vulnerability to climate variability. Crop rotation and diversification strategies can improve soil health and reduce risks associated with climate-related crop failures. Additionally, sustainable farming practices, such as conservation tillage and agroforestry, can help mitigate climate change impacts while maintaining agricultural productivity.

  • shift:이동시키다

  • flooding:홍수
  • hailstorm:우박 폭풍

Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 30]

The Science of Social Media Addiction

Social media addiction shares many characteristics with other behavioral addictions, involving compulsive use despite negative consequences for personal relationships, work, or mental health. The design of social media platforms exploits psychological principles to maximize user engagement and time spent on the platform. Variable ratio reinforcement schedules, similar to those used in gambling, create intermittent rewards through likes, comments, and shares, making the behavior highly addictive. The brain releases dopamine when users receive social validation through these platforms, creating a cycle of seeking and receiving rewards. Fear of Missing Out (FOMO) drives users to constantly check their feeds to stay updated on others' activities, while social comparison mechanisms can lead to decreased self-esteem and anxiety. Excessive social media use has been linked to sleep disruption, reduced face-to-face social skills, and increased rates of depression and anxiety, particularly among adolescents. Neuroimaging studies show that heavy social media users display similar brain activation patterns to those seen in substance addictions. Understanding these mechanisms has led to the development of interventions, including digital wellness apps, usage tracking tools, and cognitive-behavioral therapy approaches specifically designed to address problematic social media use.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • addiction:중독
  • behavioral:행동의
  • compulsive:강박적인
  • exploit:이용하다
  • reinforcement:강화
  • intermittent:간헐적인
  • dopamine:도파민
  • validation:인정
  • anxiety:불안
  • disruption:방해
  • face-to-face:대면의
  • neuroimaging:뇌영상
  • activation:활성화
  • intervention:개입

[지문 31]

The Benefits of Reading Books

Reading books provides numerous benefits for both mental and physical health. When people read regularly, their vocabulary expands and their language skills improve significantly. Reading also enhances concentration and focus, as it requires sustained attention for extended periods. Studies have shown that reading fiction helps develop empathy by allowing readers to experience different perspectives and emotions through characters' experiences. Additionally, reading before bedtime can improve sleep quality by helping the mind relax and reducing stress levels. Regular reading has been linked to slower cognitive decline in older adults, suggesting that it may help prevent memory-related diseases. Furthermore, reading increases general knowledge and keeps people informed about various topics, making them better conversationalists and more well-rounded individuals.

Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 32]

The Rise of Online Learning

Online learning has become increasingly popular due to its flexibility and accessibility. Students can access course materials from anywhere with an internet connection, allowing them to learn at their own pace and schedule. This format is particularly beneficial for working adults who cannot attend traditional classroom sessions. Online courses often cost less than in-person classes, making education more affordable for many people. However, online learning also presents certain challenges. Students must be highly self-motivated and disciplined to succeed without the structure of regular classroom attendance. Technical difficulties and poor internet connections can disrupt the learning process. Additionally, some subjects that require hands-on practice or laboratory work are difficult to teach effectively online.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • flexibility:유연성
  • accessibility:접근성
  • schedule:일정
  • affordable:감당할 수 있는
  • self-motivated:자기주도적인
  • disciplined:자기통제력이 있는
  • technical:기술적인
  • disrupt:방해하다

Questions

  • ② "Online Learning: Advantages and Challenges"
  • ③ "How to Choose the Best Online Course"
  • ④ "Technology Problems in Education"
  • ⑤ "The Future of Traditional Classrooms"
146
Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 33]

Urban Gardening Movement

Urban gardening has emerged as a popular solution to several modern challenges. In crowded cities where green space is limited, people are creating gardens on rooftops, balconies, and small plots of land. These urban gardens provide fresh, organic produce that would otherwise be expensive to purchase. Growing food locally also reduces the environmental impact of transportation and packaging. Urban gardening projects often bring communities together, as neighbors collaborate to maintain shared garden spaces. Children who participate in these gardens learn valuable lessons about where food comes from and develop healthier eating habits. Additionally, urban gardens help improve air quality and provide habitat for birds and insects in city environments.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • limited:제한된
  • organic:유기농의
  • environmental impact:환경 영향
  • transportation:운송
  • collaborate:협력하다
  • habitat:서식지

  • ② Urban gardening as a solution to city challenges

  • ④ The importance of organic food production
  • ⑤ Community building activities in neighborhoods
147
Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 34]

The Science of Habits

Understanding how habits form can help people develop positive behaviors and break negative ones. Neuroscientists have discovered that habits are created through a three-step process called the "habit loop." First, there is a cue or trigger that tells the brain to enter automatic mode. Next comes the routine, which is the actual behavior or action. Finally, there is a reward that helps the brain remember and repeat the pattern. The more often this loop is repeated, the stronger the habit becomes. To change a habit, experts suggest keeping the same cue and reward while substituting a new routine. For example, if someone wants to stop eating unhealthy snacks in the afternoon, they could keep the afternoon timing (cue) and the satisfaction feeling (reward) while replacing the unhealthy snack with a healthy one (new routine).

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • neuroscientist:신경과학자
  • cue:신호, 단서
  • routine:일상, 루틴
  • reward:보상
  • substitute:대체하다

Questions

34.다음 글의 요지로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① 습관의 형성 과정과 변화 방법에 대한 과학적 이해
  • ② 건강한 식습관 개발을 위한 구체적 방법
  • ③ 뇌과학 연구의 최신 발전 동향
  • ④ 자동 모드와 의식적 행동의 차이점
  • ⑤ 부정적 행동 패턴의 위험성
148
Vera's Flavor - PART 2 대의 파악
[지문 35]

The Impact of Music on Exercise

Research shows that listening to music during exercise can significantly improve performance and make workouts more enjoyable. Music with a fast tempo and strong beat helps people maintain a steady rhythm during activities like running or cycling. The brain releases endorphins when people listen to music they enjoy, which can reduce the perception of effort and make exercise feel easier. Music also serves as a distraction from physical discomfort, allowing people to exercise for longer periods. Different types of music work better for different activities - upbeat songs are ideal for cardio workouts, while slower music may be better for stretching or yoga. Many fitness experts recommend creating playlists that match the intensity of planned workouts.

  • performance:성과
  • tempo:템포, 박자

  • distraction:주의 분산
  • upbeat:경쾌한
  • intensity:강도

Sustainable Transportation

Cities around the world are adopting sustainable transportation solutions to reduce pollution and traffic congestion. Electric buses and trains produce fewer emissions than vehicles powered by fossil fuels. Bike-sharing programs encourage people to use bicycles for short trips instead of cars. Many cities are creating dedicated bike lanes and pedestrian-only zones to promote alternative forms of transportation. Public transit systems are being expanded and improved to make them more convenient and reliable. Some cities offer incentives such as reduced fees for electric vehicle owners or free parking for hybrid cars. These changes not only help protect the environment but also improve air quality and create more livable urban spaces for residents.

[지문 37]

The Benefits of Multilingualism

Speaking multiple languages provides numerous cognitive and social benefits. Bilingual and multilingual individuals often show improved problem-solving skills and greater mental flexibility. They can switch between different ways of thinking and are better at multitasking. Research suggests that speaking multiple languages may help delay the onset of dementia and other age-related cognitive disorders. From a social perspective, knowing multiple languages opens doors to different cultures and allows for better communication with diverse groups of people. In today's globalized world, multilingual individuals often have better job opportunities and higher earning potential. Additionally, learning new languages can boost confidence and provide personal satisfaction.

[지문 38]

Food Preservation Methods

Throughout history, humans have developed various methods to preserve food and extend its shelf life. Traditional techniques include drying, salting, smoking, and fermentation. Drying removes moisture that bacteria and mold need to grow, while salting draws out water and creates an environment where harmful microorganisms cannot survive. Smoking not only removes moisture but also adds chemicals that prevent spoilage. Fermentation uses beneficial bacteria to create an acidic environment that prevents dangerous bacteria from growing. Modern preservation methods include refrigeration, freezing, and canning. These techniques have made it possible to store food safely for longer periods and transport it over great distances, helping to ensure food security worldwide.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • preserve:보존하다
  • shelf life:유통기한
  • drying:건조
  • salting:염장
  • smoking:훈제
  • fermentation:발효
  • harmful microorganism:유해 미생물
  • spoilage:부패
  • refrigeration:냉장
  • freezing:냉동
  • canning:통조림
  • transport:운송하다

Questions

  • ③ "How Bacteria Affects Food Safety"
  • ④ "The Development of the Refrigerator"
  • ⑤ "Global Food Distribution Systems"

[지문 39]

The Psychology of Color

Colors have a significant psychological impact on human emotions and behavior. Warm colors like red, orange, and yellow tend to create feelings of energy, enthusiasm, and warmth. Red is often associated with passion and excitement, while yellow is linked to happiness and optimism. Cool colors such as blue, green, and purple typically have a calming effect and are associated with tranquility and relaxation. Blue is often used in bedrooms and offices because it promotes focus and productivity. Green, the color of nature, is believed to reduce stress and promote healing. The intensity of colors also matters - bright, vibrant colors tend to be stimulating, while soft, muted colors are more soothing. Understanding color psychology can help in choosing paint colors for homes, designing workspaces, and even selecting clothing.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • psychological:심리적인
  • warm color:따뜻한 색
  • enthusiasm:열정

  • cool color:차가운 색

  • productivity:생산성
  • intensity:강도
  • vibrant:생생한

[지문 40]

The Importance of Water Conservation

Water is one of Earth's most precious resources, yet many people take it for granted. Freshwater makes up only a small percentage of all water on Earth, and much of it is not easily accessible for human use. As the global population grows and climate change affects weather patterns, water scarcity is becoming an increasingly serious problem. Simple conservation efforts can make a significant difference. Households can reduce water usage by fixing leaks, taking shorter showers, and using efficient appliances. Industries and agriculture also need to adopt water-saving technologies and practices. Governments can implement policies to protect water sources and encourage conservation. Individual actions, while seemingly small, can have a collective impact when adopted by many people working toward the same goal.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • precious:소중한
  • freshwater:담수
  • accessible:접근 가능한

  • household:가정
  • efficient:효율적인

  • technology:기술
  • government:정부
  • individual:개인의
  • collective:집단적인

[지문 41]

Social Media and Teenagers

Social media has become a major part of teenagers' daily lives. Most teens spend several hours each day on platforms like Instagram, TikTok, and Snapchat. While social media helps them stay connected with friends and express themselves creatively, it can also cause problems. Many teenagers compare themselves to others online, which can lead to feelings of anxiety and low self-esteem. Sleep problems are also common when teens use their phones late at night. Additionally, cyberbullying through social media can seriously hurt young people's mental health. Parents and teachers need to help teenagers learn how to use social media in a healthy and balanced way.

  • connected:연결된

The Power of Exercise

Regular exercise is one of the best things people can do for their health. Physical activity helps strengthen muscles and bones, making the body stronger. Exercise also improves heart health and helps people maintain a healthy weight. Beyond physical benefits, exercise has positive effects on mental health too. When people exercise, their bodies release chemicals called endorphins that make them feel happier and more relaxed. Exercise can also help people sleep better at night and have more energy during the day. Even simple activities like walking or riding a bike can make a big difference in overall health and well-being.

Learning a foreign language can be challenging but very rewarding. When people learn new languages, they open doors to different cultures and meet new people from around the world. Many employers prefer to hire workers who can speak multiple languages because they can communicate with more customers and business partners. Language learning also helps improve memory and thinking skills. Students who study foreign languages often do better in other subjects too. With today's technology, there are many ways to learn languages, including apps, online courses, and language exchange programs. The key to success is practice and patience.

Recycling in Our Community

Recycling is an important way to protect our environment and save natural resources. When we recycle paper, plastic, glass, and metal, these materials can be made into new products instead of being thrown away. This helps reduce the amount of trash that goes to landfills and prevents pollution. Many communities have programs that make recycling easy for families. They provide special bins for different types of materials and pick them up regularly. Some schools also teach students about recycling and organize competitions to see which class can recycle the most. By working together, communities can make a real difference in protecting the planet for future generations.

The Importance of Breakfast

Many people skip breakfast because they are in a hurry or want to lose weight, but eating a healthy breakfast is very important. Breakfast provides the fuel your body and brain need after not eating all night. Students who eat breakfast usually have better concentration in class and perform better on tests. A good breakfast should include foods from different groups, such as whole grains, fruits, and protein. These foods give steady energy throughout the morning. People who eat breakfast regularly also tend to make healthier food choices during the rest of the day. Simple breakfast options like cereal with milk, toast with peanut butter, or yogurt with fruit can make a big impact on daily performance.

Pets and Human Health

Having pets can bring many benefits to people's lives. Pet owners often feel less lonely because their animals provide companionship and unconditional love. Playing with pets and taking dogs for walks gives people regular exercise, which is good for physical health. Pets can also help reduce stress and anxiety. Studies show that petting a dog or cat can lower blood pressure and help people feel more relaxed. For children, having pets teaches responsibility as they learn to feed, clean, and care for their animals. However, owning pets also requires time, money, and commitment. People should carefully consider whether they are ready for this responsibility before getting a pet.

Teamwork in Sports

Sports teach us many valuable lessons, and one of the most important is teamwork. In team sports like basketball, soccer, or volleyball, players must work together to achieve a common goal. Each player has different strengths and skills, and the best teams know how to use each person's abilities effectively. Good teamwork requires communication, trust, and putting the team's success before individual achievements. Players learn to support each other, especially when someone makes a mistake. These teamwork skills are useful not only in sports but also in school projects, jobs, and other areas of life. Learning to work well with others is a skill that will help people throughout their lives.

Reading for Pleasure

Many students only read when they have to for school assignments, but reading for pleasure can be very enjoyable and beneficial. When people read books they choose themselves, they can explore different worlds and learn about topics that interest them. Reading fiction helps develop imagination and creativity, while non-fiction books can teach new skills or provide information about hobbies. Regular reading also improves vocabulary and writing ability. People who read for fun often find it relaxing and use books as a way to escape from daily stress. Libraries and bookstores offer thousands of books on every topic imaginable. Finding the right book can turn reading from a chore into a favorite activity.

Volunteer Work

Volunteering is a great way for people to help their communities while also gaining valuable experiences. Volunteers can work with different organizations, such as animal shelters, food banks, hospitals, or schools. These activities help people in need and make communities stronger. Volunteering also provides many personal benefits. People often learn new skills, meet new friends, and feel good about making a positive difference in the world. For students, volunteer work can help them explore different career paths and build their resumes. Many colleges and employers look favorably on applicants who have volunteer experience because it shows they care about others and are willing to work hard.

[지문 50]

Healthy Sleep Habits

Good sleep is essential for both physical and mental health, especially for teenagers whose bodies and brains are still developing. Most teens need between 8-10 hours of sleep each night, but many don't get enough. Poor sleep can affect mood, concentration, and academic performance. To improve sleep quality, experts recommend several healthy habits. Going to bed and waking up at the same time every day helps establish a regular sleep schedule. Avoiding screens for at least an hour before bedtime is also important because the blue light from phones and computers can make it harder to fall asleep. Creating a quiet, dark, and cool sleeping environment can also help people get better rest.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary
