After reviewing another one of her disappointing assignments, her instructor calmly told her, "You cannot fill a cup that is already full." The bluntness of the statement startled Sophie. She finally recognized that her own pride and refusal to learn were the true barriers to her growth. Humbled, she apologized and asked to start the next project with a beginner's mind.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • arrogant:거만한
  • stubbornly:완고하게
  • superior:우월한
  • consequently:결과적으로
  • bluntness:직설적임, 무딤
  • beginner's mind:초심

Questions

17.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① It is physically impossible to add liquid to a full cup.
  • ② One must be open-minded and humble to acquire new knowledge or skills.
  • ③ Pride and arrogance prevent people from seeing their own flaws.
  • ④ To learn something new, one must first forget everything they know.
  • ⑤ A good student must always be ready to receive advice.
236
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 18]

The Desert Rose

Emma often lamented her circumstances, feeling trapped in a small, uneventful town with limited opportunities. She was deeply envious of her friends who lived in vibrant, bustling cities, believing their lives were inherently more exciting. Her grandmother, a woman who had weathered immense hardships in her life, listened to Emma's complaints one afternoon. She then said, with a quiet strength, "The most beautiful roses often grow in the harshest deserts." Emma didn't grasp the meaning at first, but years later, she realized how the challenges of her youth had forged in her a resilience and compassion she might never have developed in an easier environment.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • lament:한탄하다, 애통해하다
  • uneventful:특별한 일이 없는
  • envious:부러워하는
  • weather:(역경 등을) 이겨내다, 견디다
  • immense:엄청난
  • resilience:회복력
  • compassion:연민, 동정심

Questions

18.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Roses that grow in deserts are scientifically proven to be more beautiful.
  • ② Great strength and character can be forged through adversity and difficult circumstances.
  • ③ One should not complain about their situation but try to make the best of it.
  • ④ Beauty can be found in the most unexpected places.
  • ⑤ Hardships are a necessary prerequisite for a successful life.
237
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 19]

The Locked Door

Ryan was perpetually searching for shortcuts, convinced there had to be an easy path to success. He envied classmates who seemed to achieve greatness without any apparent effort, and he often complained to his father about the unfairness of it all. His father, a self-made man, listened to his son's frustrations. He then stated a simple, unyielding truth: "There are no shortcuts to any place worth going." Ryan initially resisted the idea, clinging to his desire for a simpler way. However, when he finally committed to consistent, hard work, he discovered that the satisfaction from his earned achievements was far more profound than any easy victory could have been.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • perpetually:끊임없이
  • shortcut:지름길
  • apparent:외견상의, 분명한
  • unyielding:확고한, 굽히지 않는
  • resist:저항하다
  • profound:깊은, 심오한

Questions

19.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① All valuable destinations are physically far away and difficult to reach.
  • ② Meaningful achievements and true success require dedicated effort and cannot be reached through easy methods.
  • ③ The easiest path is often the most dangerous one.
  • ④ One should be suspicious of anything that seems too easy.
  • ⑤ The journey is more important than the destination.
238
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 20]

The Wounded Bird

Jessica found a small bird with a broken wing huddled in her backyard. Her immediate instinct was to help it, to capture it and tend to its injury. But every time she approached, the frightened bird would flutter away painfully, despite its wound. Frustrated, Jessica asked her mother why the bird wouldn't accept her help. Her mother watched the struggling creature for a moment, then said softly, "Sometimes, true love means letting go, even when it hurts to watch." Jessica realized that her desire to control the situation might be causing more harm. Instead of trying to capture it, she left food and water nearby, respecting the bird's need for space and freedom.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • huddled:웅크린
  • tend to:~을 돌보다
  • flutter:퍼덕거리다
  • compassion:연민
  • respect:존중하다

Questions

20.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① If you truly love something, you must set it free.
  • ② Genuine care sometimes requires respecting another's autonomy, even if it's difficult to witness their struggle.
  • ③ Injured animals should be observed from a distance rather than captured.
  • ④ Love is an inherently painful emotion.
  • ⑤ The desire to help can sometimes be selfish.
239
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 21]

The Potter's Wheel

Daniel, a novice potter, grew deeply frustrated with his clumsy first attempts. He yearned to create the elegant vases that his teacher crafted with such ease, but his hands lacked the necessary skill. On the verge of quitting, he lamented that he simply had no talent for the art. His teacher, without a word, took a shapeless lump of wet clay and slammed it onto the wheel. As it spun, she said, "The clay must be broken down and centered before it can become something beautiful." Daniel understood. His current failures weren't an end, but a necessary, formative part of the learning process itself.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • novice:초심자
  • yearn:갈망하다, 동경하다
  • on the verge of:~의 직전에
  • lament:한탄하다
  • formative:발달에 중요한, 형성되는

Questions

21.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① To create pottery, one must first prepare the clay properly.
  • ② One must undergo a process of deconstruction and realignment before true growth can occur.
  • ③ Failure is an essential ingredient for eventual success in any art form.
  • ④ The initial stages of learning are always the most difficult and frustrating.
  • ⑤ True talent lies in the ability to start over after a failure.
240
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 22]

The Compass

Sarah felt utterly lost and overwhelmed in her vast new high school. With no friends and a bewildering array of clubs and activities, she didn't know where to begin. She confided in her older brother, who saw her paralysis of choice. He offered a simple piece of advice he'd learned from their grandfather. "You don't need to see the whole staircase to take the first step." Taking this to heart, Sarah decided to ignore the overwhelming big picture and simply join one activity that seemed interesting: the photography club. This single step led to new friendships and a growing sense of confidence that made the rest of the staircase seem far less daunting.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • utterly:완전히, 순전히
  • overwhelmed:압도된
  • bewildering:어리둥절하게 만드는
  • confide in:~에게 비밀을 털어놓다
  • paralysis:마비
  • daunting:벅찬, 주눅이 들게 하는

Questions

22.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① It is unsafe to climb a staircase without seeing its entirety.
  • ② To achieve a large goal, one should focus on the immediate, manageable action rather than the intimidating final outcome.
  • ③ The first step of any journey is always the most significant.
  • ④ Progress should be made incrementally, one step at a time.
  • ⑤ Overthinking can prevent one from taking necessary action.
241
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 23]

The Seed's Journey

Mark, a young and impatient gardener, was disappointed. Weeks after planting his garden, there was no visible sign of life, just barren soil. He complained to his grandfather that all his effort had been a waste of time. His grandfather simply picked up a spare seed from his pocket, held it in his palm, and said with a knowing smile, "What grows in darkness will eventually shine in the light." Mark was puzzled by the cryptic remark. But months later, when his garden erupted in a riot of color and life, he finally understood that the most critical and foundational growth happens unseen, beneath the surface.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • barren:척박한, 불모의
  • knowing:모든 것을 안다는 듯한
  • cryptic:수수께끼 같은, 아리송한
  • erupt:분출하다, 폭발하다
  • foundational:기초적인, 근본적인

Questions

23.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Plants that grow in the dark are stronger than those that grow in the sun.
  • ② Periods of unseen preparation and struggle are essential for future visible success.
  • ③ It is important to have faith during difficult times when progress is not apparent.
  • ④ The beginning of any project is always characterized by a lack of results.
  • ⑤ True growth is a slow and gradual process.
242
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 24]

The Bridge Builder

Anna was terrified of public speaking, consistently finding ways to avoid presentations at work. An important conference was approaching where she was expected to speak, and she was on the verge of feigning illness to get out of it. Her coach, sensing her panic, sat her down. "Anna," he said, "everyone feels fear." He then added, "Courage is not the absence of fear; it's walking across the bridge while your knees are shaking." The image resonated with her. Despite her pounding heart and trembling hands, Anna delivered her speech, discovering that bravery wasn't about not feeling afraid, but about acting in spite of it.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • feign:~인 체하다, 가장하다
  • resonate:울려 퍼지다, 공명하다
  • in spite of:~에도 불구하고

Questions

24.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① True courage is defined as acting despite feeling fear, not the lack of fear itself.
  • ② It is dangerous to cross a bridge if one feels physically unstable.
  • ③ Overcoming fear is the first step to building courage.
  • ④ Everyone experiences fear, but only brave people can hide it.
  • ⑤ Courage is a skill that can be developed through practice.
243
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 25]

The Weaver's Pattern

Tom felt bitter and resentful about the constant stream of challenges in his life. His friends, it seemed, sailed through life with ease while he was always struggling. He confided his frustrations to his wise aunt, a master weaver. She led him to her loom and showed him the back of a complex tapestry she was working on. It was a chaotic mess of tangled threads and knots. Then, she flipped it over to reveal the stunning, intricate masterpiece on the front. "From the back, you only see tangled threads," she explained, "but from the front, you see the grand design." Tom began to suspect that his own struggles might be part of a larger, meaningful pattern he just couldn't see yet.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • resentful:분개하는, 원망하는
  • confide:(비밀을) 털어놓다
  • weaver:직공, 베 짜는 사람
  • loom:베틀
  • tapestry:태피스트리(여러 가지 색실로 그림을 짜 넣은 직물)
  • intricate:복잡한, 정교한

Questions

25.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① A different perspective can reveal the hidden beauty in chaos.
  • ② Current hardships and struggles may be integral parts of a larger, meaningful purpose that is not yet apparent.
  • ③ It is important to focus on the final outcome rather than the messy process.
  • ④ Life is a combination of both chaotic and beautiful moments.
  • ⑤ One must look at things from the correct side to appreciate them.
244
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 26]

The Mountain Echo

Lisa had a habit of criticizing others and spreading negative gossip about her classmates. She couldn't understand why people seemed to avoid her, leaving her feeling isolated and lonely. One day, her teacher overheard her making another sharp complaint about a classmate. The teacher didn't scold her, but instead asked her a question. "Lisa, have you ever shouted into a valley?" She then added, "What you send out into the world comes back to you, like an echo." The simple analogy struck Lisa profoundly. She realized that the negativity and unfriendliness she was experiencing were merely reflections of her own actions and attitude.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • gossip:험담, 소문
  • isolated:고립된
  • scold:꾸짖다
  • profoundly:깊이, 심오하게
  • reflection:반영, 반사

Questions

26.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Shouting in a valley will produce an echo.
  • ② One's actions and attitudes toward others will ultimately be reflected back upon oneself.
  • ③ Negative comments travel quickly and can harm one's reputation.
  • ④ The world is a fair place where people get what they deserve.
  • ⑤ To receive positivity, one must first be positive.
245
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 27]

The Gardener's Secret

Ben was perplexed. His neighbor's garden was always a vibrant tapestry of color, far more beautiful than his own, even though he used the same expensive fertilizers and followed the same gardening techniques from books. Finally, he swallowed his pride and asked his neighbor for his secret. The old man smiled warmly, gesturing to his flourishing plants. "I talk to my plants every day," he said, "not because they understand my words, but because they feel my care." Ben came to understand that genuine, consistent attention and affection were the most potent ingredients of all, in gardening as in life.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • perplexed:당혹스러운, 혼란스러운
  • vibrant:활기찬, 생생한
  • fertilizer:비료
  • flourishing:무성한, 번창하는
  • genuine:진짜의, 진실한
  • potent:강력한, 효과적인

Questions

27.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Talking to plants can help them grow better.
  • ② The most crucial element for success is not technique, but genuine love and dedication.
  • ③ The neighbor believes his plants have the ability to feel emotions.
  • ④ Emotional connection is more important than material investment.
  • ⑤ The secret to success often lies in simple, overlooked actions.
246
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 28]

The Clock's Wisdom

Jake was perpetually stressed, always rushing from one task to the next, complaining that there were never enough hours in the day. He felt like he was in a constant battle against time. His grandmother, observing his frantic pace, offered him a cup of tea and some gentle advice. "Time is like water," she mused, "the tighter you grasp it, the more it slips through your fingers." Jake was skeptical at first, but when he tried to consciously relax and prioritize his tasks instead of fighting the clock, he found that he accomplished more with far less anxiety.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • perpetually:끊임없이
  • frantic:정신없이 서두는, 광적인
  • mus(e):사색하다, 골똘히 생각하다
  • grasp:꽉 잡다
  • skeptical:회의적인
  • prioritize:우선순위를 매기다

Questions

28.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① It is impossible to hold water tightly in one's hand.
  • ② Trying too hard to control or manage time can lead to inefficiency and stress.
  • ③ Time, like water, is a precious and essential resource.
  • ④ One should let time flow naturally without trying to interfere with it.
  • ⑤ A relaxed approach to work is more productive than a stressed one.
247
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 29]

The Artist's Canvas

Emily was a perfectionist, an artist who would discard any painting that contained the slightest flaw. Her studio was filled with crumpled canvases, evidence of her frustration. She felt she could never create the perfect work she envisioned. Her art teacher, visiting one day, picked up one of her "failed" paintings, a beautiful landscape marred by a single, accidental streak of black paint. "In art, as in life," the teacher said softly, "the cracks are where the light gets in." Emily learned to embrace her imperfections, discovering that they often added a unique character and depth to her work that perfection never could.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • perfectionist:완벽주의자
  • discard:버리다
  • flaw:결점, 흠
  • mar:손상시키다, 망치다
  • embrace:(껴)안다, 받아들이다
  • imperfection:불완전함

Questions

29.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Light can physically enter through cracks in an object.
  • ② Flaws and imperfections can unexpectedly become sources of beauty and insight.
  • ③ It is through our mistakes that we learn the most valuable lessons.
  • ④ True art is about accepting things as they are, not forcing them to be perfect.
  • ⑤ A positive perspective can turn a negative situation into a positive one.
248
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 30]

The Tree's Lesson

David was consumed by jealousy. His friend had just received a major promotion, and David couldn't help but compare his own slower career path, feeling inadequate and unhappy. His father, noticing his son's gloom, took him for a walk in a dense forest. He pointed to a towering oak standing next to a slender pine. "The oak doesn't try to be a pine, and the pine doesn't envy the oak," his father observed. "Each has its own purpose and its own beauty." In the quiet of the forest, David realized the futility of his comparisons. He had to focus on his own unique growth, not someone else's.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • consumed by:~에 사로잡힌
  • inadequate:부적절한, 불충분한
  • gloom:우울
  • towering:높이 솟은
  • slender:날씬한, 가느다란
  • futility:무익함, 헛됨

Questions

30.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Oak trees and pine trees are fundamentally different species.
  • ② One should accept and appreciate one's own unique qualities and path instead of comparing oneself to others.
  • ③ In nature, different species coexist without competition.
  • ④ True happiness comes from understanding one's purpose in life.
  • ⑤ Trying to be someone you are not will only lead to unhappiness.
249
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 31]

The Fisherman's Net

Carlos, an impatient young fisherman, grew increasingly frustrated as the hours passed without a single bite. Convinced the fish were simply in a different part of the vast lake, he constantly pulled up his anchor and moved his boat, leaving ripples of agitation in his wake. His experienced grandfather, sitting calmly in the same spot since dawn, watched his grandson's restless movements with a knowing sigh. After Carlos had moved for the fifth time, the old man called out across the water, his voice steady and clear, "A fisherman who chases the fish all day will only come home with an empty net and a tired heart." Carlos paused, the wisdom of the words sinking in. He learned that day that success in fishing, and perhaps in life, often comes not from frantic searching, but from patient, focused waiting.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • agitation:동요, 불안
  • in one's wake:~의 뒤를 따라, ~의 결과로
  • restless:가만히 못 있는
  • frantic:정신없이 서두는, 광적인
  • focused:집중된

Questions

31.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Constantly changing one's strategy without patience leads only to exhaustion and failure.
  • ② A good fisherman must know the right spots to find fish.
  • ③ Chasing after something aggressively is less effective than waiting for it.
  • ④ Fishing requires a calm and peaceful state of mind.
  • ⑤ An empty net is a sign of a fisherman's lack of skill.
250
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 32]

The Musician's Practice

Maya, a dedicated piano student, dreamed of playing complex concertos like a professional. However, she often felt discouraged when her daily practice sessions didn't yield immediate, dramatic results. She complained to her teacher that her slow progress felt insignificant. Her teacher, a wise old maestro, didn't offer words of comfort. Instead, he led her to a window overlooking a small stream that trickled down the mountain. "A single raindrop makes no sound in the river," he said, "but enough of them together can carve a canyon." Maya understood. Each seemingly small practice session was a single drop, contributing to a powerful, cumulative force that would eventually shape her mastery.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • concerto:협주곡
  • yield:산출하다, 낳다
  • insignificant:하찮은, 중요하지 않은
  • maestro:거장, 명연주자
  • trickle:졸졸 흐르다
  • cumulative:누적되는

Questions

32.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① The sound of a single raindrop is too small to be heard.
  • ② Consistent, small efforts, though seemingly minor on their own, accumulate to create significant and powerful results over time.
  • ③ Great achievements are the result of many people working together.
  • ④ The power of nature is a good metaphor for the process of learning music.
  • ⑤ Patience is required to see the results of one's hard work.
251
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 33]

The Sculptor's Vision

Jason, a student of woodworking, became deeply frustrated. The figures he tried to carve never matched the grand visions in his imagination; his creations were clumsy and lifeless. Convinced he lacked any innate artistic talent, he was ready to abandon the craft. His mentor, a master carver, took a rough, gnarled piece of driftwood. He turned it over in his hands, studying its grain and contours. "I do not force a shape onto the wood," the mentor explained softly. "I listen to the wood and simply free the sculpture that is already waiting inside." Jason learned to work *with* the material's natural character, discovering that true artistry was a partnership, not a dictatorship.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • innate:타고난, 선천적인
  • abandon:포기하다
  • gnarled:울퉁불퉁하고 비틀린
  • driftwood:유목(물에 떠내려온 나무)
  • contour:윤곽
  • dictatorship:독재

Questions

33.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Every piece of wood contains a pre-existing sculpture.
  • ② The artist's role is to discover and reveal the inherent potential within a medium, rather than imposing a foreign idea upon it.
  • ③ One must have a clear vision before starting a creative project.
  • ④ The natural form of a material is always more beautiful than an artificial one.
  • ⑤ True talent involves listening to one's intuition.
252
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 34]

The Garden's Teaching

Sophie was consumed with envy for her neighbor's breathtakingly beautiful garden. She wanted the same spectacular results, and she wanted them immediately. She spared no expense, planting the most expensive flowers and using high-tech irrigation systems. But her garden remained a pale imitation. Her wise neighbor, observing her frantic efforts, offered a gentle piece of advice over the fence one day. "A garden is not built in a day, my dear. It is woven together with days." Sophie began to understand that a truly beautiful garden wasn't a project to be completed, but a relationship to be nurtured through daily, patient care and observation.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • breathtakingly:숨 막힐 정도로
  • spectacular:장관을 이루는, 극적인
  • irrigation:관개, 물을 대기
  • a pale imitation:서툰 모방, 아류
  • weave:(실 등을) 엮다, 짜다
  • nurture:양육하다, 가꾸다

Questions

34.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① It takes more than one day to build a garden.
  • ② Lasting and beautiful results are achieved through consistent, cumulative daily effort, not a single, intense burst of activity.
  • ③ Gardening is a process that unfolds over many seasons.
  • ④ The passage of time is the most important ingredient for a beautiful garden.
  • ⑤ One should focus on the process rather than the final product.
253
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 35]

The Candle's Wisdom

Peter, a brilliant but insecure analyst, was terrified of sharing his innovative ideas during team meetings. He harbored a deep-seated fear that his colleagues might steal his concepts and claim them as their own. This fear kept him silent and isolated, his potential locked away. His manager, a perceptive leader, noticed his reluctance to contribute. In a private coaching session, she told him, "A candle's flame is not diminished when it is used to light another. In fact, it creates more light." The simple metaphor illuminated Peter's fear for what it was: irrational. He realized sharing his ideas would not reduce his own worth, but rather amplify the collective intelligence of the team.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • insecure:자신이 없는, 불안정한
  • harbor:(생각, 감정 등을) 품다
  • perceptive:통찰력 있는
  • reluctance:꺼림, 주저
  • diminish:줄어들다, 약해지다
  • amplify:증폭시키다

Questions

35.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① A candle's flame can be shared without any loss to itself.
  • ② Sharing knowledge and ideas enhances the collective good without diminishing the individual's value.
  • ③ Collaboration leads to better outcomes than individual work.
  • ④ Fear of being copied should not prevent one from being creative.
  • ⑤ The purpose of a good idea is to be shared with others.
254
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 36]

The River's Journey

Amanda felt her life was in ruins. Her carefully constructed five-year plan had been completely derailed by an unexpected company downsizing. The career path she had meticulously mapped out had vanished overnight. Feeling lost and powerless, she confided in her therapist. The therapist listened to her story of frustration and then offered a perspective shift. "A river that encounters a boulder does not stop flowing," she said. "It simply finds a new path around it on its journey to the sea." Amanda began to see that her rigid plan was the problem, not the unexpected obstacle. She learned to value adaptability, eventually finding a more fulfilling career in a related but different field.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • derail:탈선시키다, 무산시키다
  • meticulously:꼼꼼하게
  • vanish:사라지다
  • boulder:큰 바위
  • adaptability:적응성, 융통성

Questions

36.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Rivers are powerful enough to overcome any obstacle in their path.
  • ② When faced with an insurmountable obstacle, one must find an alternative route to achieve one's ultimate goal.
  • ③ It is futile to resist the natural flow of life's events.
  • ④ Flexibility and resilience are more valuable than rigid planning.
  • ⑤ Every journey, like a river's, eventually reaches its destination.
255
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 37]

The Seed's Faith

Michael felt the immense weight of expectation as he started his first year of college. Coming from a low-income family, he was the first to pursue higher education, and the four-year journey to a degree seemed like an insurmountable mountain. He was overwhelmed by anxiety, constantly worrying about the final outcome. His academic advisor, sensing his distress, gave him a single acorn. "A seed does not need to see the whole forest to begin its journey of growth," she told him. Michael understood. He needed to stop worrying about the entire degree and focus on what was right in front of him: taking one class, reading one chapter, and passing one exam at a time.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • immense:엄청난
  • insurmountable:극복할 수 없는
  • overwhelmed:압도된
  • distress:고통, 괴로움
  • acorn:도토리

Questions

37.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① A seed can grow without being aware of the larger ecosystem.
  • ② One should begin a large undertaking by focusing on the immediate first steps, without being intimidated by the final goal.
  • ③ Great things often have very small and humble beginnings.
  • ④ Faith in oneself is essential for starting a long journey.
  • ⑤ The journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.
256
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 38]

The Mirror's Truth

Rachel lived in a state of constant anxiety, her self-worth entirely dependent on the approval of others. She meticulously curated her social media presence and altered her personality to fit what she thought her friends wanted to see. She felt exhausted from the performance. Her mentor, a wise woman who had known her for years, observed this behavior with concern. During a quiet conversation, she told Rachel, "You will never see your own true reflection if you are always looking in other people's mirrors." The statement struck Rachel with the force of truth. She realized she had to turn her gaze inward and cultivate a sense of self independent of external validation.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • dependent on:~에 의존하는
  • approval:인정, 승인
  • cultivate:경작하다, 함양하다
  • validation:확인, 인정

Questions

38.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① It is impossible to see oneself while looking at someone else.
  • ② Relying solely on the opinions of others for self-worth prevents one from discovering their authentic self.
  • ③ To truly understand yourself, you must spend time in solitude.
  • ④ Other people's perceptions of you are often distorted and unreliable.
  • ⑤ Self-awareness is the key to building genuine confidence.
257
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 39]

The Clock's Teaching

Nathan was a young entrepreneur, always rushing, perpetually stressed, and convinced that 24 hours in a day was a fundamental design flaw of the universe. He complained constantly about his lack of time. His grandfather, a retired craftsman who lived a remarkably peaceful and productive life, listened to his grandson's frantic complaints. He then said calmly, "The challenge is not to get more hours in your day, but to get more life in your hours." Nathan began to understand that the issue wasn't the quantity of time, but the quality of his attention and the wisdom of his priorities. He learned to make his hours count, rather than just counting them.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • entrepreneur:사업가
  • perpetually:끊임없이
  • fundamental:근본적인
  • flaw:결함
  • craftsman:장인
  • priorities:우선순위

Questions

39.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① The quality and meaningfulness of how time is spent are more important than the sheer quantity of time.
  • ② It is impossible to increase the number of hours in a day.
  • ③ A fulfilling life is measured by experiences, not by duration.
  • ④ Effective time management is about setting the right priorities.
  • ⑤ One should focus on living in the present moment.
258
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 40]

The Eagle's Flight

Christina was paralyzed by her fear of failure. She consistently chose safe, familiar options in her career and personal life, deliberately avoiding any challenge that carried the risk of defeat. Her coach, frustrated by her stagnant potential, tried a new approach to motivate her. He pointed to a documentary playing on a nearby screen, showing a mother eagle nudging her fledgling out of the nest. "An eagle does not learn to fly by studying the view from the nest," the coach said pointedly. Christina realized that true growth and mastery could only be achieved by leaving her comfort zone and embracing the very risks she feared.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • paralyzed:마비된
  • stagnant:침체된, 고여있는
  • motivate:동기를 부여하다
  • nudge:(팔꿈치로) 툭 찌르다, 살살 밀다
  • fledgling:갓 둥지를 떠난 어린 새
  • pointedly:날카롭게, 신랄하게

Questions

40.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① An eagle must leave its nest to learn how to fly.
  • ② Growth and learning require taking risks and moving beyond one's area of comfort and safety.
  • ③ Theoretical knowledge is useless without practical application.
  • ④ The nest represents a place of safety that ultimately hinders development.
  • ⑤ Courage is necessary to face new challenges.
259
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 41]

The Blacksmith's Fire

Oliver, a young apprentice blacksmith, complained incessantly about the searing heat of the forge and the grueling labor of hammering metal. He was convinced there must be an easier, more clever way to shape the steel. His master, a man of few words and powerful arms, listened to his complaints in silence. One day, he took a piece of untreated, brittle iron and a piece of finely tempered steel. He handed both to Oliver. "Steel that has never known the fire remains forever weak," the master stated simply. Oliver finally understood that the intense, difficult process he so despised was not a punishment, but the very thing that transformed weak iron into strong, valuable steel.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • apprentice:제자, 수습생
  • incessantly:끊임없이
  • searing:타는 듯한
  • forge:대장간, 용광로
  • grueling:녹초로 만드는
  • brittle:부서지기 쉬운
  • tempered:단련된, 강화된
  • despise:경멸하다

Questions

41.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Steel becomes strong only after being subjected to intense heat.
  • ② Hardship and difficult challenges are necessary to build strength and character.
  • ③ The process of blacksmithing is inherently difficult and requires great endurance.
  • ④ Weak materials cannot withstand the pressures of the forge.
  • ⑤ One must embrace difficult processes to create valuable things.
260
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 42]

The Teacher's Legacy

Ms. Park often felt a deep sense of futility teaching in an underfunded school with students facing immense challenges. Progress was slow, and appreciation was rare. She seriously contemplated leaving the profession, wondering if her tireless efforts were ultimately worthless. Years later, a former student, now a successful architect, visited her. He explained how her belief in him had been the foundation of his success. That evening, as she walked through the quiet school garden she had planted years ago, a thought crystallized in her mind: "The trees I water today will one day give shade to children I may never meet." This realization renewed her sense of purpose, anchoring her once more to her calling.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • futility:무익함, 헛됨
  • underfunded:재정 지원이 부족한
  • contemplate:고려하다, 생각하다
  • worthless:가치 없는
  • crystallize:구체화되다, 명확해지다
  • anchor:닻을 내리다, 고정시키다
  • calling:소명, 천직

Questions

42.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Planting trees is a long-term investment for the future.
  • ② The positive influence of one's current efforts can have far-reaching, unseen benefits for future generations.
  • ③ A teacher's job is to care for students, much like a gardener cares for trees.
  • ④ The full impact of education is often not realized by the teacher herself.
  • ⑤ One should not expect immediate results from their hard work.
261
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 43]

The Photographer's Lens

Julia, an amateur photographer, was perpetually frustrated. The images she captured with her camera never seemed to match the breathtaking beauty of the scenery she saw with her own eyes. She blamed her entry-level equipment, convinced that an expensive, professional-grade camera was the key to unlocking her potential. Her seasoned instructor, however, showed her a stunning portfolio of photos all taken with a simple smartphone. He then tapped his own temple and said, "The most important part of the camera is the twelve inches behind it." Julia learned that vision, creativity, and the ability to see light and composition were far more critical than the price tag of her gear.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • amateur:아마추어, 비전문가
  • perpetually:끊임없이
  • entry-level:초급자용의
  • seasoned:노련한, 경험 많은
  • portfolio:포트폴리오, 작품집
  • composition:구성, 구도
  • gear:장비

Questions

43.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① The back part of a camera is its most critical component.
  • ② The photographer's skill, vision, and creativity are more important than the quality of the equipment.
  • ③ A good photographer can take great pictures with any camera.
  • ④ One should invest in education and training rather than expensive gear.
  • ⑤ The distance between the photographer and the subject is crucial.
262
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 44]

The Baker's Patience

Marcus poured his life savings into opening his dream bakery. He was a talented baker, but an impatient businessman. He expected customers to be lining up from the very first day. When business was initially slow, a deep anxiety began to set in, and he considered cutting corners or even giving up. His mentor, a retired baker with flour dust seemingly etched into his skin, visited the quiet shop. He watched Marcus pace nervously, then said calmly, "You cannot command the bread to rise; you can only give it the warmth and time it needs." Marcus learned that building a loyal customer base, like baking good bread, required patience and consistent quality, not frantic commands.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • impatient:성급한
  • cut corners:(일을 쉽게 하려고) 절차를 무시하다, 대충하다
  • etch:새기다, 에칭하다
  • pace:서성거리다
  • command:명령하다

Questions

44.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① The process of bread rising is controlled by natural factors like time and temperature.
  • ② Certain processes cannot be rushed and require patience and the right conditions to unfold naturally.
  • ③ A successful business grows organically over time.
  • ④ One should not try to force results but rather create an environment where success can grow.
  • ⑤ Patience is the most important virtue for a baker and a businessman.
263
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 45]

The Mountain Climber's Wisdom

Sarah, on her first major mountain expedition, found herself constantly staring up at the distant, intimidating summit. The sheer scale of the remaining climb felt overwhelming, and a sense of hopelessness began to creep in. She felt her resolve wavering and was tempted to turn back. Her experienced guide, noticing her gaze fixed on the peak and her slowing pace, came to her side. He didn't offer words of encouragement about the summit. Instead, he pointed to the patch of rock directly in front of her boots. "Do not try to climb the mountain," he advised. "Simply climb the step in front of you." Sarah shifted her focus, and with each conquered step, she found the strength to continue.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • expedition:원정, 탐험
  • intimidating:위협적인, 겁을 주는
  • summit:정상
  • sheer scale:엄청난 규모
  • waver:흔들리다, 약해지다

Questions

45.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① One should focus on the immediate, manageable task at hand rather than being overwhelmed by the larger goal.
  • ② Mountain climbing is a step-by-step process.
  • ③ It is more practical to focus on the journey than the destination.
  • ④ Large goals can be broken down into smaller, more achievable parts.
  • ⑤ Looking too far ahead can lead to discouragement and failure.
264
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 46]

The Writer's Block

For months, Emma had been trapped in the paralyzing grip of writer's block. She would sit at her desk for hours, but every sentence she managed to write seemed clumsy and inadequate. She would delete everything in frustration, leaving behind a glaringly blank page. Convinced her talent had vanished, she was ready to abandon her novel. Her writing coach, after listening to her despair, offered a blunt but transformative piece of advice. "You can't edit a blank page," he said. Emma realized her quest for perfection was preventing her from even starting. She needed to allow herself to write an imperfect first draft, a messy foundation that she could later shape and refine.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • paralyzing:마비시키는
  • inadequate:부적절한, 불충분한
  • glaringly:눈에 띄게, 확연히
  • transformative:변화시키는
  • refine:다듬다, 정제하다

Questions

46.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① It is impossible to edit a page that has no text on it.
  • ② To improve something, one must first create something, even if it is imperfect.
  • ③ The fear of imperfection is the greatest obstacle to creativity.
  • ④ The writing process involves multiple stages, including drafting and editing.
  • ⑤ It is better to have a flawed draft than no draft at all.
265
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 47]

The Gardener's Philosophy

Robert was consumed by a friendly but intense rivalry with his neighbor, whose roses were always the talk of the neighborhood. He tried every technique, every exotic fertilizer, and every pruning method he could read about, yet his roses remained merely average. One evening, he finally asked his neighbor for his ultimate secret. The neighbor, a man with soil permanently under his fingernails, smiled. "The secret ingredient isn't in the soil or the water," he confided. "It's in the shadow the gardener casts upon the plants each day." Robert understood. The secret wasn't a product or a technique, but the consistent, loving presence and devotion of the gardener himself.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • rivalry:경쟁
  • exotic:외래의, 이국적인
  • pruning:가지치기
  • confide:(비밀을) 털어놓다
  • devotion:헌신

Questions

47.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① The gardener's shadow provides necessary shade for the roses.
  • ② The most critical factor for success is the gardener's consistent, personal attention and care.
  • ③ The best gardening secrets are not written in books.
  • ④ Passion and love are more important than any specific technique.
  • ⑤ Success comes from daily, dedicated practice.
266
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 48]

The Musician's Journey

Kevin, a diligent piano student, practiced for hours every day. Yet, he often felt a crushing sense of inadequacy whenever he listened to recordings of professional musicians or attended concerts. The gap between his current ability and their mastery seemed impossibly wide, and he wondered if he could ever be "good enough." He expressed these frustrations to his teacher. His teacher listened patiently, then said, "Do not compare your chapter one to someone else's chapter twenty." The analogy resonated deeply with Kevin. He realized that every master was once a beginner, and he needed to focus on his own unique journey and progress, not on an unfair comparison.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • diligent:근면한, 성실한
  • inadequacy:부적절함, 무능함
  • mastery:숙달, 통달
  • resonate:공명하다, 울려 퍼지다

Questions

48.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① It is illogical to compare one's beginning stages with someone else's advanced stage.
  • ② Everyone's journey of learning and development is different and proceeds at its own pace.
  • ③ Comparing oneself to others is often a source of unnecessary frustration and discouragement.
  • ④ One should focus on their own progress rather than on the achievements of others.
  • ⑤ The first chapter of a book is fundamentally different from the last.
267
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 49]

The Lighthouse Keeper's Duty

George kept the lighthouse on a remote, windswept island. Weeks would go by with only a few ships passing in the distant sea lanes. He often felt a profound sense of loneliness, questioning if his solitary, repetitive work truly mattered in an age of GPS. During a hurricane of historic intensity, the island's power failed, but George kept the light's massive lens turning by hand all night. The next morning, a grateful coast guard captain radioed his thanks; the light had been the only beacon for a rescue boat in the storm. George's friend later remarked, "Your light may not be needed by every ship, but for the one that is lost in the dark, it is everything."

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • windswept:바람이 휘몰아치는
  • solitary:고독한
  • repetitive:반복적인
  • beacon:신호등, 봉화

Questions

49.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① The lighthouse's primary function is to help ships that are in trouble.
  • ② The value of one's work should not be measured by its frequency, but by its impact in critical moments.
  • ③ Even a single act of service can have a profound and life-saving impact.
  • ④ One's duty may seem insignificant most of the time but can become crucial unexpectedly.
  • ⑤ It is important to be prepared for emergencies.
268
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 50]

The Artist's Canvas

Lisa was a talented painter but a crippling perfectionist. She would abandon a canvas if a single brushstroke felt out of place or a color was not the exact shade she had envisioned. Consequently, her studio was a graveyard of unfinished works, and she felt deeply unproductive. Her art teacher, observing her struggle, picked up one of the "failed" paintings. He then took a brush and made a bold, seemingly random stroke across it. "Sometimes," he said, "the most beautiful accidents happen when we stop trying to control every single outcome." Lisa learned to embrace the unexpected, finding that surrendering a little control could open the door to a new, more dynamic form of creativity.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • crippling:심각한 손상을 주는, 마비시키는
  • envision:마음속에 그리다, 상상하다
  • graveyard:묘지
  • unproductive:비생산적인
  • surrender:포기하다, 넘겨주다
  • dynamic:역동적인

Questions

50.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Accidents can sometimes improve a work of art.
  • ② Relinquishing absolute control can lead to unexpected and valuable creative discoveries.
  • ③ Perfectionism is a major obstacle to artistic productivity.
  • ④ True creativity involves an element of randomness and chance.
  • ⑤ It is important to be open to making mistakes in the creative process.
269
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 51]

The Mason's Stone

David, an apprentice mason, was growing disheartened. No matter how carefully he cut his stones, they never seemed to fit together as perfectly as his master's work. His walls looked rough and amateurish. Convinced he was simply not suited for the trade, he told his master he wanted to quit. The master mason picked up an irregularly shaped, seemingly flawed stone that David had discarded. He expertly fitted it into a gap in the wall, where it sat perfectly. "Every stone has its place in the wall," the master said, "even the ones that appear broken." David learned that perceived imperfections could, with skill, contribute to the overall strength and character of the whole.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • apprentice:제자, 수습생
  • mason:석공
  • disheartened:낙담한
  • amateurish:비전문적인, 서투른
  • irregularly:불규칙적으로
  • flawed:결함이 있는
  • perceived:인식된, 인지된

Questions

51.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Broken stones can still be used in construction.
  • ② Every individual, despite their perceived flaws or differences, has a valuable role to play within a larger community or structure.
  • ③ A skilled mason can make any stone fit perfectly.
  • ④ Imperfections should be embraced as they add unique character.
  • ⑤ The strength of a wall depends on using a variety of stones.
270
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 52]

The Runner's Pace

Michelle was training for her first marathon and became obsessed with the times of professional runners, which she followed online. The vast gap between their pace and her own left her feeling deeply discouraged and inadequate, making her question why she was even trying. Her coach noticed her dwindling motivation and the logbook where she compared her times to the elites. He sat her down and pointed to her own previous entries. "You are not racing against them," he stated firmly. "You are only racing against the person you were yesterday." This shift in perspective freed Michelle from the trap of comparison, allowing her to find joy and motivation in her own personal improvement.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • obsessed with:~에 집착하는
  • inadequate:부적절한, 무능한
  • dwindling:(점점) 줄어드는
  • logbook:일지, 기록부
  • elite:엘리트, 정예

Questions

52.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① The true measure of progress is self-improvement, not comparison with others.
  • ② Marathon running is primarily an individual sport.
  • ③ One should aim to beat their own personal records.
  • ④ Comparing oneself to professionals is an unrealistic and demotivating practice.
  • ⑤ Focusing on the past can help motivate future performance.
271
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 53]

The Painter's Mistake

Alex was meticulously painting a serene landscape when his hand slipped, leaving a large, ugly smudge of dark paint right across the tranquil sky. His heart sank. The entire composition was ruined. He was about to discard the canvas in disgust when his art teacher intervened. She studied the smudge for a long moment, a thoughtful expression on her face. "Sometimes, what we call a mistake," she mused, "is simply an opportunity in disguise." Inspired by her words, Alex didn't try to cover the smudge. Instead, he transformed it into the heart of a dramatic, swirling storm cloud, creating a painting far more powerful and dynamic than his original, placid vision.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • meticulously:꼼꼼하게
  • serene:고요한, 평화로운
  • smudge:얼룩
  • tranquil:고요한
  • intervene:개입하다, 끼어들다
  • in disguise:변장한, 위장한
  • placid:차분한, 잔잔한

Questions

53.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① An apparent mistake or accident can be transformed into a positive and creative element.
  • ② Great art often arises from unplanned accidents.
  • ③ It is important not to be discouraged by one's errors.
  • ④ A true artist can find a use for any mistake.
  • ⑤ A change in perspective can turn a problem into a solution.
272
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 54]

The Farmer's Harvest

Having inherited his family's farm, James was driven by a desire for quick profits. He ignored the time-honored traditions of soil preparation, opting instead for fast-growing crops and heavy chemical fertilizers. He believed modern shortcuts could bypass the slow rhythms of nature. When his harvest was meager and the soil was left depleted, he blamed the unpredictable weather. An old, experienced farmer from the next property, having observed James's methods all season, offered a quiet piece of wisdom. "You cannot hope to harvest in the autumn what you did not sow in the spring." James realized his failure wasn't due to bad luck, but to his own lack of foundational work and patience.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • time-honored:오랜 세월에 걸쳐 존중받아 온, 전통적인
  • opt for:~을 선택하다
  • bypass:우회하다, 건너뛰다
  • meager:빈약한, 불충분한
  • depleted:고갈된
  • sow:(씨를) 뿌리다

Questions

54.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① One must plant seeds in the spring to have a harvest in the autumn.
  • ② Desired outcomes cannot be achieved without first putting in the necessary foundational effort and preparation.
  • ③ Shortcuts and quick fixes often lead to poor long-term results.
  • ④ One should not blame external factors for failures caused by one's own actions.
  • ⑤ Success is a direct result of the work that is invested.
273
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 55]

The Sculptor's Patience

Rosa was sculpting her first life-sized statue and was consumed by an overwhelming desire to see it finished. This impatience made her rush through crucial stages, leading to several small but significant errors in proportion and detail. Her instructor, a master sculptor renowned for his meticulous work, watched her frantic efforts with a calm demeanor. He didn't criticize her mistakes directly. Instead, he handed her a very fine, delicate chisel. "In our craft," he said, "patience is the sharpest tool." Rosa slowed down, understanding that haste was dulling her skills, and that careful, deliberate work was the only way to achieve the quality she desired.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • life-sized:실물 크기의
  • proportion:비율, 균형
  • renowned:유명한, 명성 있는
  • meticulous:꼼꼼한, 세심한
  • demeanor:태도, 품행
  • haste:서두름, 성급함
  • deliberate:신중한, 의도적인

Questions

55.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① A sculptor's most important tool is a sharp chisel.
  • ② Patience is the most essential quality for achieving high-quality, detailed work in a craft.
  • ③ Rushing through a project inevitably leads to errors.
  • ④ The quality of one's tools can affect the quality of one's work.
  • ⑤ True mastery requires not just skill, but also the right temperament.
274
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 56]

The Teacher's Impact

Mrs. Lee taught in a challenging, low-income neighborhood where progress was often slow and setbacks were frequent. She poured her heart into her work, but often felt her efforts were like stones dropped into a bottomless well—unnoticed and unappreciated. After many years, feeling burnt out, she considered a career change. That week, she received a flood of emails and letters, organized by a former student for Teacher Appreciation Week. They were from students she had taught decades ago, now doctors, artists, and community leaders, all citing her influence as a turning point. As she read them, she finally understood: "A teacher's influence does not stop at the classroom door; it echoes in eternity."

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • setback:차질, 좌절
  • bottomless well:밑 빠진 독
  • burnt out:소진된, 지친
  • turning point:전환점
  • eternity:영원

Questions

56.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① The lessons learned in a classroom are remembered for a lifetime.
  • ② The positive impact of a teacher is profound and continues to affect students long after they have left school.
  • ③ A good teacher's reputation can last forever.
  • ④ The true value of education is immeasurable and timeless.
  • ⑤ A teacher's work has consequences that extend far into the future.
275
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 57]

The Gardener's Vision

Tom planted his first garden with a perfect, orderly picture from a magazine in his mind. When the actual garden began to grow, it seemed chaotic and messy, nothing like his idealized vision. The plants grew at different rates, and weeds popped up in unexpected places. Deeply disappointed, he complained to his experienced neighbor. The neighbor looked over the thriving, if somewhat unruly, patch of green. "A garden is not a static painting," he chuckled. "It is a slow-motion dance, choreographed by the seasons." Tom learned to let go of his rigid expectations and began to appreciate the dynamic, ever-changing beauty of a living garden.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • orderly:정돈된, 질서 있는
  • idealized:이상화된
  • chaotic:혼란스러운
  • unruly:제멋대로 구는, 다루기 힘든
  • static:고정된, 정적인
  • choreograph:안무를 짜다
  • dynamic:역동적인

Questions

57.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① A garden's appearance changes with the seasons.
  • ② One should embrace the natural, dynamic, and sometimes unpredictable process of growth rather than imposing rigid expectations.
  • ③ A real garden can never be as perfect as a picture.
  • ④ The beauty of a garden lies in its constant transformation.
  • ⑤ Patience is required to see a garden develop over time.
276
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 58]

The Writer's Voice

Emma deeply admired the works of several famous authors and tried to emulate their sophisticated styles in her own writing. However, her stories always felt artificial and forced, lacking a genuine core. She grew frustrated, feeling that her own voice was simply not good enough. Her writing mentor read one of her derivative pieces and offered a crucial insight. "You are trying to sing in someone else's voice," he told her. "But the world is waiting to hear the song that only you can sing." Emma stopped trying to imitate others and began the difficult but rewarding journey of discovering and developing her own unique narrative style.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • emulate:모방하다, 따라가다
  • sophisticated:세련된, 정교한
  • artificial:인위적인
  • forced:억지스러운
  • derivative:파생적인, 모방한
  • narrative:서사, 이야기

Questions

58.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① It is impossible to perfectly imitate another author's style.
  • ② True creativity and value lie in expressing one's own unique perspective and style, not in copying others.
  • ③ The world values originality more than imitation.
  • ④ Finding one's own voice is the most important goal for a writer.
  • ⑤ One should be confident in their own abilities.
277
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 59]

The Fisherman's Wisdom

Lucas was an impatient fisherman, constantly fidgeting with his gear and moving his boat every few minutes, convinced that constant action would yield results. His grandfather, who had been fishing the same lake for fifty years, watched his grandson's restless energy with a serene smile. The old man hadn't moved for hours, yet his bucket was half-full. "The fish do not come to the thrashing water," he finally said, his voice as calm as the lake's deep center. "They come to the quiet line." Lucas learned that in fishing, as in many pursuits, a state of calm focus and patience is often more effective than restless, frantic activity.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • fidget:꼼지락거리다
  • yield:산출하다
  • serene:고요한, 평화로운
  • thrashing:요동치는
  • pursuit:일, 활동, 추구

Questions

59.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Fish are scared away by loud noises and sudden movements.
  • ② A calm, patient, and focused approach is more likely to lead to success than anxious, constant activity.
  • ③ To be a good fisherman, one must learn to be still and quiet.
  • ④ Over-eagerness can be counterproductive.
  • ⑤ Wisdom comes from years of experience.
278
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 60]

The Clock Maker's Craft

Peter, a young apprentice to a master clockmaker, was frustrated by the painstaking, intricate details required for the craft. He saw the tiny gears and springs as tedious obstacles and often tried to skip steps to finish a timepiece faster. His master, after examining a clock Peter had hastily assembled, pointed to its erratic ticking. "Time itself is our greatest teacher," the master said gravely. "It teaches us that precision cannot be rushed." Peter realized that the very nature of what he was trying to measure—time—demanded a slow, deliberate, and precise approach. Quality, he learned, was a function of patience.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • painstaking:공들인, 힘든
  • intricate:복잡한
  • tedious:지루한, 싫증 나는
  • hastily:성급하게, 서둘러서
  • erratic:불규칙한, 변덕스러운
  • gravely:엄숙하게, 중대하게
  • precision:정확성, 정밀함

Questions

60.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① The concept of time demonstrates that accuracy requires a slow and careful process.
  • ② High-quality, precise work cannot be achieved through haste and shortcuts.
  • ③ A good clockmaker must have a deep respect for time.
  • ④ The process of creating something should reflect the nature of the thing being created.
  • ⑤ Patience and attention to detail are the keys to mastery in any craft.
279
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 61]

The Conductor's Orchestra

Ryan, a talented violinist, recently joined the prestigious school orchestra. However, he quickly grew frustrated because, amidst the thunder of the brass and the swell of the strings, he could barely hear his own individual instrument. He felt his contribution was insignificant, lost in the massive soundscape. Considering quitting, he confided his feelings to the conductor. The wise conductor listened, then tapped his baton for silence at the next rehearsal. "A symphony is not one instrument playing a magnificent solo," he announced to the entire orchestra. "It is the magic of one hundred instruments playing as one." Ryan realized that true harmony wasn't about being heard individually, but about contributing perfectly to the whole.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • prestigious:명망 있는, 일류의
  • swell:(소리, 감정이) 부풀어 오름, 커짐
  • soundscape:음향 경관
  • magnificent:장엄한, 훌륭한
  • harmony:조화

Questions

61.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① The loudest instrument is not the most important in an orchestra.
  • ② Collective harmony and collaboration are more valuable than individual prominence.
  • ③ A symphony requires a large number of musicians to be successful.
  • ④ It is more difficult to play together than to play alone.
  • ⑤ Each musician must listen carefully to the conductor's direction.
280
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 62]

The Jeweler's Gem

On a beachcombing trip, Kate found a dull, rough-looking stone and was about to toss it back into the waves. Her grandmother, a retired jeweler with a keen eye, stopped her. She took the unremarkable stone, turned it over in the sunlight, and felt its texture. "The most precious diamond," she said with a knowing smile, "spends a million years looking like an ordinary rock. It only reveals its fire when it meets the right hands." Intrigued, Kate took the stone to a local lapidary. After careful cutting and polishing, the "ordinary rock" was revealed to be a stunning, semi-precious agate, which became Kate's most treasured possession.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • beachcombing:해변에서 표류물 등을 줍기
  • toss:(가볍게) 던지다
  • keen:예리한, 날카로운
  • unremarkable:평범한, 특별할 것 없는
  • lapidary:보석 세공인
  • agate:마노 (보석의 일종)
  • treasured:소중한

Questions

62.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Diamonds are geologically indistinguishable from common rocks in their raw state.
  • ② Hidden potential and value can be revealed through proper skill, effort, and recognition.
  • ③ It takes a trained expert to identify something truly valuable.
  • ④ The true worth of an object is not always apparent on the surface.
  • ⑤ Patience and time are required to create something of great value.
281
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 63]

The Chef's Recipe

Daniel, an ambitious culinary student, dreamed of becoming a celebrity chef overnight. He constantly tried to replicate the most complex and flamboyant dishes from famous television chefs, but his attempts were consistently disastrous. Discouraged and frustrated, he presented his latest failed creation to his instructor. The seasoned chef tasted the dish, sighed, and then offered a piece of fundamental advice. "You are trying to build the roof," he said, "before you have laid the foundation." Humbled, Daniel went back to mastering the basic skills—knife work, sauce making, and heat control—gradually building the foundation upon which he could later construct his culinary masterpieces.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • ambitious:야심 있는
  • replicate:복제하다, 모사하다
  • flamboyant:화려한, 현란한
  • disastrous:처참한, 형편없는
  • seasoned:노련한, 경험 많은
  • culinary:요리의

Questions

63.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① One is attempting advanced techniques without having mastered the fundamental skills.
  • ② It is illogical to build the roof of a house before the foundation.
  • ③ Ambitious goals require a solid and well-planned base.
  • ④ Basic skills are more important than complex recipes in cooking.
  • ⑤ Success in any field must be built step-by-step.
282
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 64]

The Builder's Foundation

Mark was excited to build a large treehouse and impatiently ignored his father's detailed advice about laying a strong, level foundation. He wanted to get to the "fun part"—building the walls and the roof—as quickly as possible. He rushed through the foundational work, eager to see his structure take shape. For a week, it looked impressive. Then, after a heavy rainstorm, the entire structure tilted and collapsed. His father, surveying the wreckage, put a hand on his son's shoulder. "A building is only as strong as the parts you cannot see," he said quietly. Mark learned a hard lesson about the critical importance of the unseen, foundational work that supports everything else.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • impatiently:성급하게
  • level:평평한
  • structure:구조물
  • tilt:기울다
  • collapse:무너지다
  • wreckage:잔해

Questions

64.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① The strength of a building depends on its hidden foundation.
  • ② The unseen, foundational elements of any endeavor are the most critical for its long-term stability and success.
  • ③ One should not judge things based on their outward appearance.
  • ④ Proper preparation is essential to withstand unexpected challenges.
  • ⑤ The most important work is often the least visible and exciting.
283
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 65]

The Navigator's Compass

Sophie, a novice hiking guide, was leading a group through a dense forest. Lacking confidence, she kept second-guessing her decisions. Whenever she encountered a steep hill or a muddy patch, she would change her intended route, hoping to find an easier way. The group became confused, exhausted, and demoralized by the constant changes in direction. An experienced hiker in the group finally approached her privately. "A compass that points in every direction," he said gently but firmly, "is the same as having no compass at all." Sophie understood. She needed to commit to a clear path and navigate obstacles, not abandon her course at the first sign of difficulty.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • novice:초심자
  • second-guess:(사후에) 비판하다, 의심하다
  • demoralized:사기가 저하된
  • navigate:길을 찾다, 항해하다
  • obstacle:장애물

Questions

65.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① A broken compass is useless for navigation.
  • ② A lack of consistent direction and commitment renders any plan or guidance ineffective.
  • ③ It is better to face challenges head-on than to constantly try to avoid them.
  • ④ A good leader must be decisive and confident.
  • ⑤ Constantly changing one's mind leads to confusion and failure.
284
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 66]

The Doctor's Patience

Jennifer, a first-year medical student, was deeply impatient. She was frustrated by the endless hours of studying dense textbooks on biochemistry and anatomy. She wanted to be in the hospital, helping patients, not memorizing abstract theories. She complained to her professor, a renowned physician, that the theoretical study seemed disconnected from the practical reality of healing. The professor listened, then looked at her over his spectacles. "You cannot hope to heal a body," he said with quiet authority, "whose language you do not yet understand." Jennifer realized that the "boring" theories were the very language of the human body, and a thorough understanding was the non-negotiable prerequisite to responsible healing.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • biochemistry:생화학
  • anatomy:해부학
  • abstract:추상적인
  • renowned:유명한, 명성 있는
  • non-negotiable:협상 불가능한
  • prerequisite:전제 조건

Questions

66.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① To heal a body, one must first learn the language of medicine.
  • ② A deep and thorough understanding of fundamental principles is essential before one can effectively apply practical skills.
  • ③ Theoretical knowledge and practical skills are two sides of the same coin.
  • ④ Impatience can be a dangerous quality in a medical professional.
  • ⑤ One must not attempt to solve a problem without fully understanding its nature.
285
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 67]

The Dancer's Grace

Ashley, a talented but undisciplined ballet student, focused solely on the most dramatic and impressive moves—the high leaps and rapid spins. She neglected the tedious but essential barre exercises and basic positions. While her performances were momentarily spectacular, they lacked control and felt awkward. Her instructor, a former prima ballerina, watched her practice one day. "My dear, you are chasing the thunder," she said, "but grace lives in the quiet rain that follows." Ashley began to understand that true elegance and mastery in dance came not from the explosive, attention-grabbing moments, but from the quiet, controlled precision that connected them.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • undisciplined:훈련되지 않은, 제멋대로의
  • neglect:소홀히 하다
  • tedious:지루한
  • prima ballerina:주역 발레리나
  • precision:정확성, 정밀함

Questions

67.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Quiet moments are more beautiful than loud ones.
  • ② True artistry and grace are found in subtle control and fundamental skill, not just in dramatic, showy displays.
  • ③ One should pay more attention to the basics than to advanced techniques.
  • ④ A great performance requires a balance of both dramatic and quiet moments.
  • ⑤ The most impressive skills are often the most difficult to master.
286
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 68]

The Librarian's Knowledge

Chris, a university student, believed the library was an obsolete institution in the age of instant online searches. For a major thesis paper, however, he found himself drowning in a sea of unreliable blogs, biased articles, and contradictory information online. Frustrated, he finally visited the university library. The subject librarian, a quiet but knowledgeable woman, helped him navigate scholarly databases and locate primary sources within an hour. As she handed him a stack of academic journals, she remarked, "The internet gives you a million answers, but a library helps you ask the right questions." Chris discovered that the true challenge wasn't finding information, but discerning its quality and relevance.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • obsolete:시대에 뒤떨어진, 구식의
  • thesis:학위 논문
  • contradictory:모순되는
  • scholarly:학문적인
  • primary source:1차 자료
  • discern:알아차리다, 파악하다

Questions

68.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Libraries provide better quality answers than the internet.
  • ② The value of a library lies in guiding users toward critical thinking and meaningful inquiry, not just providing raw information.
  • ③ Asking the right questions is more important than finding answers.
  • ④ One should use library resources to verify information found online.
  • ⑤ The internet is a source of quantity, while the library is a source of quality.
287
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 69]

The Mechanic's Tools

Jordan was trying to restore his first classic car and constantly blamed his failures on his basic, inexpensive set of tools. He was convinced that if he only had the professional-grade equipment he saw on TV, all his problems would be solved. He finally saved up and bought a top-of-the-line toolkit. To his dismay, he continued to struggle, now making expensive mistakes with his fancy new tools. His mentor, an old mechanic who could seemingly fix anything with a wrench and a screwdriver, watched him with a wry smile. "It is a poor craftsman," he said, "who blames his tools." Jordan realized that the fault was not in his equipment, but in his own lack of skill and patience.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • restore:복원하다
  • blame:탓하다
  • top-of-the-line:최고급의
  • dismay:실망, 경악
  • wry:비꼬는, 풍자적인
  • craftsman:장인

Questions

69.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① A good craftsman can work effectively even with simple tools.
  • ② One should take responsibility for their own lack of skill instead of blaming external factors.
  • ③ The quality of the tools does not determine the quality of the work.
  • ④ Expensive equipment cannot compensate for a lack of skill.
  • ⑤ True skill is more valuable than any piece of equipment.
288
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 70]

The Student's Question

Amy was terrified of asking questions in her advanced physics class. She feared her classmates would judge her for not understanding, and that the professor would think she wasn't paying attention. So, she struggled in silence, her confusion growing with each new, complex concept. The professor, a perceptive educator, noticed her furrowed brow and hesitant posture. After class, he spoke to her privately. "A locked door remains locked forever," he said. "But a question is a key that can open it." Amy understood that her silence was the wall, and asking a question wasn't a sign of weakness, but an act of courage to seek understanding.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • physics:물리학
  • judge:판단하다
  • concept:개념
  • perceptive:통찰력 있는
  • furrowed brow:찌푸린 이마
  • hesitant:주저하는

Questions

70.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① A locked door can only be opened with a key.
  • ② Asking questions is the essential act that unlocks understanding and overcomes the barrier of ignorance.
  • ③ One should not be afraid to admit when they do not understand something.
  • ④ Silence leads to continued confusion, while inquiry leads to knowledge.
  • ⑤ The courage to ask a question is the first step toward learning.
289
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 71]

The Photographer's Light

Ben, a budding photographer, was perpetually frustrated with his indoor portraits. Despite his expensive camera and lenses, the photos always turned out dark and lifeless. He complained that he needed a professional studio lighting kit. His mentor, a veteran photojournalist, simply took him to a room with a single large window. She positioned the subject near the window, using a simple white reflector to bounce the natural light. The resulting portrait was luminous and full of life. "You are trying to buy light," she told him, "when your only job is to find it." Ben learned that observation and the creative use of available resources were far more powerful than expensive equipment.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • budding:신진의, 싹트기 시작하는
  • portrait:초상화, 인물 사진
  • lifeless:생기 없는
  • reflector:반사판
  • luminous:빛나는, 아주 선명한
  • available:이용 가능한

Questions

71.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Professional lighting equipment is not necessary for good photography.
  • ② Creativity and resourcefulness in using existing conditions are more important than acquiring expensive tools.
  • ③ Natural light is always superior to artificial light for portraits.
  • ④ The primary skill of a photographer is the ability to find good light.
  • ⑤ One should not spend money on things that can be found for free.
290
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 72]

The Gardener's Seasons

Maria planted a diverse flower garden in the spring but soon grew anxious. Some seeds sprouted almost overnight, while others showed no signs of life for weeks. She was tempted to dig up the "lazy" seeds, convinced they were duds. Her experienced neighbor, observing her worry, offered a piece of horticultural wisdom. "You cannot expect the tulips of April to bloom with the roses of June," she said. "Each has its own perfect time." Maria learned to trust the unique rhythm of each plant, understanding that growth and blooming happen on different schedules, and patience was key to seeing the full beauty of her garden unfold.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • diverse:다양한
  • sprout:싹이 트다
  • dud:불발탄, 실패작
  • horticultural:원예의
  • unfold:펼쳐지다

Questions

72.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Different types of flowers bloom in different seasons.
  • ② Individuals, like plants, develop and reach their potential at different times and should not be judged by a single timeline.
  • ③ One must be knowledgeable about the specific needs of each plant.
  • ④ Patience is essential for a gardener to see the results of their work.
  • ⑤ It is a mistake to compare the progress of different individuals.
291
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 73]

The Musician's Silence

Carlos, a gifted young jazz pianist, had a tendency to fill every possible moment of his solos with a flurry of notes. He believed that more notes equaled more impressive music. His performances, while technically dazzling, often felt cluttered and exhausting to the listener. His instructor, a legendary jazz veteran, listened to one of his breathless solos. He then sat down at the piano and played a simple, sparse melody. "The notes are only half the music," the instructor said. "The other half lives in the silence between them." Carlos began to understand that pauses, space, and restraint were not empty moments, but powerful musical tools that gave the notes meaning.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • flurry:질풍, 소나기
  • dazzling:눈부신, 현혹적인
  • cluttered:어수선한
  • sparse:드문드문한, 희박한
  • restraint:자제, 억제

Questions

73.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① A musical piece is composed of both notes and rests.
  • ② In art and communication, deliberate pauses and empty space are as meaningful as the content itself.
  • ③ Using fewer notes can sometimes be more effective than using many.
  • ④ The true skill of a musician is knowing when not to play.
  • ⑤ Overwhelming the audience with too much information can be counterproductive.
292
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 74]

The Writer's Draft

Elena had just finished the first draft of her novel, and she was devastated. The story on the page was a clumsy, tangled mess, a pale shadow of the magnificent, perfect vision she held in her mind. She felt like a failure and considered abandoning the project entirely. She tearfully showed the draft to her creative writing professor. He read a few pages, then smiled kindly. "Don't worry," he said. "The first draft is just you telling yourself the story. Now, your real work begins." Elena realized that the initial draft wasn't meant to be the final product, but merely the raw material from which the real story could be sculpted through revision.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • devastated:엄청난 충격을 받은, 망연자실한
  • tangled:얽힌, 복잡한
  • magnificent:장엄한, 훌륭한
  • sculpt:조각하다
  • revision:수정, 개정

Questions

74.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① The first draft is a personal and private stage of the writing process.
  • ② The initial version of a creative work is meant to be an exploratory and imperfect foundation, not the finished piece.
  • ③ The real work of writing is in the editing and revision process, not the initial creation.
  • ④ One should not be discouraged by the poor quality of a first draft.
  • ⑤ Writing is a process of discovering the story for oneself first.
293
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 75]

The Sailor's Anchor

Jake owned a beautiful sailboat and dreamed of epic voyages to distant lands. Yet, year after year, his boat remained safely moored in the harbor. He always had an excuse: the weather wasn't perfect, he needed more supplies, he had to do more research. His sailing instructor, seeing through the excuses, pointed to the heavy anchor on the deck. "That anchor keeps your ship safe from the storm," he said. "But it also keeps it from discovering new oceans." Jake realized that the very thing he thought was keeping him safe—his cautious preparation and fear of the unknown—had become the anchor that was holding him back from his dreams.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • epic:서사시의, 장대한
  • voyage:항해
  • moored:정박한
  • harbor:항구

Questions

75.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① An anchor has both a positive function (safety) and a negative function (restriction).
  • ② Things that provide safety and security can also become obstacles that prevent growth and new experiences.
  • ③ One must be willing to give up safety to achieve their dreams.
  • ④ Excessive preparation and caution can be a form of self-sabotage.
  • ⑤ Fear of the unknown is the greatest barrier to adventure.
294
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 76]

The Baker's Ingredient

Lily was a technically proficient baker. She followed recipes with scientific precision, measuring every ingredient to the milligram. Yet, her cakes and pastries, while perfectly formed, always tasted bland and lacked a certain warmth. Frustrated, she asked her grandmother, whose simple-looking cookies were legendary, for her secret. Her grandmother tasted Lily's technically perfect but soulless cake. "You have all the right ingredients from the pantry," she said, "but you forgot the one from the heart." Lily learned that passion, care, and a genuine desire to bring joy to others were the intangible ingredients that transformed good baking into great baking.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • proficient:능숙한, 숙달된
  • precision:정확성, 정밀함
  • pastry:페이스트리, 빵과자
  • bland:맛없는, 특징 없는
  • pantry:식료품 저장실
  • intangible:무형의, 만질 수 없는

Questions

76.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① The most important ingredient in cooking is love.
  • ② Technical skill alone is insufficient; genuine passion and care are essential for creating something truly special.
  • ③ The best recipes include ingredients that cannot be measured.
  • ④ Food made with love always tastes better.
  • ⑤ One's emotional state can affect the outcome of their work.
295
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 77]

The Artist's Canvas

Robert, an art student, was obsessed with photorealism. He would spend weeks painting a single landscape, trying to replicate every leaf and every shadow with perfect accuracy. His paintings were technically astounding, virtual photographs in oil paint, but they felt cold and devoid of emotion. His art teacher examined his latest, technically flawless work. "You have painted the tree exactly as it is," she acknowledged. "But you have not painted how it feels to stand under it." Robert began to understand that the purpose of art wasn't just to replicate reality, but to interpret it and convey the emotional experience of the artist.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary
