  • photorealism:극사실주의
  • replicate:복제하다
  • accuracy:정확성
  • astounding:몹시 놀라게 하는
  • devoid of:~이 없는, 결여된
  • interpret:해석하다
  • convey:전달하다

Questions

77.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① A painting should capture the feeling of a scene, not just its appearance.
  • ② True art transcends mere technical replication to convey emotional truth and personal interpretation.
  • ③ The artist's emotional connection to the subject is crucial for creating powerful art.
  • ④ There is a significant difference between seeing and feeling.
  • ⑤ Technical perfection can sometimes lead to a lack of emotional depth.
296
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 78]

The Climber's Peak

Nancy, a determined but anxious climber, was tackling the most challenging mountain of her career. Throughout the ascent, she found her gaze constantly drawn upward to the distant, formidable peak, and with every glance, her anxiety grew. The remaining distance seemed impossible. Her climbing partner, a seasoned veteran, noticed her distress. He gently turned her head away from the summit and towards her own feet. "The mountain is not conquered in one great leap to the top," he said. "It is conquered in a thousand small steps of 'here' and 'now'." Nancy learned to focus on the immediate task—the next handhold, the next foothold—and found her anxiety replaced by a calm, focused determination.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • anxious:불안해하는
  • tackle:(힘든 문제, 상황과) 씨름하다
  • ascent:등반, 오르기
  • formidable:어마어마한, 만만치 않은
  • handhold:손으로 잡을 곳
  • foothold:발 디딜 곳

Questions

78.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① A mountain is climbed through a series of many small steps.
  • ② Overwhelming goals are best achieved by breaking them down and focusing on the present, immediate action.
  • ③ Focusing too much on the final destination can cause debilitating anxiety.
  • ④ The journey of climbing is made up of countless 'here and now' moments.
  • ⑤ Mindfulness and living in the present are key to overcoming great challenges.
297
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 79]

The Teacher's Seed

Mr. Wilson felt like a failure. He taught in a tough, inner-city school where apathy was rampant and progress was glacial. After a particularly difficult week, he confided in a senior colleague that he felt his work was making no difference at all. The older teacher listened, then reminded him of a troubled student from years past who had recently been featured in the news for starting a successful community program. "We are not planting flowers to bloom in our own garden," the colleague said. "We are planting a forest, and we may never stand in its full shade." The words reminded Mr. Wilson that the true impact of his work was long-term and often unseen.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • apathy:무관심
  • rampant:만연하는, 걷잡을 수 없는
  • glacial:빙하의, 지극히 느린
  • confide in:~에게 비밀을 털어놓다

Questions

79.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Teachers are planting forests, not flowers.
  • ② The influence of a teacher is vast and long-term, often yielding results that the teacher may never personally witness.
  • ③ The goal of education is to create a lasting, positive impact on society as a whole.
  • ④ One should not expect immediate or personal rewards for their work.
  • ⑤ The true results of teaching are often indirect and delayed.
298
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 80]

The Craftsman's Tool

David was learning woodworking from his grandfather, a master craftsman. He was constantly frustrated by his simple hand tools, envying the advanced power machinery he saw in catalogs. He believed better tools would instantly elevate his clumsy work to a professional level. One day, his grandfather watched him struggle to make a clean cut with a handsaw. The old man then took the same saw and, with a few effortless strokes, made a perfectly straight cut. He handed the saw back to David. "The saw does not know if it is in the hand of a master or a fool," he said. "It only knows the hand." David understood that the tool was only an extension of the user's skill and patience.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • woodworking:목공
  • craftsman:장인
  • machinery:기계류
  • elevate:높이다, 승격시키다
  • effortless:힘들이지 않는

Questions

80.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① A saw is an inanimate object that responds to the user's control.
  • ② The effectiveness of a tool is determined entirely by the skill and control of the person using it, not by the tool itself.
  • ③ A master can achieve great results even with simple tools.
  • ④ One should focus on developing their own skills rather than coveting better equipment.
  • ⑤ The difference between a master and a fool lies in how they use their tools.
299
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 81]

The Mirror's Reflection

Sandra lived her life through the screen of her phone, her self-worth fluctuating with the number of likes and positive comments on her social media posts. She meticulously crafted an idealized online persona, constantly checking for others' applause. Her therapist, after listening to her anxieties for several weeks, offered a powerful metaphor. "When you live for the applause of an audience," she said, "you will perform their play, not your own." The words struck a deep chord. Sandra realized she had handed over the direction of her life to others and began the difficult process of disconnecting her self-worth from external validation and rediscovering her own authentic script.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • fluctuate:변동하다, 오르내리다
  • meticulously:꼼꼼하게
  • idealized:이상화된
  • persona:(다른 사람 눈에 비치는) 모습
  • validation:인정, 확인
  • authentic:진정한

Questions

81.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Acting in a play requires listening to the audience's applause.
  • ② When one's life is driven by the need for others' approval, they lose their autonomy and live according to others' expectations.
  • ③ It is impossible to please everyone in the audience.
  • ④ Social media encourages people to live inauthentic lives.
  • ⑤ True happiness comes from writing and directing one's own life story.
300
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 82]

The Potter's Wheel

Marcus, a novice potter, was on the brink of despair. Every time he tried to raise the walls of a pot on the wheel, the clay would inevitably wobble and collapse into a shapeless, muddy heap. He was convinced he lacked the fundamental talent for the craft. His instructor, however, picked up the collapsed clay, kneaded it, and centered it back on the wheel. "The clay that collapses," she said calmly, "teaches the hands far more than the clay that stands perfectly on the first try." Marcus began to see his failures not as evidence of his inadequacy, but as crucial, tactile lessons that were refining his touch and understanding.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • on the brink of:~의 직전에
  • inevitably:불가피하게
  • wobble:흔들리다
  • knead:(반죽 등을) 이기다, 주무르다
  • inadequacy:무능함, 불충분함
  • tactile:촉각의

Questions

82.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Clay must be properly prepared before it can be shaped.
  • ② Valuable learning and skill development come more from analyzing and correcting failures than from easy, initial successes.
  • ③ It is common for beginners to fail many times before they succeed.
  • ④ The most important lessons in a craft are learned through physical practice.
  • ⑤ One should not be discouraged by early failures.
301
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 83]

The Swimmer's Current

Lisa was a talented swimmer, but she had a habit of always choosing the path of least resistance during training. She would pick the calmest lane in the pool and avoid practicing in challenging conditions like choppy water or against a current. Her coach observed this pattern with growing concern. Before a major open-water competition, he told her, "A ship that has only ever sailed in a calm sea is not prepared for a storm." During the actual race, when the wind picked up and the water became rough, Lisa struggled immensely, realizing too late that avoiding challenges in training had left her unprepared for the reality of competition.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • path of least resistance:가장 쉬운 길
  • choppy:파도가 일렁이는
  • open-water:탁 트인 수역의
  • immensely:엄청나게

Questions

83.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① A ship must be tested in a storm to prove its seaworthiness.
  • ② Avoiding challenges and difficulties during preparation leads to a lack of readiness for real-world situations.
  • ③ It is important to train in conditions that are similar to the actual competition.
  • ④ One's true ability is only revealed when faced with adversity.
  • ⑤ Overconfidence gained in easy conditions can be dangerous.
302
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 84]

The Compass Rose

Tony was a bright but indecisive college student, constantly changing his major based on the latest job market trends and his parents' suggestions. One semester he was pre-med, the next he was computer science, and then business. He felt perpetually lost, his future a blurry landscape. His academic advisor, reviewing his chaotic transcript, drew a compass rose on a piece of paper. "A compass that points to every wind that blows," she said, "will never find its own true north." Tony realized he had been looking for direction everywhere but within himself. He needed to discover his own passions and strengths to set a steady course.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • indecisive:우유부단한
  • pre-med:의과 대학 예과 과정
  • perpetually:끊임없이
  • transcript:성적 증명서
  • compass rose:나침도 (나침반의 방위 그림)

Questions

84.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① A compass is designed to point north, not in the direction of the wind.
  • ② Constantly changing one's direction based on external influences prevents the discovery of one's true passion and purpose.
  • ③ It is important to have a single, unwavering goal in life.
  • ④ Following popular trends is an unreliable strategy for career planning.
  • ⑤ One must look inward to find true direction.
303
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 85]

The Garden's Roots

Rachel, an impatient gardener, was frustrated that her neighbor's tomato plants seemed to be growing much faster and taller than her own. Determined to catch up, she began to over-water her plants and apply excessive amounts of "miracle-growth" fertilizer. Her plants did shoot up quickly, but they looked weak and spindly. Her experienced gardening mentor observed her efforts and offered a word of caution. "Be careful," he warned. "A tree that grows too fast in the sun often has the shallowest roots in the storm." Rachel learned that slow, steady, natural growth creates a stronger, more resilient foundation than artificially accelerated growth.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • spindly:가늘고 긴, 허약한
  • caution:경고, 조심
  • shallow:얕은
  • resilient:회복력 있는
  • artificially:인위적으로
  • accelerated:가속화된

Questions

85.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Trees with shallow roots are more likely to fall in a storm.
  • ② Rapid, superficial growth often lacks the strong foundation necessary to withstand challenges.
  • ③ It is dangerous to pursue quick results at the expense of solid preparation.
  • ④ Natural processes are superior to artificial interventions.
  • ⑤ True strength is developed slowly and steadily over time.
304
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 86]

The Writer's Voice

Hannah was a talented writer, but she was also a chameleon. She would adopt the terse style of Hemingway for one story, then the ornate prose of Faulkner for another, trying to please different literary tastes. As a result, her body of work felt disjointed, lacking a core personality. Her writing coach, after reading several of her disparate pieces, gave her a direct piece of feedback. "When you try to wear every mask," he said, "you forget the shape of your own face." Hannah understood that in her effort to be versatile, she had neglected the crucial task of developing her own authentic and recognizable voice.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • chameleon:카멜레온, 변덕스러운 사람
  • terse:간결한
  • ornate:화려하게 장식된
  • prose:산문
  • disjointed:연결이 안 되는, 일관성이 없는
  • disparate:이질적인
  • versatile:다재다능한

Questions

86.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Wearing too many masks can make one lose their identity.
  • ② Constantly trying to imitate others or cater to different expectations prevents the development of one's own authentic style and identity.
  • ③ A writer should have a consistent and recognizable voice.
  • ④ It is more important to be authentic than to be versatile.
  • ⑤ Finding one's true self is a difficult but necessary journey.
305
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 87]

The Builder's Blueprint

Kevin was eager to build a new deck for his house and decided to skip the "boring" step of creating a detailed blueprint. He thought he could just "figure it out" as he went along, saving time. Halfway through the project, he discovered his measurements were off, the support beams were uneven, and he had wasted a significant amount of expensive lumber. His contractor friend, called in to assess the mess, shook his head. "An hour of planning," he said, "can save you ten hours of fixing." Kevin learned the hard way that proper preparation, far from being a waste of time, is the most effective way to prevent costly errors and ensure a quality result.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • deck:데크 (집에 붙어 있는 나무 테라스)
  • blueprint:청사진, 상세한 계획
  • lumber:목재
  • assess:평가하다
  • contractor:계약자, 도급업자

Questions

87.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Planning is a more efficient use of time than fixing mistakes.
  • ② Investing time in careful preparation and planning upfront can prevent much larger problems and save more time later.
  • ③ It is a common mistake to underestimate the importance of planning.
  • ④ A lack of planning is the primary cause of failure in construction projects.
  • ⑤ One should always consult an expert before starting a major project.
306
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 88]

The Musician's Practice

Oliver, a gifted young guitarist, was impatient to play flashy, complex solos. He considered the practice of basic scales and chords to be tedious and beneath him. He wanted to jump straight to the performance. His music teacher listened to his frustrated and sloppy attempts at a difficult piece. He then stopped him and played a single, perfectly tuned C-major scale. "You cannot build a cathedral," the teacher said, "on a foundation of sand." Oliver realized that the fundamental skills he disdained were the very bedrock upon which all advanced techniques and true musicality were built.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • flashy:화려한, 현란한
  • scale:음계
  • chord:화음
  • tedious:지루한
  • beneath him:그의 격에 맞지 않는
  • cathedral:대성당
  • bedrock:기반암, 기초

Questions

88.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① It is impossible to build a large building on a weak foundation.
  • ② Complex and impressive achievements cannot be built without a solid mastery of fundamental skills.
  • ③ One should not neglect the basics in pursuit of advanced skills.
  • ④ A strong foundation is the most important part of any structure.
  • ⑤ True mastery is built upon a foundation of disciplined practice.
307
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 89]

The Fisherman's Patience

Sam, a young and energetic fisherman, believed that the more he did, the more fish he would catch. He constantly recast his line, changed his bait, and moved his boat around the small cove. His grandfather, sitting silently on the shore, watched his grandson's frantic activity with an amused expression. After an hour of this, Sam had caught nothing, while the old man's bucket was nearly full. "The fish can hear a restless heart through the line," the grandfather finally called out. Sam learned that a state of calm, patient anticipation was often a more powerful tool than ceaseless, anxious action.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • cove:작은 만
  • frantic:정신없이 서두는
  • amused:재미있어하는
  • anticipation:기대, 예상
  • ceaseless:끊임없는

Questions

89.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Fish are sensitive to vibrations in the water.
  • ② An anxious and impatient mindset can be counterproductive and hinder success.
  • ③ A state of inner calm and patience is essential for achieving one's goals.
  • ④ Sometimes, doing less is more effective than doing more.
  • ⑤ Wisdom involves understanding when to act and when to be still.
308
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 90]

The Artist's Imperfection

Grace was a talented sculptor but a crippling perfectionist. She would work on a piece for weeks, only to destroy it if she found a single, tiny flaw that deviated from her ideal vision. As a result, she had never completed a major work and was sinking into a state of creative despair. Her mentor, visiting her studio, saw the fragments of her destroyed sculptures. He picked up a piece of a "failed" work. "You are chasing a ghost," he told her. "Perfection is the enemy of the good." Grace realized that her relentless pursuit of an unattainable ideal was preventing her from creating anything good at all. She learned to accept imperfection as part of the process.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • crippling:심각한 손상을 주는
  • flaw:결함
  • deviate:벗어나다
  • fragment:조각, 파편
  • relentless:끈질긴, 가차 없는
  • unattainable:도달할 수 없는

Questions

90.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① The pursuit of perfection is like chasing a ghost.
  • ② Striving for an impossible standard of perfection can prevent one from achieving perfectly acceptable and valuable results.
  • ③ It is better to complete something imperfectly than to never complete it at all.
  • ④ Perfectionism can be a major barrier to productivity and creativity.
  • ⑤ One should learn to be satisfied with "good enough."
309
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 91]

The Lighthouse's Purpose

Emma, the solitary keeper of a lighthouse on a remote, fog-shrouded island, often felt her work was an anachronism. In an age of GPS and advanced radar, she wondered if her nightly vigil truly mattered. Ships were rare, and the silence was vast. During a sudden, violent squall that disabled the electronics of a small ferry, her light became its only guide to safety. The ferry's captain later radioed his profound gratitude. Emma's supervisor, hearing the story, sent her a short message: "A lighthouse does not choose which ships to save. It just stands, and it shines." Emma understood her purpose was not to be needed always, but to be there, unwavering, for when she was needed most.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • solitary:고독한
  • fog-shrouded:안개에 휩싸인
  • anachronism:시대착오
  • vigil:밤샘, 불침번
  • squall:돌풍, 스콜
  • unwavering:확고한, 변함없는

Questions

91.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① A lighthouse provides light for all ships without discrimination.
  • ② The true value of a duty is in its steadfast, impartial performance, regardless of who benefits or how often it is needed.
  • ③ The purpose of a lighthouse is to be a universal symbol of hope.
  • ④ One should perform their duties without questioning their importance.
  • ⑤ Even outdated technology can be crucial in an emergency.
310
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 92]

The Sculptor's Stone

James, a student of sculpture, was on the verge of quitting. He felt he was fighting the stone, and the stone was always winning. His attempts to impose his will on the marble resulted in fractured, lifeless forms. His master sculptor watched his struggle. He then picked up a particularly stubborn piece of granite, a stone known for its hardness. "Do not fight the stone," he advised. "The stone that resists the chisel the most is the one that holds the finest detail." James learned that the challenge presented by the material was not an obstacle to be overcome, but a quality that, when understood and worked with, could lead to the most profound and lasting beauty.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • on the verge of:~의 직전에
  • impose one's will:자신의 의지를 강요하다
  • fractured:부서진, 균열이 생긴
  • stubborn:완고한
  • granite:화강암
  • chisel:정, 조각칼

Questions

92.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① The hardest stones are the best materials for detailed sculptures.
  • ② The greatest challenges and difficulties often yield the most valuable and refined results.
  • ③ One must work in harmony with their materials, not against them.
  • ④ The process of creating something beautiful is often a struggle.
  • ⑤ Persistence is required to overcome difficult obstacles.
311
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 93]

The Farmer's Wisdom

Laura, having inherited her family's farm, was eager to modernize. She dismissed her grandfather's traditional, slow methods of crop rotation and soil enrichment, opting for aggressive, chemical-heavy shortcuts to maximize short-term yield. The first year's harvest was decent, but the second was poor, and by the third, the soil was barren. Her elderly neighbor, who had farmed the adjacent land for fifty years, looked at her depleted fields and said with a sad shake of his head, "The earth keeps a perfect ledger of all debts and all deposits." Laura learned that nature has its own system of accounting, and shortcuts often create debts that must eventually be paid.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • crop rotation:돌려짓기, 윤작
  • enrichment:비옥화, 풍부하게 함
  • maximize:극대화하다
  • yield:수확량
  • barren:척박한, 불모의
  • adjacent:인접한
  • ledger:(회계) 원장
  • deposit:예금, 맡기기

Questions

93.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① The earth has a memory of all past actions.
  • ② Actions have long-term consequences, and neglecting foundational principles for short-term gains will ultimately lead to failure.
  • ③ Sustainable success requires working in harmony with natural systems, not exploiting them.
  • ④ One cannot cheat nature without eventually facing the consequences.
  • ⑤ It is important to maintain a balance of giving and taking in any system.
312
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 94]

The Teacher's Influence

Michael, a young teacher in a tough inner-city school, felt his passion waning. His students seemed trapped in difficult circumstances, and his lessons felt like a drop of rain in a vast desert. He questioned if he was making any real impact. Years later, he attended a conference and was approached by a keynote speaker, a renowned scientist he didn't recognize. The scientist introduced herself as a former student from his very first class. "A teacher never knows where their influence stops," she said, her voice thick with emotion. "It is a river that flows into other rivers, creating a sea you will never see." Michael understood that his influence was not a single act, but a chain reaction of inspiration.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • waning:약해지는, 시들해지는
  • keynote speaker:기조 연설자
  • renowned:유명한, 명성 있는
  • chain reaction:연쇄 반응

Questions

94.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① It is impossible to measure the full extent of a teacher's influence.
  • ② The impact of a teacher extends far beyond the individual student, multiplying and spreading in unforeseen ways through society.
  • ③ A teacher's work is like a river that flows continuously.
  • ④ The true results of education are often indirect and invisible to the teacher.
  • ⑤ A single person can have a ripple effect that changes many lives.
313
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 95]

The Musician's Journey

Sarah, a dedicated violinist, practiced with fierce discipline. Yet, she was haunted by a sense of inadequacy, constantly comparing her progress to that of child prodigies she saw online. She viewed her musical development as a race, and she felt she was losing. Her wise teacher, sensing her distress, put down his own violin. "Music is not a race to a finish line," he told her gently. "It is a landscape to be explored for a lifetime." This simple shift in metaphor changed everything for Sarah. She stopped seeing music as a competition and started seeing it as a vast, beautiful territory to be explored at her own pace, with its own unique discoveries along the way.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • discipline:훈련, 규율
  • haunted:(생각, 감정이) 뇌리에서 떠나지 않는
  • inadequacy:무능함, 불충분함
  • prodigy:영재
  • distress:고통, 괴로움

Questions

95.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Music is not a competition with a clear winner or loser.
  • ② A skill or passion should be viewed as a lifelong journey of exploration and personal growth, not a competitive race against others.
  • ③ One should enjoy the process of learning without being obsessed with the final goal.
  • ④ The path of a musician is a long and continuous one.
  • ⑤ Comparing oneself to others can ruin the joy of a personal pursuit.
314
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 96]

The Gardener's Seasons

Tom, a novice gardener, was filled with despair as the first frost of winter arrived. His vibrant summer garden turned brown and appeared to die overnight. He felt all his hard work had been for nothing and was ready to clear the plot. His experienced neighbor stopped him as he was about to pull up a dormant rose bush. "Do not mourn what looks like an end," the old gardener advised. "In the heart of every winter, there is a trembling spring." Tom learned that periods of dormancy and apparent lifelessness are often essential phases of rest and preparation for future renewal and growth.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • novice:초심자
  • frost:서리
  • vibrant:활기찬
  • plot:작은 땅 구획
  • dormant:휴면기의
  • mourn:애도하다
  • trembling:떨리는

Questions

96.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Spring always follows winter in the cycle of seasons.
  • ② Within every period of hardship or apparent inactivity, there lies the potential for new beginnings and growth.
  • ③ One should not give up hope during difficult times.
  • ④ What appears to be an ending can actually be a period of necessary rest.
  • ⑤ Nature teaches us that life is a cycle of death and rebirth.
315
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 97]

The Writer's Well

Jennifer, a prolific novelist, had hit a wall. For months, she had been suffering from a severe case of writer's block, and the words simply would not come. She felt barren, her creativity completely depleted. She forced herself to sit at her desk for eight hours a day, staring at a blank screen, which only deepened her anxiety. Her writing mentor, an older, wiser author, advised her to take a break. "You cannot draw water from an empty well," he told her. "A well must be given time for the rain to refill it." Jennifer learned that creative output requires periods of input and rest, and that forcing creativity can be counterproductive.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • prolific:다작하는
  • barren:불모의, 메마른
  • depleted:고갈된
  • counterproductive:역효과를 낳는

Questions

97.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① It is impossible to get water from a well that has no water.
  • ② Creativity, like a well, is a resource that can be depleted and requires periods of rest and new experiences to be replenished.
  • ③ Forcing oneself to be creative when feeling uninspired is an ineffective strategy.
  • ④ It is important to take breaks to avoid creative burnout.
  • ⑤ To produce creative work, one must first gather new ideas and inspiration.
316
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 98]

The Mountain Guide's Advice

Alex, a young and ambitious climber, was determined to reach the summit of a treacherous peak faster than anyone before him. He ignored his guide's advice to rest and acclimatize properly, pushing himself to the brink of exhaustion. The experienced guide, seeing the dangerous signs of altitude sickness in Alex, physically blocked his path. "The mountain is not going anywhere," the guide said sternly. "But you will be, if you do not respect it." The stark warning cut through Alex's ego-driven haste. He realized that his quest for speed was jeopardizing the journey itself, and that patience and respect for the challenge were prerequisites for success, not signs of weakness.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • ambitious:야심 있는
  • treacherous:위험한
  • acclimatize:(새로운 환경에) 순응하다
  • brink:직전, 가장자리
  • sternly:엄격하게, 단호하게
  • jeopardize:위태롭게 하다

Questions

98.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① The mountain will remain, but the climber's life is fragile.
  • ② Disregarding safety and showing disrespect for a challenge in pursuit of speed or ego can lead to catastrophic failure.
  • ③ Patience and caution are more important than speed in high-stakes endeavors.
  • ④ The opportunity to climb a mountain is a privilege that should not be taken for granted.
  • ⑤ One's ego can be the greatest obstacle to achieving a goal.
317
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 99]

The Chef's Secret

Ryan was a technically brilliant chef. His knife skills were flawless, his plating was artistic, and his understanding of food chemistry was profound. Yet, the head chef of the Michelin-starred restaurant where he worked felt his dishes, while perfect, lacked a certain soul. After tasting Ryan's latest, technically impeccable creation, the head chef gave him some unexpected advice. "You cook with your brilliant head," he said. "Now, learn to cook with your foolish heart." Ryan began to understand that the highest level of artistry required not just precision and knowledge, but also intuition, passion, and a willingness to be vulnerable.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • flawless:흠 없는
  • plating:(음식을) 접시에 담기
  • profound:깊은, 심오한
  • Michelin-starred:미슐랭 별을 받은
  • impeccable:흠잡을 데 없는
  • vulnerable:취약한, 상처받기 쉬운

Questions

99.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Cooking with one's heart is more important than cooking with one's head.
  • ② To achieve true mastery, one must complement technical perfection with genuine passion, intuition, and emotional expression.
  • ③ The best dishes are made with love, not just skill.
  • ④ It is important to take risks and be "foolish" to create something truly great.
  • ⑤ Logic and emotion must be balanced in any creative pursuit.
318
Vera's Flavor - PART 3 함축의미추론
[지문 100]

The Clockmaker's Final Lesson

On his last day before retirement, the master clockmaker, William, was giving his final lesson to his young apprentice. The apprentice, eager and ambitious, was already sketching designs for highly complex, multi-functional timepieces. William, however, drew the young man's attention to a simple, elegant grandfather clock he had made fifty years prior. It had only one function: to tell time perfectly. "We spend our youth trying to build the most complicated locks," the old master mused. "But we spend our old age searching for the simplest key." The apprentice understood that true mastery lay not in complexity for its own sake, but in the profound, elegant simplicity that comes from a deep understanding of fundamental principles.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • retirement:은퇴
  • apprentice:제자
  • timepiece:시계
  • grandfather clock:대형 괘종시계
  • profound:깊은, 심오한
  • fundamental:근본적인

Questions

100.밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?

  • ① Young people prefer complexity, while old people prefer simplicity.
  • ② The journey to mastery often involves moving from a fascination with complexity to an appreciation for profound simplicity.
  • ③ The simplest solutions are often the most difficult to find.
  • ④ True wisdom lies in understanding the basics, not in creating complicated systems.
  • ⑤ The purpose of a lock is to be opened by a key.
319
PART 3

정답 및 해설

Vera's Flavor - Answer Key Part 3: 함축의미추론 (1-20)

Passage 1-10

  • 1: ②잦은 방법론 변경이 의미 있는 성장을 방해할 수 있음을 의미.
  • 2: ②부정직함의 장기적 피해가 진실의 일시적 고통보다 훨씬 크다는 의미.
  • 3: ②실패한 도전을 통해 자신의 잠재력과 인내력의 한계를 발견했다는 의미.
  • 4: ②자신의 행동이 낳은 긍정적 영향이 당장은 보이지 않아도 의미 있다는 것.
  • 5: ②외적인 모습에만 치중하고 내면의 성장을 소홀히 하고 있음을 지적.
  • 6: ②자만하여 노력을 멈추는 순간 실패는 불가피하다는 의미.
  • 7: ②진정한 봉사는 외부의 인정이나 보상이 아닌, 그 자체를 위해 수행된다는 것.
  • 8: ②진정한 예술성은 내재된 잠재력을 발견하고 드러내는 데 있다는 의미.
  • 9: ④재정적 이득과 더 깊고 본질적인 정신적 만족감 사이의 근본적 차이를 의미.
  • 10: ②도서관의 진정한 목적은 영감과 포부를 키우는 공간이지 단순 보관소가 아니라는 것.

Passage 11-20

  • 11: ②자연적 과정은 인위적으로 서두르면 해가 될 수 있다는 의미.
  • 12: ①이론적 지식을 효과적으로 만들려면 실용적 적용과 경험이 중요하다는 것.
  • 13: ②진정한 목적은 안전에만 머무는 것이 아니라 위험을 감수하며 발견된다는 의미.
  • 14: ①명확한 자기 인식과 이해를 위해서는 평온한 마음이 필요하다는 것.
  • 15: ①자신의 지식이나 능력을 나누어도 자신의 가치는 줄어들지 않는다는 의미.
  • 16: ②장애물에도 불구하고 궁극적 목표에 도달할 대안적 경로를 찾을 수 있다는 것.
  • 17: ②새로운 지식이나 기술을 습득하려면 열린 마음과 겸손함이 필요하다는 의미.
  • 18: ②역경과 어려운 환경을 통해 위대한 힘과 인격이 형성될 수 있다는 것.
  • 19: ②의미 있는 성취는 헌신적인 노력이 필요하며 쉬운 방법으로는 도달할 수 없다는 의미.
  • 20: ②진정한 보살핌은 때로 상대의 자율성을 존중하는 것을 요구한다는 의미.
320
Vera's Flavor - Answer Key Part 3: 함축의미추론 (21-40)

Passage 21-30

  • 21: ②진정한 성장을 위해서는 해체와 재정렬의 과정이 필요하다는 의미.
  • 22: ②큰 목표를 달성하려면 최종 결과에 압도되지 말고 즉각적이고 관리 가능한 행동에 집중해야 함.
  • 23: ②보이지 않는 준비와 노력의 기간이 미래의 가시적인 성공에 필수적이라는 의미.
  • 24: ①진정한 용기는 두려움이 없는 상태가 아니라, 두려움을 느끼면서도 행동하는 것임.
  • 25: ②현재의 고난이 아직 보이지 않는 더 큰 의미 있는 목적의 일부일 수 있다는 것.
  • 26: ②타인에 대한 자신의 행동과 태도는 결국 자신에게 되돌아온다는 의미.
  • 27: ②성공의 가장 중요한 요소는 기술이 아니라 진정한 사랑과 헌신이라는 것.
  • 28: ②시간을 너무 통제하려 하면 비효율과 스트레스를 낳는다는 의미.
  • 29: ②결점과 불완전함이 예상치 못하게 아름다움과 통찰의 원천이 될 수 있다는 것.
  • 30: ②타인과 비교하기보다 자신만의 고유한 자질과 길을 받아들이고 감사해야 함.

Passage 31-40

  • 31: ①인내심 없이 계속 전략을 바꾸면 피로와 실패만 남는다는 의미.
  • 32: ②작고 꾸준한 노력이 쌓여 시간이 지나면 중요하고 강력한 결과를 만들어낸다는 것.
  • 33: ②예술가의 역할은 매체의 내재된 잠재력을 발견하고 드러내는 것이라는 의미.
  • 34: ②지속적이고 아름다운 결과는 꾸준한 일상의 노력으로 이루어진다는 것.
  • 35: ②지식과 아이디어를 나누는 것은 개인의 가치를 줄이지 않고 집단의 선을 향상시킨다는 의미.
  • 36: ④경직된 계획보다 유연성과 회복력이 더 가치 있다는 것.
  • 37: ②큰 과업을 시작할 때는 최종 목표에 위축되지 말고 즉각적인 첫걸음에 집중해야 함.
  • 38: ②타인의 의견에만 의존하면 진정한 자아를 발견할 수 없다는 의미.
  • 39: ①시간의 양보다 시간을 어떻게 의미 있게 보내는지가 더 중요하다는 것.
  • 40: ②성장과 학습은 안전지대를 벗어나 위험을 감수할 때 이루어진다는 의미.
321
Vera's Flavor - Answer Key Part 3: 함축의미추론 (41-60)

Passage 41-50

  • 41: ②고난과 어려운 도전은 힘과 인격을 형성하는 데 필요하다는 의미.
  • 42: ②현재 노력의 긍정적 영향은 미래 세대에게 보이지 않는 혜택을 줄 수 있다는 것.
  • 43: ②사진가의 기술, 비전, 창의성이 장비의 질보다 더 중요하다는 의미.
  • 44: ④결과를 강요하기보다 성공이 자랄 수 있는 환경을 조성해야 한다는 것.
  • 45: ①압도적인 목표는 큰 목표에 압도되지 않고 당면한 과제에 집중함으로써 달성된다는 의미.
  • 46: ②무언가를 개선하려면, 비록 불완전하더라도 먼저 무언가를 만들어야 한다는 것.
  • 47: ②성공의 가장 중요한 요소는 정원사의 꾸준한 개인적 관심과 보살핌이라는 의미.
  • 48: ①자신의 시작 단계를 다른 사람의 고급 단계와 비교하는 것은 비논리적이라는 것.
  • 49: ②일의 가치는 빈도가 아니라 결정적인 순간의 영향력으로 측정되어야 한다는 의미.
  • 50: ②절대적인 통제를 포기하면 예상치 못한 가치 있는 창의적 발견으로 이어질 수 있다는 것.

Passage 51-60

  • 51: ②결함에도 불구하고 모든 개인은 더 큰 공동체 내에서 가치 있는 역할을 할 수 있다는 의미.
  • 52: ①진정한 진보의 척도는 타인과의 비교가 아닌 자기 개선이라는 것.
  • 53: ①명백한 실수나 사고가 긍정적이고 창의적인 요소로 변형될 수 있다는 의미.
  • 54: ②원하는 결과는 필요한 기초 노력과 준비 없이는 달성될 수 없다는 것.
  • 55: ②인내심은 고품질의 세밀한 작업을 달성하는 데 가장 필수적인 자질이라는 의미.
  • 56: ②교사의 긍정적 영향은 학생이 학교를 떠난 후에도 오랫동안 계속된다는 것.
  • 57: ②경직된 기대를 강요하기보다 자연스럽고 역동적인 성장 과정을 받아들여야 한다는 의미.
  • 58: ②진정한 창의성과 가치는 타인을 모방하는 것이 아니라 자신만의 고유한 관점을 표현하는 데 있다는 것.
  • 59: ②불안하고 조급한 마음가짐은 성공을 방해할 수 있다는 의미.
  • 60: ②고품질의 정밀한 작업은 서두름과 지름길을 통해 달성될 수 없다는 것.
322
Vera's Flavor - Answer Key Part 3: 함축의미추론 (61-80)

Passage 61-70

  • 61: ②집단적 조화와 협력이 개인의 두각보다 더 가치 있다는 의미.
  • 62: ②숨겨진 잠재력과 가치는 적절한 기술, 노력, 인식을 통해 드러날 수 있다는 것.
  • 63: ①기본 기술을 습득하지 않고 고급 기술을 시도하고 있다는 의미.
  • 64: ②어떤 노력의 보이지 않는 기초 요소가 장기적 안정과 성공에 가장 중요하다는 것.
  • 65: ②일관된 방향성과 헌신이 부족하면 어떤 계획도 효과가 없다는 의미.
  • 66: ②기본 원리에 대한 깊은 이해가 실용적 기술을 효과적으로 적용하기 전에 필수적이라는 것.
  • 67: ②진정한 예술성은 극적인 표현뿐만 아니라 미묘한 통제와 기본 기술에서 발견된다는 의미.
  • 68: ②도서관의 가치는 비판적 사고와 의미 있는 탐구를 유도하는 데 있다는 것.
  • 69: ②외부 요인을 탓하기보다 자신의 기술 부족에 대한 책임을 져야 한다는 의미.
  • 70: ②질문하는 것은 무지의 장벽을 극복하고 이해를 여는 필수적인 행위라는 것.

Passage 71-80

  • 71: ②기존 조건을 창의적으로 활용하는 것이 비싼 도구를 구하는 것보다 더 중요하다는 의미.
  • 72: ②개인은 각기 다른 시기에 잠재력을 발휘하므로 단일한 시간표로 판단해서는 안 된다는 것.
  • 73: ②예술과 소통에서 의도적인 쉼과 여백은 내용 자체만큼이나 의미 있다는 것.
  • 74: ②창작물의 초기 버전은 완성품이 아닌 탐색적이고 불완전한 기초라는 의미.
  • 75: ②안전을 제공하는 것들이 성장과 새로운 경험을 막는 장애물이 될 수도 있다는 것.
  • 76: ②기술적 능력만으로는 부족하며, 진정한 열정과 보살핌이 특별한 것을 만드는 데 필수적이라는 의미.
  • 77: ②진정한 예술은 단순한 기술적 복제를 넘어 감정적 진실과 개인적 해석을 전달해야 한다는 것.
  • 78: ②압도적인 목표는 현재의 즉각적인 행동에 집중함으로써 가장 잘 달성된다는 의미.
  • 79: ②교사의 영향력은 광범위하고 장기적이어서 교사가 직접 목격하지 못하는 결과를 낳는다는 것.
  • 80: ②도구의 효과는 도구 자체가 아니라 사용하는 사람의 기술과 통제력에 의해 결정된다는 의미.
323
Vera's Flavor - Answer Key Part 3: 함축의미추론 (81-100)

Passage 81-90

  • 81: ②타인의 인정에 따라 살면 자율성을 잃고 타인의 기대에 맞춰 살게 된다는 의미.
  • 82: ②가치 있는 배움은 쉬운 성공보다 실패를 분석하고 교정하는 과정에서 더 많이 온다는 것.
  • 83: ②준비 과정에서 도전을 피하면 실제 상황에 대한 준비가 부족해진다는 의미.
  • 84: ②외부 영향에 따라 계속 방향을 바꾸면 진정한 열정과 목적을 발견할 수 없다는 것.
  • 85: ②빠르고 피상적인 성장은 도전을 견딜 수 있는 강한 기반이 부족하다는 의미.
  • 86: ②끊임없이 타인을 모방하면 자신만의 진정한 스타일과 정체성을 개발하지 못하게 된다는 것.
  • 87: ②초기에 신중하게 계획하는 것이 나중에 더 큰 문제를 예방하고 시간을 절약한다는 의미.
  • 88: ②복잡하고 인상적인 성취는 탄탄한 기본기 없이는 이룰 수 없다는 것.
  • 89: ②불안하고 조급한 마음가짐은 성공을 방해하고 역효과를 낳을 수 있다는 의미.
  • 90: ②불가능한 완벽함을 추구하면 가치 있는 결과를 얻지 못하게 될 수 있다는 것.

Passage 91-100

  • 91: ②의무의 진정한 가치는 누가 혜택을 받는지에 관계없이 꾸준하고 공정하게 수행하는 데 있다는 것.
  • 92: ②가장 큰 도전과 어려움이 종종 가장 가치 있고 세련된 결과를 낳는다는 의미.
  • 93: ②장기적인 결과는 행동에 따르며, 단기 이익을 위해 기본 원칙을 무시하면 실패로 이어진다는 것.
  • 94: ②교사의 영향력은 개인을 넘어 사회에 예측할 수 없는 방식으로 퍼져나간다는 의미.
  • 95: ②기술이나 열정은 타인과의 경쟁이 아닌 평생의 탐험과 개인적 성장 여정으로 봐야 한다는 것.
  • 96: ②모든 고난이나 비활동기 속에는 새로운 시작과 성장의 잠재력이 있다는 의미.
  • 97: ②창의력은 고갈될 수 있으며, 재충전을 위해 휴식과 새로운 경험이 필요하다는 것.
  • 98: ②안전을 무시하고 도전을 존중하지 않으면 파국적인 실패로 이어질 수 있다는 의미.
  • 99: ②진정한 경지에 이르려면 기술적 완벽함에 열정과 감성적 표현을 더해야 한다는 것.
  • 100: ②숙달의 여정은 복잡성에 대한 매료에서 심오한 단순함에 대한 감사로 나아간다는 의미.
324
PART 4

문장 삽입

Vera's Flavor - PART 4 문장 삽입
[문제 1]

주어진 문장

This process saves about 30 percent of the energy needed to make glass from virgin materials.

Glass is one of the few materials that can be recycled endlessly without loss of quality. ( ① ) When glass products are collected and sent to the recycling plant, they are crushed into small pieces called cullet. ( ② ) The cullet is then mixed with raw materials like sand and soda ash and melted in a furnace. ( ③ ) The resulting mixture can be molded into new bottles and jars identical to the originals. ( ④ ) In fact, using cullet reduces the energy required to melt the batch because it melts at a lower temperature. ( ⑤ ) For this reason, recycling glass not only conserves resources but also cuts greenhouse-gas emissions.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • cullet:유리 파편
  • furnace:용광로
  • conserve:보존하다
[문제 2]

주어진 문장

Even a single night of poor sleep can impair memory and judgment the following day.

Most adults need between seven and nine hours of sleep each night, yet surveys show that many people fall short of this target. ( ① ) Chronic lack of sleep has been linked to a higher risk of heart disease, obesity, and depression. ( ② ) Laboratory studies also reveal that reaction times slow dramatically after extended wakefulness. ( ③ ) Drivers who have been awake for 20 hours perform about as poorly as those with a blood-alcohol level of 0.08 percent. ( ④ ) Consequently, drowsy driving is responsible for thousands of accidents each year. ( ⑤ ) Getting consistent, high-quality rest is therefore one of the simplest ways to protect both physical and mental health.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • impair:손상시키다
  • chronic:만성적인
  • drowsy:졸린
325
Vera's Flavor - PART 4 문장 삽입
[문제 3]

주어진 문장

If temperatures remain elevated, the algae may never return, causing the coral to die.

Coral reefs owe their brilliant colors to symbiotic algae that live inside coral tissue. ( ① ) When ocean temperatures rise, corals become stressed and expel these algae. ( ② ) Without their colorful partners, the corals turn ghostly white, a phenomenon known as bleaching. ( ③ ) Bleached corals are weaker and more susceptible to disease. ( ④ ) Large-scale bleaching events have become more frequent over the past three decades, threatening reef ecosystems worldwide. ( ⑤ ) Scientists warn that limiting global warming is essential to preserve these biodiverse habitats.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • symbiotic:공생의
  • expel:내쫓다
  • susceptible:취약한
[문제 4]

주어진 문장

As a result, more drivers feel confident making the switch to electric models.

Battery technology has improved rapidly in recent years. ( ① ) Modern lithium-ion packs store more energy while weighing less than their predecessors. ( ② ) As ranges climb above 500 kilometers per charge, so-called range anxiety begins to fade. ( ③ ) Governments are also expanding charging-station networks along major highways. ( ④ ) In addition, many countries now offer tax credits or rebates to purchasers of electric vehicles. ( ⑤ ) Analysts predict that electric cars could account for half of all new car sales by 2030.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • predecessor:이전 모델, 선임자
  • range anxiety:주행거리 불안
  • rebate:환급
326
Vera's Flavor - PART 4 문장 삽입
[문제 5]

주어진 문장

Instead of striving for photographic realism, they sought to convey the fleeting impressions of a moment.

In the late nineteenth century, a group of young painters broke away from the rigid rules of France's art academies. ( ① ) They began painting outdoors to capture changing light and atmosphere. ( ② ) Critics at the time dismissed their loose brushstrokes and bright colors as unfinished and childish. ( ③ ) Nevertheless, the public slowly warmed to their fresh perspective. ( ④ ) Today works by Monet, Renoir, and Degas are celebrated worldwide. ( ⑤ ) Their movement forever altered the course of modern art.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • strive for:~을 위해 노력하다
  • fleeting:순식간의
  • rigid:엄격한
[문제 6]

주어진 문장

These particles are small enough to be ingested by plankton at the very base of the oceanic food web.

Plastic trash does not disappear once it enters the ocean. ( ① ) Waves and sunlight gradually break larger items into tiny fragments called microplastics. ( ② ) These fragments can travel vast distances, turning up on remote Arctic beaches and deep-sea trenches alike. ( ③ ) Studies have detected microplastics in seafood, table salt, and even drinking water. ( ④ ) Because plastics attract toxic chemicals, they may concentrate pollutants as they move through the food chain. ( ⑤ ) Reducing single-use plastics on land is therefore critical for protecting marine life.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • ingest:섭취하다
  • fragment:파편, 조각
  • pollutant:오염물질
327
Vera's Flavor - PART 4 문장 삽입
[문제 7]

주어진 문장

This practice laid the groundwork for what we now call paper money.

Centuries ago, trade in China grew so brisk that bulky metal coins became impractical. ( ① ) Merchants began leaving their heavy currency with trusted agents and receiving paper receipts instead. ( ② ) Over time, these receipts started circulating hand-to-hand as payment for goods. ( ③ ) The government soon recognized the convenience of this system and issued officially sanctioned notes. ( ④ ) Counterfeiting remained a persistent challenge, prompting the use of special dyes and intricate designs. ( ⑤ ) The innovation later spread along the Silk Road, influencing monetary systems across Eurasia.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • brisk:활발한
  • impractical:비실용적인
  • sanctioned:승인된
[문제 8]

주어진 문장

In theory, these regulations give individuals more control over how their information is used.

Sharing personal information online has never been easier—or riskier. ( ① ) Social-media platforms generate revenue by collecting user data and selling targeted advertisements. ( ② ) Meanwhile, data breaches at major corporations have exposed millions of customers' records. ( ③ ) As public concern mounts, several governments have enacted strict privacy laws requiring companies to obtain explicit consent before gathering data. ( ④ ) Critics argue that enforcement remains uneven and penalties too lenient. ( ⑤ ) Ultimately, users must stay vigilant and adjust privacy settings to safeguard their digital identities.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • breach:(데이터) 유출
  • enact:제정하다
  • lenient:관대한
328
Vera's Flavor - PART 4 문장 삽입
[문제 9]

주어진 문장

Climate change further complicates the picture by lengthening fire seasons and drying out soils.

Wildfires play a natural role in many forest ecosystems. ( ① ) Low-intensity burns clear away dead vegetation, making room for new growth. ( ② ) Decades of aggressive fire suppression, however, have allowed flammable material to accumulate. ( ③ ) As a result, today's fires often burn hotter and spread faster than historical ones. ( ④ ) Fire ecologists now advocate selective thinning and controlled burns to restore healthier conditions. ( ⑤ ) Communities located near forests must adapt to increased smoke and the potential for sudden evacuations.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • suppression:진압
  • flammable:가연성의
  • advocate:옹호하다

( ② ) The act of giving time fosters a sense of purpose and social connection. ( ③ ) It can even lower stress by shifting attention away from personal worries. ( ④ ) Retirement counselors therefore encourage seniors to find volunteer roles that match their interests and skills. ( ⑤ ) Simple acts of service can create ripple effects that strengthen entire communities.

( ③ ) Efforts to curb overuse of antibiotics in livestock and improve stewardship in clinics are under way. ( ④ ) Researchers are also racing to develop novel treatments, but progress remains slow. ( ② ) Sessions often involve singing familiar songs or tapping to a beat. ( ③ ) Therapists use these moments to stimulate conversation and foster social connection. ( ④ ) Such measures not only reduce cooling costs but also improve air quality. ( ⑤ ) These initiatives show how urban design can mitigate climate change impacts.

( ③ ) The ingenuity of the design kept water flowing even during prolonged droughts. ( ④ ) Without such engineering, large-scale settlement in the desert plain would have been impossible. ( ② ) Storing the hydrogen effectively turns clean electricity into a transportable fuel. ( ③ ) Researchers are now developing catalytic membranes that slash production costs. ( ④ ) Countries like Germany plan to build “hydrogen valleys” where industry, heating, and transport all run on the fuel. Economists use the term sunk cost fallacy to describe irrational decision-making. ( ① ) Imagine buying an expensive concert ticket and then falling ill on the day of the show. ( ② ) Rationally, staying home would maximize comfort without changing the fact that the ticket is paid for. ( ③ ) Many concertgoers still drag themselves to the venue despite feeling miserable. ( ④ ) Psychologists suggest that admitting a loss feels like accepting failure, triggering defensive behavior. ( ⑤ ) Recognizing this bias is the first step toward making objectively better choices.

  • sunk cost fallacy:매몰 비용 오류

( ② ) Alan Turing and his colleagues built electromechanical bombes that tested thousands of key settings every minute. ( ③ ) Daily intelligence gleaned from decrypted messages guided Allied convoys around U-boat wolf packs. ( ④ ) Secrecy surrounding the project persisted for decades after the war. Residential water use places significant strain on municipal supplies in arid regions. ( ① ) Installing low-flow fixtures is one strategy to reduce consumption. ( ④ ) Homeowners who adopt these measures often see utility bills drop by a third. ( ⑤ ) Community workshops teach residents how to maintain small-scale systems safely.

Bioluminescence—the production of light by living organisms—evolved multiple times across the tree of life. ( ① ) Marine environments host the greatest diversity, from plankton to predators nearly a meter long. ( ② ) The chemical reaction typically involves luciferin substrates and the enzyme luciferase. ( ③ ) Scientists are exploring medical applications such as tracking cancer cells in mice. ( ④ ) Bio-artists have even engineered glowing houseplants using bacterial genes. ( ⑤ ) These experiments raise ethical questions about gene editing for aesthetic purposes.

( ② ) Comparative studies reveal that regions where neighbors routinely cooperate experience faster growth. ( ③ ) Policy makers therefore invest in programs that encourage civic participation, from volunteering to local referenda. ( ④ ) Measuring intangible qualities such as trust remains methodologically challenging. ( ⑤ ) Critics caution that cultural factors can distort cross-country comparisons.

( ② ) Its carbon-fiber rotors spin five times faster than those of Earth helicopters to compensate for the thin atmosphere. ( ③ ) Early test hops gathered aerial images that complement the Perseverance rover’s ground-level views. ( ④ ) Mission planners will use these photos to chart safer routes and identify science targets. ( ⑤ ) The technology could pave the way for larger aerial scouts on future missions.

( ② ) Smart appliances receive real-time price signals that automate these shifts with minimal inconvenience. ( ③ ) Large industrial facilities participate by pausing non-essential processes for short intervals. ( ④ ) Economists estimate that widespread adoption could negate the need for several peak-load power plants. ( ② ) Microscopic analysis shows they did not form through natural mineral seepage. ( ③ ) If confirmed, the discovery would make Neanderthals the first known cave artists. ( ④ ) Critics argue that contamination might have skewed the age estimates. ( ⑤ ) Further sampling is planned to settle the debate.

This exponential curve explains why starting early yields disproportionately large gains.

Individual investors often underestimate the power of compound interest. ( ① ) A person who deposits 100 dollars a month at 6 percent annual return will have about 200,000 dollars after 40 years. ( ② ) By contrast, someone who waits ten years to begin must save almost double to catch up. ( ③ ) Financial-literacy courses now use interactive graphs to illustrate the concept. ( ④ ) Policymakers hope better education will narrow retirement-savings gaps. ( ⑤ ) Automatic enrollment in workplace plans is also gaining traction.

Vera's Secret Flavor - Vocabulary

  • exponential:기하급수적인
  • disproportionately:불균형적으로
  • enrollment:등록

Algorithms now examine light reflections in the eyes—details so subtle that human reviewers miss them.

Deepfake videos have grown so convincing that politicians worry about disinformation campaigns. ( ① ) Early detection tools focused on irregular blinking patterns, but creators quickly patched that flaw. ( ② ) Researchers are therefore turning to more granular biometric markers. ( ③ ) Initial tests show the method flags forgeries with 94 percent accuracy. ( ④ ) Social-media platforms plan to integrate the technology before major elections. ( ⑤ ) Civil-rights groups insist that transparency about false positives is essential.

  • deepfake:딥페이크

During an El Niño year, fisheries off Peru often collapse while floods strike normally arid regions.

El Niño refers to the periodic warming of surface waters in the eastern Pacific. ( ① ) The phenomenon disrupts atmospheric circulation on a global scale. ( ② ) Meteorologists use ocean-buoy data and satellite imagery to predict its onset months in advance. ( ③ ) Governments rely on these forecasts to allocate disaster-relief funds. ( ④ ) La Niña, the cooler counterpart, triggers almost opposite weather anomalies. ( ⑤ ) Understanding both cycles remains crucial for climate-adaptation planning.

  • onset:시작
  • anomaly:변칙, 이례

( ② ) A recent clinical trial linked higher diversity to reduced inflammation markers. ( ③ ) Scientists caution, however, that benefits vary depending on individual genetics. ( ④ ) Ultra-processed foods, by contrast, appear to diminish beneficial species. ( ⑤ ) Public-health campaigns now encourage adding at least one fermented item to daily meals.

( ② ) Over time, data showed elk herds becoming more vigilant and less stationary. ( ③ ) The resulting vegetation recovery boosted habitats for songbirds and beavers. ( ④ ) Tourists now flock to the park hoping to glimpse a wolf pack at dawn. ( ⑤ ) Balancing conservation and local livelihoods remains an ongoing challenge.

By plotting lines that converge toward a single vanishing point, they achieved unprecedented depth on flat panels.

Renaissance painters revolutionized visual art through the systematic use of linear perspective. ( ① ) Filippo Brunelleschi famously demonstrated the technique with a painted panel of Florence’s Baptistery. ( ② ) His experiment inspired artists like Masaccio and Piero della Francesca. ( ③ ) The mathematical principles later spread across Europe via printed treatises. ( ④ ) Modern architects still study these texts to understand spatial illusion. ( ⑤ ) Digital-rendering software now automates the calculations in seconds.

( ② ) Researchers tracked variables such as job-seeking behavior and mental health. ( ③ ) Critics argued that the sample size was too small to draw firm conclusions. ( ④ ) Other countries, including Canada and Spain, have announced smaller-scale pilots. ( ⑤ ) Economists debate whether unconditional cash transfers can replace fragmented welfare systems.

( ② ) Implementation involves strong authentication, micro-segmentation, and continuous monitoring. ( ③ ) Migrating legacy systems poses technical hurdles and cultural resistance. ( ④ ) The U.S. federal government has mandated agencies to adopt zero-trust by 2027. ( ⑤ ) Vendors now market turnkey solutions, though experts warn no framework is foolproof.

( ② ) Psychologists link the phenomenon to perfectionism and minority status in a workplace. ( ③ ) Cognitive-behavioral therapy teaches clients to reframe negative self-talk. ( ② ) Spectroscopic data confirmed the presence of heavy elements like gold and platinum. ( ③ ) The multi-messenger event opened a new era of coordinated astronomy. ( ④ ) Future detectors with greater sensitivity may reveal mergers at cosmological distances. ( ⑤ ) Such observations could refine the Hubble constant by providing independent distance measures.

Gift exchanges create social obligations that bind clans together across vast ocean distances.

The Kula ring is a ceremonial exchange system practiced in Papua New Guinea’s Trobriand Islands. ( ① ) Participants travel great lengths by canoe to trade shell necklaces and armbands. ( ② ) The items never remain with one owner but circulate clockwise and counterclockwise. ( ③ ) Anthropologist Bronisław Malinowski documented the practice in the early twentieth century. ( ④ ) His work challenged Western notions that economics is purely material. ( ⑤ ) Modern researchers analyze how the tradition adapts to tourism and cash economies.
